1.6: ° The titles of the Psalms and their value .In the Septuagint and Vulgate versions, all the psalms, except the first two, have a title. These titles also usually exist in the Hebrew text; however, there are as many as thirty-four psalms without one. They indicate sometimes the character, subject, or purpose of the poem; sometimes the author, sometimes the historical occasion, sometimes the musical accompaniment, and sometimes all these details at once. The following lines from Lamartine will suffice: «This divine bard (David) has often touched my heart and captivated my thoughts. He is the foremost of poets of feeling; he is the king of lyric poetry. 2.1: Why do the nations rage and tumult, and the peoples devise vain plans?
2.2: The kings of the earth rise up and the princes take counsel together, against the Lord and against his Anointed.
2.3: "Let us break their bonds," they say, "and cast away their chains.".
2.4: He who sits in the heavens smiles; the Lord mocks them.
2.5: Then he will speak to them in his anger and fury, and terrify them.
2.6: And I have installed my king on Zion, my holy mountain.
2.7: I will publish the decree: The Lord said to me: You are my Son, today I have begotten you.
2.8: Ask and I will make the nations your inheritance, the ends of the earth your possession.
2.9: You will break them with an iron scepter, you will smash them to pieces like a potter's vessel.
2.10: And now, kings, be wise, receive warning, judges of the earth.
2.11: Serve the Lord with fear, and rejoice with trembling.
2.12: Embrace the Son, lest he be angry and you perish in your way, for soon his anger would be kindled; blessed are all who put their trust in him.

3.1: A song of David on the occasion of his flight from Absalom, his son.
3.2: Lord, how many are my enemies! How many rise up against me!.
3.3: Many are saying about me, "He has no salvation from God." Sela.
3.4: But you, Lord, are my shield, you are my glory, and you lift up my head.
3.5: With my voice I cry out to the Lord, and he answers me from his holy mountain. Sela.
3.6: I lay down and fell asleep, I woke up because the Lord is my support.
3.7: I am not afraid of the countless people who besiege me on all sides.
3.8: Arise, Lord. Save me, my God, for you strike all my enemies on the cheek. You break the teeth of the wicked.
3.9: Salvation belongs to the Lord. May your blessing be upon your people. Sela.

4.1: To the choirmaster, with stringed instruments, a psalm of David.
4.2: When I call upon you, answer me, God of my justice, you who in my distress set me free. Have mercy on me and hear my prayer.
4.3: Sons of men, how long will my glory be insulted? How long will you love vanity and seek after lies? Sela.
4.4: Know that the Lord has chosen a pious man for himself; the Lord hears when I call upon him.
4.5: Tremble and sin no more. Speak to yourselves on your beds and cease. Sela.
4.6: Offer righteous sacrifices and trust in the Lord.
4.7: Many say: Who will show us happiness? Let the light of your face shine upon us, Lord.
4.8: You have put more joy in my heart than they have in the time when their wheat and new wine abound.
4.9: In peace I will lie down and sleep at once, for you, Lord, you alone, make me dwell in safety.

5.1: To the choirmaster, with flutes, a psalm of David.
5.2: Give ear to my words, Lord, hear my sighs,
5.3: Hear my cries, O my King and my God, for it is to you that I address my prayer.
5.4: Lord, from the morning you hear my voice, from the morning I prepare my requests and I wait.
5.5: For you are not a God who takes pleasure in evil; the wicked cannot dwell with you.
5.6: The foolish cannot stand before your eyes; you hate all the workers of iniquity.
5.7: You destroy the liars. The Lord abhors the bloodthirsty and deceitful man.
5.8: For me, by your great mercy, I will go into your house, I will bow down in fear of you, before your holy temple.
5.9: Lord, lead me in your righteousness; because of my enemies, make your way straight beneath me.
5.10: because there is no sincerity in their mouths, their hearts are full of malice, their throats are open tombs, and their tongues are caressing.
5.11: Punish them, O God, that they may fail in their plans. Because of their countless sins, cast them down, for they are in revolt against you.
5.12: Then all who trust in you will rejoice; they will be filled with perpetual joy, and you will protect them; those who love your name will be filled with joyful delight.
5.13: For you bless the righteous, Lord, you surround them with favor as with a shield.

6.1: To the choirmaster, with stringed instruments, at the octave, a psalm of David.
6.2: Lord, do not punish me in your anger, nor chastise me in your fury.
6.3: Have mercy on me, Lord, for I am weak; heal me, Lord, for my bones are trembling.
6.4: My soul is in extreme turmoil, and you, Lord, how long?
6.5: Return, Lord, deliver my soul, save me because of your mercy.
6.6: For he who dies no longer remembers you; who will praise you in Sheol?
6.7: I am exhausted from moaning, every night my bed is soaked with my tears, my bed is drenched with my crying.
6.8: My eye is consumed by grief, it has aged because of all those who persecute me.
6.9: Depart from me, all you who do evil, for the Lord has heard the voice of my tears.,
6.10: The Lord has heard my supplication, the Lord accepts my prayer.
6.11: All my enemies will be confounded and seized with terror; they will retreat, suddenly covered in shame.

7.1: A dithyramb of David, which he sang to the Lord on the occasion of the words of Cush the Benjamite.
7.2: Lord, my God, in you I trust, save me from all my persecutors and deliver me
7.3: lest he tear me apart, like a lion, devour his prey, with no one to snatch it away from him.
7.4: Lord, my God, if I have done this, if there is iniquity in my hands,
7.5: If I have repaid evil to those who are at peace with me, if I have plundered those who oppress me without cause,
7.6: Let the enemy pursue me and overtake me, let him trample my life to the ground, let him lay my glory in the dust.
7.7: Arise, Lord, in your anger; come against the fury of my adversaries; awake to my aid, you who decree judgment.
7.8: Let the assembly of the peoples surround you. Then, rising above it, ascend to the heights.
7.9: The Lord judges the peoples: grant me justice, Lord, according to my right and my innocence.
7.10: Put an end to the malice of the wicked and strengthen the righteous, you who search hearts and minds, O righteous God.
7.11: My shield is in God, who saves the upright in heart.
7.12: God is a just judge; every day, the Almighty makes his threats heard.
7.13: Indeed, once again he sharpens his sword, he strings his bow and he aims,
7.14: He directs deadly arrows at him, he makes his arrows burning hot.
7.15: Here is the wicked one, in the labor of iniquity: he conceives evil and gives birth to lies.
7.16: He opens a pit, he digs it, and he falls into the abyss he was preparing.
7.17: His iniquity falls back upon his own head, and his violence descends upon his brow.
7.18: I will praise the Lord for his justice; I will sing the name of the Lord Most High.

8.1: To the choirmaster, according to the Gittite, a song of David.
8.2: Lord, our God, how glorious is your name throughout the earth. You who have clothed the heavens with your majesty.
8.3: Through the mouths of children and infants, you have established power to confound your enemies, to silence the adversary and the blasphemer.
8.4: When I consider your heavens, the work of your fingers, the moon and the stars that you have created, I exclaim:
8.5: What is man that you are mindful of him, and the son of man that you care for him?
8.6: You made him a little lower than God, and crowned him with glory and honor.
8.7: You gave him dominion over the works of your hands; you put all things under his feet:
8.8: Sheep and oxen, all together, and the animals of the field,
8.9: Birds of the sky and fish of the sea and all that travels the paths of the seas.
8.10: Lord, our God, how glorious is your name throughout the earth.

9.1: To the choirmaster, to the tune of Death to the Son, a psalm of David.
9.2: I will praise the Lord with all my heart; I will tell of all your wonderful things.
9.3: I will rejoice and exult in you, I will sing your name, O Most High.
9.4: My enemies retreat, they stumble and fall before your face
9.5: for you have upheld my right and my cause, you have sat on your throne as a righteous judge.
9.6: You have punished the nations, you have destroyed the wicked, you have blotted out their name forever and ever.
9.7: The enemy is annihilated. Ruins forever. The memory of the cities you overthrew has vanished.
9.8: But the Lord sits forever; he has set up his throne for judgment.
9.9: He judges the world with justice, he judges the peoples with righteousness.
9.10: And the Lord is a refuge for the oppressed, a refuge in times of distress.
9.11: In you all those who know your name trust, for you do not forsake those who seek you, Lord.
9.12: Sing to the Lord who dwells in Zion, proclaim among the peoples his mighty deeds.
9.13: For he who demands retribution for shed blood has remembered it; he has not forgotten the cry of the afflicted.
9.14: "Have mercy on me, Lord," they said, "see the affliction to which my enemies have reduced me, you who deliver me from the gates of death,",
9.15: so that I may recount all the praises, at the gates of the daughter of Zion, exulting in your salvation.
9.16: The nations have fallen into the pit they dug; their foot has been caught in the snare they hid.
9.17: The Lord has shown himself, he has executed judgment; in the work of his hands, he has ensnared the wicked. Higgaion. Sela.
9.18: The wicked return to Sheol, all the nations that forget God.
9.19: For the unfortunate are not always forgotten, the hope of the afflicted does not perish forever.
9.20: Arise, Lord, let not man triumph. Let the nations be judged before your face.
9.21: Spread terror upon them, Lord, so that the peoples may know that they are men. Sela.

10.1: Why, Lord, do you stand far away and hide yourself in times of distress?
10.2: When the wicked become proud, the unfortunate are consumed; they are caught in the schemes he has devised.
10.3: For the wicked boast of their covetousness, the accursed thief despises the Lord.
10.4: In his arrogance, the wicked man says: He does not punish, there is no God: these are all his thoughts.
10.5: His ways are prosperous at all times. Your judgments are too high for him to worry about; all his adversaries he dispels with a breath.
10.6: He said in his heart: I will not be shaken, I am forever safe from misfortune.
10.7: His mouth is full of cursing, deceit, and violence; under his tongue are malice and iniquity.
10.8: He lies in ambush near hamlets, in sheltered places, he murders the innocent. His eyes spy on the defenseless man,
10.9: He lies in wait in the covered place, like a lion in its thicket, he lies in wait to surprise the poor, he seizes the poor by pulling him into his net.
10.10: He bends down, he stoops, and the unfortunate fall into his claws.
10.11: He says in his heart: God has forgotten, he has covered his face, he never sees anything.
10.12: Arise, O Lord, O God, lift up your hand. Do not forget the afflicted.
10.13: Why does the wicked man despise God? Why does he say in his heart, “You do not punish”?
10.14: Yet you have seen, for you look upon pain and suffering, to take up their cause. To you the unfortunate surrender themselves, to the orphan you come to the aid of the orphan.
10.15: Break the arm of the wicked, the impious one; if you seek his crime, will you not find it?
10.16: The Lord is king forever and for eternity; the nations will be exterminated from his land.
10.17: You have heard the desire of the afflicted, Lord; you have strengthened their hearts; you have listened attentively.,
10.18: to do justice to the orphan and the oppressed so that man, taken from the earth, may cease to inspire terror.

11.1: To the choirmaster, of David. In the Lord I trust, how can you say to my soul: Flee to your mountain, like a bird.
11.2: For behold, the wicked bend their bow, they have fitted their arrow to the string, to shoot in the shadows at men of upright heart.
11.3: When the foundations are overturned, what can the righteous do?
11.4: The Lord in his holy temple, the Lord, whose throne is in heaven, has his eyes open, his eyelids search the children of men.
11.5: The Lord tests the righteous, but he hates the wicked and those who delight in violence.
11.6: He will rain down on the wicked traps, fire and brimstone, a scorching wind; this is the cup they will be divided against them.
11.7: For the Lord is righteous, he loves justice. The upright will behold his face.

12.1: To the choirmaster, on the octave, a song of David.
12.2: Save us, Lord, for the pious are passing away, the faithful are disappearing from among the children of men.
12.3: We tell lies to each other, we speak with flattering lips and a double heart.
12.4: May the Lord cut off all flattering lips, the tongue that speaks with pride,
12.5: Those who say: "By our tongue we are strong, we have our lips with us: who is our master?"
12.6: Because of the oppression of the afflicted, because of the groaning of the poor, I will now arise, says the Lord, I will bring them the salvation for which they yearn.
12.7: The words of the Lord are pure words, like silver melted in a crucible on the earth, purified seven times.
12.8: You, Lord, will keep them, you will preserve them forever from this generation.
12.9: Around them the wicked walk arrogantly: the higher they rise, the more humiliated the children of men will be.

13.1: To the choirmaster, a song of David.
13.2: How long, Lord, will you always forget me? How long will you hide your face from me?
13.3: How long will I form plans in my soul and sorrow fill my heart every day? How long will my enemy rise up against me?
13.4: Look, answer me, Lord, my God, give light to my eyes, so that I may not sleep in death,
13.5: so that my enemy may not say: I have defeated him, and so that my adversaries may not rejoice when they see me falter.
13.6: I trust in your goodness; my heart will rejoice in your salvation; I will sing to the Lord for the good he has done for me.

14.1: To the choirmaster, of David. The fool says in his heart, “There is no God. They are corrupt, they commit abominable acts; there is no one who does good.”.
14.2: The Lord looks down from heaven upon the sons of men, to see if there is anyone who is wise, anyone who seeks God.
14.3: They are all astray, they are all corrupted together; there is not one who does good, not a single one
14.4: Do not all those who commit iniquity have knowledge? They devour my people as they eat bread; they do not call upon the Lord.
14.5: They will suddenly tremble with terror because God is in the midst of the righteous race.
14.6: You want to confound the plans of the unfortunate, but the Lord is his refuge.
14.7: Oh, may deliverance for Israel come from Zion! When the Lord restores the fortunes of his people, Jacob will rejoice, Israel will be glad!.

15.1: A Psalm of David. Lord, who may dwell in your tent? Who may abide on your holy mountain?
15.2: He who walks in innocence, who practices justice, and who speaks the truth in his heart.
15.3: He does not slander with his tongue, he does not harm his brother, and he does not cast shame upon his neighbor.
15.4: In his eyes the reprobate is worthy of shame, but he honors those who fear the Lord. If he has sworn to his own detriment, he does not change it.
15.5: He does not lend his money at interest and he does not accept a bribe against the innocent: he who behaves thus will never be shaken.

16.1: A hymn of David. Keep me safe, O God, for in you I take refuge.
16.2: I say to the Lord: You are my Lord, you alone are my possession.
16.3: The saints who are in the country, these illustrious ones, are the object of all my affection.
16.4: They multiply idols, they run after foreign gods, I will not pour out their libations of blood, I will not put their names on my lips.
16.5: The Lord is my portion and my cup; you are the one who secures my lot.
16.6: The measuring line has measured out a delicious portion for me, yes, a splendid inheritance is granted to me.
16.7: I bless the Lord who has given me counsel; even at night my loins warn me.
16.8: I keep the Lord always before me, for he is at my right hand: I shall not be shaken.
16.9: Therefore my heart is filled with joy, my soul with gladness, my body itself rests securely.
16.10: For you will not abandon my soul to Sheol, you will not allow him who loves you to see corruption.
16.11: You will show me the path of life; in your presence there is fullness of joy; at your right hand are pleasures forevermore.

17.1: David's Prayer. Lord, hear my plea for justice; listen to my cry; incline your ear to my prayer, which is not uttered by deceitful lips.
17.2: Let my judgment come out of your face; let your eyes behold justice.
17.3: You have tested my heart, you have visited it by night, you have put me in the crucible: you find nothing. My mouth is not at odds with my thoughts.
17.4: As for the actions of man, faithful to the word of your lips, I have heeded the ways of the violent.
17.5: My steps have clung to your paths and my feet have not faltered. I call upon you, for you answer me, O God; incline your ear to me, hear my prayer.
17.7: Show your goodness, you who save those who take refuge in your right hand against their adversaries.
17.8: Keep me as the apple of your eye; shelter me in the shadow of your wings.
17.9: impious people who persecute me, deadly enemies who surround me.
17.10: They close their hearts to pity, and their mouths speak arrogantly.
17.11: They are on our heels, they surround us, they spy on us to bring us down.
17.12: They resemble the lion eager to devour, the lion cub camped in its thicket.
17.13: Arise, Lord, go out to meet him, strike him down, deliver my soul from the wicked by your sword,
17.14: men by your hand, of these men of the world whose portion is in this present life, whose bellies you fill with your treasures, who are satisfied with sons and leave their surplus to their grandsons.
17.15: For me, in my innocence, I will gaze upon your face; when I awaken, I will be satiated with your face.

18.17: He reached down from on high and took hold of me; he drew me out of the deep waters.
18.1: To the choirmaster, a psalm of the servant of the Lord, of David, who addressed to the Lord the words of this song, on the day when the Lord delivered him from the hand of all his enemies and from the hand of Saul.
18.2: He said: I love you, Lord, my strength.
18.3: Lord, my rock, my fortress, my deliverer, my God, my rock in whom I find refuge, my shield, the horn of my salvation, my citadel.
18.4: I called upon the one worthy of praise, the Lord, and I was delivered from my enemies.
18.5: The cords of death surrounded me, the torrents of Belial terrified me, the bonds of Sheol entangled me, the snares of death had fallen before me.
18.7: In my distress I called upon the Lord and cried to my God; from his temple he heard my voice, and my cry before him reached his ears.
18.8: The earth shook and quaked, the foundations of the mountains shook and were shaken, because he was angry.
18.9: Smoke rose from his nostrils and a devouring fire came out of his mouth, from which blazing coals sprang.
18.10: He lowered the heavens and descended; a dark cloud was under his feet.
18.11: He climbed onto a cherub and he flew, he soared on the wings of the wind.
18.12: He made darkness his refuge, his tent around him; it was dark waters and gloomy clouds.
18.13: From the brightness that preceded it sprang forth its clouds, bearing hail and burning coals.
18.14: The Lord thundered from heaven, the Most High uttered his voice: hail and burning coals.
18.15: He launched his arrows and scattered them, he multiplied his thunderbolts and confounded them.
18.16: Then the bed of the waters appeared, the foundations of the earth were laid bare, at your rebuke, Lord, at the breath of the wind from your nostrils.
18.18: He delivered me from my powerful enemy, from those who hated me, even though they were stronger than me.
18.19: They had surprised me in the day of my misfortune, but the Lord was my support.
18.20: He set me free, he saved me, because he took pleasure in me.
18.21: The Lord has rewarded me according to my righteousness; he has repaid me according to the cleanness of my hands.
18.22: For I have kept the ways of the Lord and have not sinned, to stray from my God.
18.23: All his judgments were before me, and I did not cast his laws aside.
18.24: I was blameless towards him, and I kept myself from my iniquity.
18.25: The Lord has rewarded me according to my righteousness, according to the cleanness of my hands in his sight.
18.26: With a good person you show yourself good, with an upright person you show yourself upright,
18.27: With the pure, you show yourself to be pure, and with the deceitful you act treacherously.
18.28: For you save the humbled people and you humble the proud.
18.29: Yes, you make my torch shine. Lord, my God, illuminate my darkness.
18.30: With you I rush upon the armed battalions, with my God, I leap over the walls.
18.31: God, his ways are perfect, the word of the Lord is tried and true; he is a shield for all who trust in him.
18.32: For who is God except the Lord, and who is a rock except our God?
18.33: The God who arms me with strength, who makes my way perfect,
18.34: which makes my feet like those of deer and enables me to stand upright on my heights,
18.35: which trains my hands for battle and my arms to draw the bronze bow.
18.36: You have given me the shield of your salvation, and your right hand sustains me, and your gentleness makes me grow.
18.37: You widen my step beneath me, and my feet do not falter.
18.38: I pursue my enemies and I overtake them; I do not return until I have annihilated them.
18.39: I break them and they do not rise again. They fall beneath my feet.
18.40: You gird me with strength for battle, you make my adversaries bow beneath me.
18.41: You make my enemies turn their backs on me, and I will exterminate those who hate me.
18.42: They cry out, but there is no one to save them. They cry out to the Lord, but he does not answer them.
18.43: I crush them like dust blown by the wind, I sweep them away like mud from the streets.
18.44: You deliver me from the revolts of the people, you place me at the head of the nations, peoples I did not know are enslaved to me.
18.45: As soon as they heard, they obeyed me; the sons of foreigners flattered me.
18.46: The sons of foreigners are failing; they are trembling as they emerge from their fortresses.
18.47: Long live the Lord, and blessed be my rock. May the God of my salvation be exalted!,
18.48: God, who grants me vengeance, who subdues peoples under me,
18.49: who delivers me from my enemies. Yes, you lift me up above my adversaries, you save me from the man of violence.
18.50: Therefore I will praise you among the nations, O Lord, I will sing to the glory of your name
18.51: He grants glorious deliverances to his king, he shows mercy to his anointed, to David and to his descendants forever.

19.1: To the choirmaster, a song of David.
19.2: The heavens declare the glory of God, and the firmament proclaims the work of his hands.
19.3: The day cries out praise to the day, the night teaches it to the night.
19.4: This is not a language, these are not words whose voice is not heard.
19.5: Their sound travels throughout the earth, their accents reach the ends of the world. It is there that he has pitched a tent for the sun.
19.6: And he, like the bridegroom leaving the bridal chamber, rushes forth joyfully, like a hero, to fulfill his career.
19.7: It starts at one end of the sky and its course ends at the other end: nothing escapes its heat.
19.8: The law of the Lord is perfect, restoring the soul. The testimony of the Lord is sure, giving wisdom to the simple.
19.9: The Lord's ordinances are right, they gladden the hearts. The Lord's precept is pure, it enlightens the eyes.
19.10: The fear of the Lord is holy, it endures forever. The decrees of the Lord are true, they are all just.
19.11: They are more precious than gold, than much fine gold, sweeter than honey, than that which drips from the honeycomb.
19.12: Your servant is also enlightened by them; great is the reward for those who observe them.
19.13: Who knows his own failings? Forgive me those I am unaware of.
19.14: Also, preserve your servant from the proud, that they may not rule over me, then I shall be perfect and pure from great sins.
19.15: Accept with favor the words of my mouth and the sentiments of my heart, before you, Lord, my rock and my deliverer.

20.1: To the choirmaster, a psalm of David.
20.2: May the Lord answer you in the day of distress; may the name of the God of Jacob protect you.
20.3: May he send you help from the sanctuary, and may he uphold you from Zion.
20.4: May he remember all your offerings and accept your burnt offerings. Selah.
20.5: May He give you what your heart desires and accomplish all your plans.
20.6: May we, with joyful cries, greet your victory, and raise the banner in the name of our God. May the Lord grant all your wishes.
20.7: I already know that the Lord has saved his Anointed, he will answer him from heaven, his holy dwelling place, by the mighty help of his right hand.
20.8: Some rely on their chariots, some on their horses, but we call on the name of the Lord our God.
20.9: They bend and fall, we get up and stand firm.
20.10: Lord, save the king, that he may answer us on the day we call upon him.

21.1: To the choirmaster, a psalm of David.
21.2: Lord, the king rejoices in your strength, as your help fills him with gladness.
21.3: You gave her what her heart desired, you did not refuse what her lips asked for. Sela.
21.4: For you have bestowed exquisite blessings upon him; you have placed upon his head a crown of pure gold.
21.5: He asked you for life, you gave it to him, long days forever and in perpetuity.
21.6: His glory is great thanks to your help; you bestow upon him splendor and magnificence.
21.7: You make him a blessing forever, you fill him with joy in your presence
21.8: because the king trusts in the Lord and by the goodness of the Most High, he does not falter.
21.9: Your hand, O king, will reach all your enemies, your right hand will reach those who hate you.
21.10: You will make them like a blazing furnace; on the day you show your face, the Lord will destroy them in his anger, and fire will devour them.
21.11: You will wipe from the earth their descendants and their race from among the children of men.
21.12: They have prepared ruin for you; they have devised wicked plans, but they will be powerless.
21.13: for you will make them turn their backs, and you will aim your arrows at their foreheads.
21.14: Arise, Lord, in your strength. We want to sing and celebrate your power.

22.21: Deliver my soul from the sword, my life from the power of the dog.
22.1: To the choirmaster, on the tune of "The Doe of the Dawn," a psalm of David.
22.2: My God, my God, why have you forsaken me? I groan, and salvation remains far from me.
22.3: My God, I cry out during the day and you do not answer, at night and I have no rest.
22.4: Yet you are holy, you dwell among the hymns of Israel.
22.5: In you our fathers trusted, they trusted and you delivered them.
22.6: They cried out to you and were saved; they trusted in you and were not put to shame.
22.7: But I am a worm and not a man, scorned by men and despised by the people.
22.8: Everyone who sees me mocks me, they open their lips, they nod their heads.
22.9: Let him surrender to the Lord, let him save him, let him deliver him since he loves him.
22.10: Yes, it was you who took me from my mother's breast, who gave me confidence in my mother's breasts.
22.11: From my birth I was abandoned to you; from my mother's womb you are my God.
22.12: Do not leave me, for anguish is near, for no one comes to my aid.
22.13: Many bulls surround me, and the forts of Bashan encircle me.
22.14: They open their mouths against me, like a lion tearing and roaring.
22.15: I am poured out like water and all my bones are out of joint, my heart is like wax, it melts within me.
22.16: My strength has dried up like a shard of clay and my tongue sticks to the roof of my mouth; you lay me in the dust of death.
22.17: For dogs surround me, a band of villains prowl around me; they have pierced my feet and my hands,
22.18: I could count all my bones. They watch me, they gaze at me,
22.19: They divide my clothes among themselves, they draw lots for my tunic.
22.20: And you, Lord, do not turn away. You who are my strength, come quickly to my aid.
22.22: Save me from the lion's mouth, pull me from the buffalo's horns.
22.23: Then I will declare your name to my brothers, in the midst of the assembly, I will praise you:
22.24: «You who fear the Lord, praise him. All you descendants of Jacob, glorify him. Revere him, all you descendants of Israel.”.
22.25: For he did not despise, he did not reject the suffering of the afflicted, he did not hide his face from him, and when the afflicted cried out to him, he heard.
22.26: Thanks to you, my hymn will resound in the great assembly, I will fulfill my vows in the presence of those who fear you.
22.27: The afflicted will eat and be satisfied; those who seek the Lord will praise him. May your hearts live forevermore.
22.28: All the ends of the earth will remember and turn to the Lord, and all the families of the nations will bow down before him.
22.29: For the kingdom belongs to the Lord, and he rules over the nations.
22.30: The mighty of the earth will eat and worship him; before him will all who go down to the dust, those who cannot prolong their lives.
22.31: Posterity will serve him, and the Lord will be spoken of to future generations.
22.32: They will come and proclaim his righteousness to the people yet to be born, telling them what he has done.

23.1: A Psalm of David. The Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want.
23.2: He makes me lie down in green pastures, he leads me beside refreshing waters,
23.3: He restores my soul. He leads me in the right paths for his name's sake.
23.4: Even when I walk through a valley of deadly shadow, I fear no evil, for you are with me; your rod and your staff reassure me.
23.5: You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies; you anoint my head with oil; my cup overflows.
23.6: Yes, goodness and mercy will follow me all the days of my life, and I will dwell in the house of the Lord forever.

24.1: A Psalm of David. The earth is the Lord’s, and everything in it, the world, and all who live in it.
24.2: For it is he who founded it upon the seas, who established it upon the rivers.
24.3: Who may ascend the mountain of the Lord? Who may stand in his holy place?
24.4: He who has innocent hands and a pure heart, he who does not give his soul to falsehood and does not swear to deceive.
24.5: He will receive the blessing of the Lord, the righteousness of the God of his salvation.
24.6: Such is the generation of those who seek him, of those who seek the face of the God of Jacob. Sela.
24.7: Gates, lift up your lintels, lift up yourselves ancient gates, that the King of glory may enter.
24.8: Who is this King of glory? The strong and mighty Lord, the Lord mighty in battle.
24.9: Gates, lift up your lintels, lift up yourselves ancient gates, that the King of glory may enter.
24.10: Who is this King of glory? The Lord God of the universe, that is the King of glory. Sela.

25.1: A Psalm of David. ALEPH. To you, Lord, I lift up my soul, my God.
25.2: BETH. In you I trust: let me not be put to shame. Let not my enemies rejoice over me.
25.3: GHIMEL. No, none of those who hope in you will be put to shame; those who are faithless without cause will be put to shame.
25.4: DALETH. Lord, make me know your ways, teach me your paths.
25.5: HEY. Lead me in your truth, VAV. and instruct me, for you are the God of my salvation; all day long I hope in you.
25.6: ZAÏN. Remember your mercy, Lord, and your goodness, for they are eternal.
25.7: HETH. Do not remember the sins of my youth nor my transgressions; remember me according to your mercy, because of your goodness, O Lord,
25.8: TETH. The Lord is good and upright; therefore he shows sinners the way.
25.9: YOD. He leads the humble in righteousness, he teaches the humble his way.
25.10: CAPH. All the paths of the Lord are mercy and faithfulness, for those who keep his covenant and his commandments.
25.11: LAMED. Because of your name, Lord, you will forgive my iniquity, for it is great.
25.12: MEM. Who is the man who fears the Lord? The Lord shows him the way he should choose.
25.13: NUN. His soul rests in happiness and his posterity will possess the land.
25.14: SAMECH. The Lord's intimacy is for those who fear him; he makes known to them the blessings of his covenant.
25.15: AÏN. My eyes are constantly turned towards the Lord because it is he who will pull my feet from the snare.
25.16: PHE. Look at me and have pity on me, for I am abandoned and unhappy.
25.17: TSADÉ. The anguish of my heart has increased, deliver me from my distress.
25.18: See my misery and my pain and forgive all my sins.
25.19: RESCH. See how numerous my enemies are and what violent hatred they have against me.
25.20: SCHIN. Guard my soul and save me. Let me not be put to shame, for I have placed my trust in you.
25.21: THAV. May innocence and righteousness protect me, for I place my hope in you.
25.22: O God, deliver Israel from all its troubles.

26.1: Of David. Vindicate me, Lord, for I have walked without faltering. I trust in the Lord; I shall not be shaken.
26.2: Test me, Lord, and examine me; test my mind and my heart.
26.3: for your mercy is before my eyes and I walk in your truth.
26.4: I have not sat with men of lies, I do not associate with men of deceit.,
26.5: I hate the assembly of those who do evil; I do not sit with the wicked.
26.6: I wash my hands in innocence and I surround your altar, Lord,
26.7: to raise a voice of praise and recount all your wonders.
26.8: Lord, I love the dwelling place of your house, the place where your glory resides.
26.9: Do not take away my soul with that of sinners, my life with that of bloodthirsty men
26.10: who have crime in their hands and whose right wing is full of bribes.
26.11: As for me, I walk in my innocence: deliver me and have mercy on me.
26.12: My foot stands on level ground: I will bless the Lord in the assemblies.

27.1: Of David. The Lord is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear? The Lord is the stronghold of my life; of whom shall I be afraid?
27.2: When wicked men advanced against me to devour my flesh, when my adversaries and enemies advanced, it was they who stumbled and fell.
27.3: Though an army come encamped against me, my heart will not fear; though battle be joined against me, then I will have confidence.
27.4: One thing I ask of the Lord, this is what I seek: that I may dwell in the house of the Lord all the days of my life, to enjoy the graces of the Lord, to behold his sanctuary.
27.5: For he will shelter me in his dwelling in the day of adversity, he will hide me in the secret of his tent, he will establish me upon a rock.
27.6: Then my head will be lifted up above the enemies around me. I will offer sacrifices of thanksgiving in his tabernacle, and I will sing and make hymns to the Lord.
27.7: Lord, hear my voice, I call upon you, have mercy on me and answer me.
27.8: My heart says on your behalf: «Seek my face,» I seek your face, Lord.
27.9: Do not hide your face from me, do not turn your servant away in anger; you are my help; do not forsake me or abandon me, God of my salvation.
27.10: For my father and mother have abandoned me, but the Lord will receive me.
27.11: Lord, teach me your way, lead me in a straight path, because of those who spy on me.
27.12: Do not deliver me to the fury of my adversaries, for against me rise witnesses of lies and people who breathe only violence.
27.13: Ah, if I did not believe I saw the goodness of the Lord in the land of the living.
27.14: Hope in the Lord. Be courageous and let your heart be steadfast. Hope in the Lord.

28.1: Of David. To you, Lord, I cry out; my rock, do not remain deaf to my voice, lest, if you keep silent, I should be like those who go down to the pit.
28.2: Hear the voice of my supplications, when I cry out to you, when I lift my hands toward your holy sanctuary.
28.3: Do not drag me away with the wicked and the workers of iniquity, who speak peace to their neighbors but have malice in their hearts.
28.4: Repay them according to their deeds and according to the malice of their actions, repay them according to the work of their hands, give them the reward they deserve.
28.5: For they do not heed the works of the Lord; he will destroy the work of his hands and will not build them up.
28.6: Blessed be the Lord, for he has heard the voice of my supplications.
28.7: The Lord is my strength and my shield; my heart trusts in him. I have been helped, therefore my heart rejoices, and I will praise him with my songs.
28.8: The Lord is the strength of his people; he is a fortress of salvation for his Anointed One.
28.9: Save your people and bless your heritage. Be their shepherd and carry them forever.

29.1: A Psalm of David. Give to the Lord, O Son of God, give to the Lord glory and power.
29.2: Give to the Lord the glory due his name. Worship the Lord in holy attire.
29.3: The voice of the Lord roars over the waters, the God of glory thunders, the Lord is upon the great waters.
29.4: The voice of the Lord is powerful, the voice of the Lord is majestic.
29.5: The voice of the Lord breaks the cedars, the Lord breaks the cedars of Lebanon,
29.6: He makes them leap like a young bull, Lebanon and Sirion like a calf of the buffalo.
29.7: The voice of the Lord brings forth flames of fire,
29.8: The voice of the Lord shakes the desert, the Lord shakes the desert of Kadesh.
29.9: The voice of the Lord makes the deer give birth, it strips the forests of their leaves, and in his temple all cry out: "Glory!"«
29.10: The Lord, at the flood, is seated on his throne, the Lord sits on his throne, king forever.
29.11: The Lord will give strength to his people; the Lord will bless his people with peace.

30.1: Psalm. A song for the dedication of the house, of David.
30.2: I will exalt you, Lord, for you have lifted me up; you have not given joy to my enemies over me.
30.3: Lord, my God, I cried out to you and you healed me.
30.4: Lord, you have brought my soul up from Sheol, you have given me life, far from those who go down to the pit.
30.5: Sing to the Lord, you his faithful, celebrate his holy memory
30.6: For his anger lasts only a moment, but his grace lasts a lifetime; weeping may come at night, but joy comes in the morning.
30.7: I told myself, in my sense of security: "I will never be shaken."«
30.8: Lord, by your grace you had established my mountain, you hid your face and I was troubled.
30.9: Lord, I have cried out to you, I have implored the Lord:
30.10: «"What do you gain by shedding my blood, by sending me down to the pit? Will the dust sing your praises, will it proclaim your truth?"
30.11: Listen, Lord, be merciful to me; Lord, come to my aid.»
30.12: And you have turned my mourning into joy, you have taken off my sackcloth and clothed me with gladness
30.13: so that my soul may sing to you and not be silent. Lord, my God, I will praise you forever.

31.1: To the choirmaster, a psalm of David.
31.2: Lord, in you I have placed my refuge; let me never be put to shame. In your righteousness, save me.
31.3: Incline your ear to me, hasten to deliver me. Be for me a protective rock, a fortress where I find my salvation.
31.4: For you are my rock, my fortress, and because of your name you will lead me and guide me.
31.5: You will pull me out of the net they have set for me because you are my defense.
31.6: Into your hands I commit my spirit; you will deliver me, Lord, God of truth.
31.7: I hate those who worship worthless idols. As for me, I trust in the Lord.
31.8: I will rejoice and be glad because of your goodness, for you have looked upon my affliction, you have seen the anguish of my soul
31.9: And you did not deliver me into the hands of the enemy, you gave my feet free space.
31.10: Have mercy on me, Lord, for I am in distress; my eye is worn out with grief, as are my soul and my bowels.
31.11: My life is consumed in pain and my years in groaning; my strength is spent because of my iniquity, and my bones waste away.
31.12: All my adversaries have made me an object of reproach, a burden to my neighbors, an object of dread to my friends. Those who see me outside flee from me.
31.13: I am forgotten, like a dead man, far from hearts, I am like a broken vase
31.14: for I have heard the evil talk of the crowd, the terror that reigns all around, while they take counsel against me: they plot to take my life.
31.15: And I trust in you, Lord; I say, «You are my God.»
31.16: My destiny is in your hand, deliver me from the hand of my enemies and my persecutors.
31.17: Let your face shine upon your servant; save me by your grace.
31.18: Lord, let me not be put to shame when I call upon you. Let the wicked be put to shame. Let them descend silently to Sheol.
31.19: May the lying lips that speak arrogantly against the righteous, with pride and contempt, be silenced.
31.20: How great is your goodness, which you have stored up for those who fear you, which you show to those who take refuge in you, in the sight of the children of men.
31.21: You shelter them in the sanctuary of your face, from the machinations of men; you hide them in your tent, safe from the tongues that attack them.
31.22: Blessed be the Lord, for he has shown his grace toward me, by placing me in a fortified city.
31.23: In my distress I said, "I am cast out from your sight." But you heard the voice of my supplications when I cried out to you.
31.24: Love the Lord, all you who are devout toward him. The Lord protects the faithful and severely punishes the proud.
31.25: Be courageous and let your hearts be strong, all you who hope in the Lord.

32.1: Of David. A pious meditation. Blessed is the one whose transgression is forgiven, whose sin is pardoned.
32.2: Blessed is the man to whom the Lord does not impute iniquity, and in whose spirit there is no deceit.
32.3: As long as I remained silent, my bones were consumed by my groaning each day.
32.4: For day and night your hand weighed heavily upon me, the sap of my life withered in the heat of summer. Sela.
32.5: I acknowledged my sin to you, and I did not hide my iniquity; I said, «I confess my transgressions to the Lord,» and you forgave the guilt of my sin. Selah.
32.6: Let every devout person pray to you in the time of favor. No, when the great waters rise, they will not reach him.
32.7: You are my refuge, you will protect me from distress, you will surround me with songs of deliverance. Sela.
32.8: «I will instruct you and show you the way you should follow; I will be your counselor, my eye will be upon you.»
32.9: Don't be like the unintelligent horse or mule; you must govern them with the bit and the bridle, otherwise they will not obey.
32.10: Many sorrows are the portion of the wicked, but he who trusts in the Lord is surrounded by his grace.
32.11: Rejoice in the Lord, you righteous ones, and be glad. Shout for joy, all you upright in heart.

33.1: Rejoice in the Lord, you righteous ones. Praise befits the upright.
33.2: Praise the Lord with the harp, sing to him on the ten-stringed lute.
33.3: Sing a new hymn to his glory, artfully unite your instruments and voices
33.4: for the word of the Lord is right and all his works are accomplished in faithfulness.
33.5: He loves justice and righteousness; the earth is full of the Lord’s goodness.
33.6: By the word of the Lord the heavens were made, and all their host by the breath of his mouth.
33.7: He gathers the waters of the sea into a heap, and puts the waves of the deep into storehouses.
33.8: Let all the earth fear the Lord. Let all the inhabitants of the universe tremble before him.
33.9: For he spoke and it was done, he commanded and it came to be.
33.10: The Lord thwarts the plans of the nations; he frustrates the thoughts of the peoples.
33.11: But the plans of the Lord stand forever, and the thoughts of his heart throughout all generations.
33.12: Blessed is the nation whose God is the Lord, blessed is the people he has chosen for his inheritance.
33.13: From heaven the Lord looks down; he sees all the children of men,
33.14: From his dwelling place, he observes all the inhabitants of the earth.,
33.15: He who shapes their hearts, who is attentive to all their actions.
33.16: It is not the number of soldiers that gives the king victory, it is not great strength that makes the warrior triumph.
33.17: The horse is powerless to bring salvation, and all its strength does not guarantee deliverance.
33.18: The eye of the Lord is on those who fear him, on those who hope in his goodness.,
33.19: to deliver their souls from death and to enable them to live in times of famine.
33.20: Our soul waits with confidence for the Lord; he is our help and our shield.,
33.21: for in him our heart finds its joy, for in his holy name we place our trust.
33.22: Lord, may your grace be upon us as we hope in you.

34.1: Of David when he pretended to be insane in the presence of Abimelech and, driven out by him, went away.
34.2: ALEPH. I want to bless the Lord at all times; his praise will always be on my lips.
34.3: BETH. In the Lord my soul will boast: let the humble hear and rejoice.
34.4: GHIMEL. Exalt the Lord with me, let us celebrate his name together.
34.5: DALETH. I sought the Lord and he answered me and delivered me from all my fears.
34.6: HEY. When we look to him, we are radiant with joy, VAV. And our faces are not covered with shame.
34.7: ZAÏN. This poor man cried out and the Lord heard him and saved him from all his anguish.
34.8: HETH. The angel of the Lord encamps around those who fear him, and he saves them.
34.9: TETH. Taste and see that the Lord is good. Blessed is the man who takes refuge in him.
34.10: YOD. Fear the Lord, you his saints, for there is no want for those who fear him.
34.11: CAPH. Lion cubs may experience want and hunger, but those who seek the Lord lack no good thing.
34.12: LAMED. Come, my sons, listen to me, I will teach you the fear of the Lord.
34.13: MEM. What kind of man loves life, who desires long days to enjoy happiness?
34.14: NUN. Keep your tongue from evil and your lips from deceitful speech,
34.15: SAMECH. Turn away from evil and do good, seek peace and pursue it.
34.16: AIN. The eyes of the Lord are on the righteous and his ears are attentive to their cries.
34.17: PHE. The face of the Lord is against those who do evil, to cut off their memory from the earth.
34.18: TSADÉ. The righteous cry out and the Lord hears them and delivers them from all their distress.
34.19: QOPH. The Lord is near to the brokenhearted, he saves those who are crushed in spirit.
34.20: RESCH. Many are the afflictions of the righteous, but the Lord delivers him from them all.
34.21: SCHIN. He keeps all his bones; not one of them will be broken.
34.22: THAV. Evil kills the wicked, and the enemies of the righteous are punished.
34.23: The Lord delivers the souls of his servants, and all who take refuge in him are not punished.

35.1: Of David. Lord, fight against those who fight against me, wage war against those who wage war against me.
35.2: Take hold of the small and large shields and rise up to help me.
35.3: Draw your spear and bar the way of my persecutors; say to my soul: «I am your salvation.»
35.4: May those who seek my life be ashamed and confused, may those who plot my downfall be turned back and blush.
35.5: May they be like chaff before the wind, and may the angel of the Lord drive them away before him.
35.6: May their path be dark and slippery, and may the angel of the Lord pursue them.
35.7: For without cause they hid their net for my ruin, without cause they dug the pit to make me perish.
35.8: May ruin fall upon him unexpectedly; may the net he hid seize him, and may he fall into it and perish.
35.9: And my soul will rejoice in the Lord, exult in his salvation.
35.10: All my bones will say, «Lord, who is like you, delivering the afflicted from those stronger than them, the afflicted and the poor from those who plunder them?»
35.11: Unjust witnesses are rising up; they accuse me of things I know nothing about.
35.12: They repay me evil for good; my soul is in abandonment.
35.13: And I, when they were sick, would put on sackcloth, afflict my soul with fasting, and my prayer would return to my breast.
35.14: As if for a friend, for a brother, I dragged myself along slowly, as if mourning a mother, I bowed down in sadness.
35.15: And now that I am faltering, they rejoice and gather together; against me, slanderers gather without my knowledge; they tear me apart without ceasing.
35.16: Like foul parasites with mocking tongues, they gnash their teeth at me.
35.17: Lord, how long will you see him? Deliver my soul from their persecutions, my life from the fury of these lions.
35.18: I will praise you in the great assembly; I will extol you among a multitude.
35.19: Let not those who attack me without cause rejoice over me. Let not those who hate me without cause blink their eyes.
35.20: For their language is not that of peace; they are plotting treacherous schemes against the peaceful people of the land.
35.21: They open their mouths wide against me, they say: "Ah! Ah! Our eyes have seen…."«
35.22: Lord, you see it. Do not remain silent, Lord, do not turn away from me.
35.23: Awake, rise up to do me justice, my God and my Lord, to take up my cause.
35.24: Judge me according to your righteousness, Lord my God, and let them not rejoice over me.
35.25: Let them not say in their hearts, "Our soul is satisfied," nor let them say, "We have swallowed it up."«
35.26: May they be ashamed and confounded together, those who rejoice in my misfortune. May they be covered with shame and disgrace, those who rise up against me.
35.27: Let those who desire the triumph of my rights rejoice and be glad, and let them continually say: «Glory to the Lord, who desires peace for his servant.»
35.28: And my tongue will celebrate your righteousness, your praise all day long.

36.1: To the choirmaster, of David, servant of the Lord.
36.2: Iniquity speaks to the wicked in the depths of their heart; the fear of God is not before their eyes.
36.3: For he flatters himself, under the divine gaze, doubting that God will ever discover his crime and hate him.
36.4: The words of his mouth are injustice and deceit; he has ceased to have understanding, to do good.
36.5: He plots iniquity on his bed; he stands in a way that is not good; he does not reject evil.
36.6: Lord, your goodness reaches to the heavens, your faithfulness to the clouds.
36.7: Your righteousness is like the mountains of God, your judgments are like the vast deep. Lord, you protect both people and animals:
36.8: How precious is your goodness, O God! In the shadow of your wings the children of men seek refuge.
36.9: They gorge themselves on the fat of your house, and you give them drink from the river of your delights.
36.10: For with you is the source of life, and in your light we see light.
36.11: Continue your kindness to those who know you and your justice to those who have an upright heart.
36.12: May the foot of the proud not reach me, and may the hand of the wicked not make me flee.
36.13: Fallen are those who commit iniquity. They are overthrown and cannot rise again.

37.36: I passed by, and behold, he was no more; I sought him, and he could not be found. SCHIN.
37.1: Of David. ALEPH. Do not fret because of the wicked, nor envy those who do evil.
37.2: For, like grass, they will soon be cut down; like the green of the lawn, they will wither. BETH.
37.3: Put your trust in the Lord and do good; dwell in the land and enjoy his faithfulness.
37.4: Delight yourself in the Lord and he will give you the desires of your heart. GHIMEL.
37.5: Commit your fate to the Lord and trust in him: he will act.
37.6: He will make your justice shine like the light, and your righteousness like the noonday sun. DALETH.
37.7: Be still before the Lord and wait patiently for him; do not fret because of him who prospers in his ways, because of the man who succeeds in his schemes. HE.
37.8: Leave behind anger, abandon fury; do not become irritated, for it only leads to harm.
37.9: For the wicked will be cut off, but those who hope in the Lord will inherit the land. VAV.
37.10: A little more time and the villain is no more; you look at his place and he has disappeared.
37.11: But the meek will possess the earth; they will taste the delights of profound peace. ZAÏN.
37.12: The wicked plot against the righteous, they gnash their teeth at them.
37.13: The Lord laughs at the wicked because he sees that their day is coming. HETH.
37.14: The wicked draw their swords, they bend their bows; to strike down the unfortunate and the poor, to slaughter those whose way is upright.
37.15: Their sword will pierce their own hearts and their bows will break. TETH.
37.16: Better is a little that a righteous person has than the abundance of many wicked people; ;
37.17: For the arms of the wicked will be broken, and the Lord upholds the righteous. YOD.
37.18: The Lord knows the days of the upright, and their inheritance endures forever.
37.19: They are not confounded in the day of adversity, and they are satisfied in the days of famine. CAPH.
37.20: For the wicked perish; the enemies of the Lord are like the glory of the meadows; they vanish like smoke, they vanish. LAMED.
37.21: The wicked borrow and do not repay; the righteous are compassionate and give.
37.22: For those whom the Lord blesses will inherit the land, and those whom he curses will be cut off. MEM.
37.23: The Lord makes firm the steps of the righteous man, and he delights in his way.
37.24: If he falls, he will not lie prostrate on the ground, for the Lord upholds his hand. NUN.
37.25: I was young, and now I am old, and I have not seen the righteous forsaken; nor his descendants begging for their bread.
37.26: He is always compassionate and lends freely, and his descendants are a blessing. SAMECH.
37.27: Turn away from evil and do good, and dwell forever in your home.
37.28: For the Lord loves justice and does not forsake his faithful ones. They are always under his protection, but the offspring of the wicked will be cut off.
37.29: The righteous will possess the land and dwell in it forever. PHEA.
37.30: The mouth of the righteous speaks wisdom, and his tongue proclaims justice.
37.31: The law of his God is in his heart; his steps do not falter. TSADÉ.
37.32: The wicked man spies on the righteous man and seeks to kill him.
37.33: The Lord does not abandon him in his hands, nor does he condemn him when his judgment comes. QOPH.
37.34: Wait for the Lord and keep to his way, and he will exalt you, and you will possess the land; when the wicked are cut off, you will see it. RESCH.
37.35: I saw the wicked man at the height of his power; he spread out like a green tree.
37.37: Observe the one who is blameless and look to the one who is upright; for there is a future for the man of peace.
37.38: But the rebels will all be annihilated, the posterity of the wicked will be cut off. THAV.
37.39: From the Lord comes the salvation of the righteous; he is their protector in times of distress.
37.40: The Lord comes to their aid and delivers them; he delivers them from the wicked and saves them because they have put their trust in him.

38.1: A Psalm of David. In memorial.
38.2: Lord, do not punish me in your anger, nor chastise me in your fury.
38.3: For your arrows have struck me, and your hand has come down heavily upon me.
38.4: There is nothing sound in my flesh because of your anger, there is nothing safe in my bones because of my sin.
38.5: For my iniquities have risen over my head like a heavy burden; they weigh me down with their weight.
38.6: My bruises are infected and purulent due to the effect of my madness.
38.7: I am bowed down, utterly defeated, all day long I walk in mourning.
38.8: A burning pain devours my kidneys, and there is nothing healthy in my flesh.
38.9: I am powerless, broken beyond measure, the turmoil in my heart tears groans from me.
38.10: Lord, all my desires are before you and my sighs are not hidden from you.
38.11: My heart is pounding, my strength is leaving me, and even the light in my eyes is no longer with me.
38.12: My friends and companions are moving away from my wound, and my relatives are keeping their distance.
38.13: Those who seek my life lay their traps, those who seek my downfall utter threats and all day long they plot snares.
38.14: And I am like a deaf person, I cannot hear, I am like a mute person, who does not open his mouth.
38.15: I am like a man who cannot hear and in whose mouth there is no reply.
38.16: It is in you, Lord, that I hope; you will answer, Lord, my God.
38.17: For I say, «Let them not rejoice over me, let them not rise up against me, if my foot slips.»
38.18: Because I am close to falling and my pain is still before me
38.19: for I confess my iniquity, I am afraid because of my sin.
38.20: And my enemies are full of life, they are powerful. Those who hate me without cause have multiplied.
38.21: They repay me with evil for good; they are hostile to me because I seek justice.
38.22: Do not abandon me, Lord. My God, do not be far from me.
38.23: Hurry to my aid, Lord, you who are my salvation.

39.1: To the choirmaster, at Idithun, a song of David.
39.2: I said, «I will watch my ways, lest I sin with my tongue; I will keep a muzzle on my mouth, as long as the wicked are before me.»
39.3: And I remained mute, in silence; I kept quiet, though deprived of all possessions. But my grief intensified,
39.4: My heart was ablaze within me; in my thoughts a fire was kindled and speech came upon my tongue.
39.5: Lord, let me know the end of my life, and the measure of my days, that I may know how perishable I am.
39.6: You have made my days as short as a hand's breadth, and my life is as nothing before you. Yes, every living man is but a breath. Selah.
39.7: Yes, man passes like a shadow; yes, it is in vain that he frets; he amasses wealth and does not know who will gather it.
39.8: Now, what can I expect, Lord? My hope is in you.
39.9: Deliver me from all my transgressions; do not make me the reproach of the fool.
39.10: I am silent, I will not open my mouth again because it is you who is acting.
39.11: Turn your blows away from me; under the severity of your hand, I succumb.
39.12: When you chastise a man, punishing him for his iniquity, you destroy, like a moth, what he holds most dear. Yes, every man is but a breath. Selah.
39.13: Hear my prayer, Lord, give ear to my cries, do not be indifferent to my tears, for I am a stranger with you, a traveler, like all my fathers.
39.14: Turn your gaze away from me and let me breathe, before I leave and cease to be.

40.1: From the choirmaster, a psalm of David.
40.2: I have placed all my hope in the Lord; he has inclined to me, he has heard my cry.
40.3: He lifted me out of the pit of perdition, out of the mire of the mud; he set my feet upon a rock, he made my steps firm.
40.4: He put a new song in my mouth, a hymn of praise to our God; many see him and revere the Lord, and put their trust in him.
40.5: Blessed is the man who has put his trust in the Lord and does not turn to the proud and to those whom falsehood leads astray.
40.6: You have multiplied, Lord my God, your wonders and your plans for us: none can compare to you. I would like to proclaim and declare them; they surpass all accounts.
40.7: You desire neither sacrifice nor offering, you have pierced my ears; you ask neither burnt offering nor sin offering.
40.8: Then I said, "Here I am, I have come with the scroll of the book written about me.
40.9: I want to do your will, O my God, and your law is within my heart.»
40.10: I will proclaim justice in a great assembly; I will not close my lips, Lord, you know.
40.11: I will not keep your righteousness hidden in my heart; I will proclaim your faithfulness and your salvation; I will not conceal your goodness and your truth from the great assembly.
40.12: Lord, do not close your mercies to me; may your goodness and your truth always keep me.
40.13: For countless evils surround me; my iniquities have seized me, and I cannot see; they are more numerous than the hairs of my head, and my heart abandons me.
40.14: Lord, may it please you to deliver me. Lord, hasten to my aid.
40.15: May they be confounded and ashamed together, those who seek my soul to destroy it. May they be turned back and brought to shame, those who desire my ruin.
40.16: Let them be astonished because of their shame, those who say to me, "Aha! Aha!"«
40.17: May all who seek you rejoice and be glad in you. May those who love your salvation continually say, «Glory to the Lord.».
40.18: I am poor and needy, but the Lord will take care of me. You are my help and my deliverer: my God, do not delay.

41.1: To the choirmaster, a psalm of David.
41.2: Blessed is he who cares for the poor. In the day of trouble, the Lord will deliver him.
41.3: The Lord will protect him and give him life; he will be happy on earth, and you will not give him up to the desires of his enemies.
41.4: The Lord will assist him on his bed of pain; however sick he may be, you will raise him up.
41.5: I say, «Lord, have mercy on me. Heal my soul, for I have sinned against you.»
41.6: And my enemies utter curses against me: «When will he die? When will his name perish?»
41.7: If anyone comes to visit me, he speaks only lies; his heart gathers iniquity; when he goes away, he speaks abroad.
41.8: All my enemies whisper together against me; against me they plot misfortune.
41.9: «"An irreparable illness," they say, "has befallen him; there he lies, he will never rise again."»
41.10: Even the man who was my friend, who had my trust and who ate my bread, raises his heel against me.
41.11: Lord, have mercy on me and raise me up, and I will give them what they deserve.
41.12: I will know that you love me, if my enemy does not triumph over me.
41.13: Because of my innocence, you have supported me and established me forever in your presence.
41.14: Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel, forever and ever. Amen. Amen.

42.1: To the choirmaster. A song of the sons of Korah.
42.2: As the deer longs for streams of water, so my soul longs for you, O God.
42.3: My soul thirsts for God, for the living God. When shall I go and appear before the face of God?
42.4: My tears are my food day and night, while people constantly ask me, "Where is your God?"«
42.5: I remember, and at this memory my soul melts within me, when I walked surrounded by the crowd and advanced towards the house of God, amidst the shouts of joy and thanksgiving of a festive multitude.
42.6: Why are you downcast, O my soul, and why are you troubled within me? Hope in God, for I will yet praise him, my savior and my God.
42.7: My soul is downcast within me, so I think of you, from the land of the Jordan, of Hermon, of Mount Misar.
42.8: One flood calls for another when your waterfalls roar: so all your waves and torrents pass over me.
42.9: By day, the Lord commanded his grace to visit me. By night, his song was on my lips; I offered a prayer to the God of my life.
42.10: Now I say to God, my rock: «Why have you forgotten me? Why must I walk in sorrow under the oppression of the enemy?»
42.11: I feel my bones breaking when my persecutors insult me, constantly saying, "Where is your God?"«
42.12: Why are you downcast, O my soul, and why are you troubled within me? Hope in God, for I will yet praise him, my savior and my God.

43.1: Grant me justice, O God, defend my cause against an unfaithful nation; deliver me from the man of deceit and iniquity.
43.2: For you are the God of my defense, why do you reject me? Why must I walk in sorrow under the oppression of the enemy?
43.3: Send forth your light and your faithfulness, that they may guide me, that they may lead me to your holy mountain and to your tabernacles.
43.4: I will go to the altar of God, to the God who is my joy and my gladness, and I will praise you on the harp, O God, my God.
43.5: Why are you downcast, O my soul, and why are you disturbed within me? Hope in God, for I will yet praise him, my savior and my God.

44.1: To the choirmaster, of the sons of Korah. A song.
44.2: O God, we have heard with our ears, our fathers have told us the work that you accomplished in their days, in days of old.
44.3: With your hand you drove out nations to establish them, you struck down peoples to spread them out
44.4: for it was not with their sword that they conquered the land, it was not their arm that gave them victory, but it was your right hand, it was your arm, it was the light of your face, because you loved them.
44.5: You are my king, O God, command the salvation of Jacob.
44.6: Through you we will overthrow our enemies, in your name we will crush our adversaries.
44.7: For it is not in my bow that I trust, it is not my sword that will save me.
44.8: But it is you who delivers us from our enemies and confounds those who hate us.
44.9: In God we glory every day and we celebrate your name forever. Sela.
44.10: However, you reject us and cover us with shame; you no longer go out with our armies.
44.11: You make us retreat before the enemy, and those who hate us plunder us.
44.12: You deliver us up like sheep to be slaughtered, you scatter us among the nations.
44.13: You sell your people for a pittance; you do not value them highly.
44.14: You make us an object of scorn to our neighbors, of mockery and ridicule to those around us.
44.15: You make us a fable of nations and a subject of nodding among peoples.
44.16: My shame is always before my eyes, and confusion covers my face.,
44.17: at the voice of the one who insults and outrages me, at the sight of the enemy and of the one who breathes vengeance.
44.18: All this is happening to us without us having forgotten you, without us having been unfaithful to your covenant.
44.19: Our hearts have not turned back, our steps have not strayed from your path,
44.20: so that you may crush us in the jackals' hideout and cover us with the shadow of death.
44.21: If we had forgotten the name of our God and stretched out our hands to a foreign god,
44.22: Wouldn't God have noticed it, He who knows the secrets of the heart?
44.23: But it is because of you that we are slaughtered every day, that we are treated like sheep destined for the slaughter.
44.24: Wake up. Why are you sleeping, Lord? Wake up and don't reject us forever.
44.25: Why do you hide your face, do you forget our misery and our oppression?
44.26: For our soul is cast down to the dust, our body is bound to the earth.
44.27: Arise to help us, deliver us because of your goodness.

45.1: To the choirmaster, upon the lilies. A song of the sons of Korah, a song of love.
45.2: From my heart springs a beautiful song, I say: "My work is for a king." My tongue is like the swift reed of the scribe.
45.3: You are the most handsome of the sons of men; grace is poured upon your lips; therefore God has blessed you forever.
45.4: Gird your sword on your thigh, O hero. Clothe yourself in your splendor and majesty.
45.5: And in your majesty advance, mount your chariot, fight for truth, gentleness and justice, and let your right hand accomplish marvelous deeds.
45.6: Your arrows are sharp; peoples will fall at your feet; they will pierce the hearts of the king's enemies.
45.7: Your throne, O God, is established forever; the scepter of your kingdom is a scepter of righteousness.
45.8: You love justice and hate wickedness; therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of gladness above your companions.
45.9: Myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon emanate from all your garments; from ivory palaces, lyres make you glad.
45.10: Daughters of kings are among your beloved; the queen is at your right hand, adorned with the gold of Ophir.
45.11: «Listen, my daughter, look and pay attention: forget your people and your father’s house,
45.12: and the king will be captivated by your beauty because he is your Lord: pay him homage.
45.13: The daughter of Tyre, with gifts, and the wealthiest of the people will seek your favor.»
45.14: The king's daughter is radiant, her garments within are made of fabrics of gold.
45.15: Dressed in various colours, she is presented to the king after the young girls, her companions, are brought to you.
45.16: They are brought in amidst rejoicing and jubilation, they enter the King's palace.
45.17: Your children will take the place of your fathers; you will establish them as princes over all the earth.
45.18: I will remember your name throughout all ages, and the peoples will praise you eternally and forever.

46.1: To the choirmaster, a song of the sons of Korah, in the style of virgins.
46.2: God is our refuge and our strength, a help that is always found in distress.
46.3: Therefore, we have no fear if the earth is shaken, if the mountains are plunged into the ocean,
46.4: If the sea waves churn and boil, and in their fury make the mountains tremble. Sela.
46.5: A river gladdens with its currents the city of God, the sanctuary where the Most High dwells.
46.6: God is in her midst; she is unshakeable at the breaking of dawn; God comes to her aid.
46.7: Nations are in turmoil, kingdoms are crumbling, he makes his voice heard and the earth melts with terror.
46.8: The Lord of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our fortress. Sela.
46.9: Come, behold the works of the Lord, the desolations he has wrought on the earth.
46.10: He has stopped the fighting to the ends of your land, he has broken the bow, he has shattered the spear, he has burned the chariots with fire.
46.11: «Be still and acknowledge that I am God. I rule over the nations, I rule over the earth.»
46.12: The Lord of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our fortress. Sela.

47.1: To the choirmaster, of the sons of Korah, a psalm.
47.2: All you peoples, clap your hands, praise God with shouts of joy!
47.3: for the Lord is most high, awesome, a great king over all the earth.
47.4: He subjugates peoples under us, he puts nations under our feet.
47.5: He chose our inheritance for us, the glory of Jacob, his beloved. Selah.
47.6: God ascends to his sanctuary amid shouts of acclamation, the Lord, to the sound of the trumpet.
47.7: Sing to God, sing, sing, to our King, sing,
47.8: For God is king of all the earth. Sing a song of praise.
47.9: God reigns over the nations; he sits on his holy throne.
47.10: The princes of the peoples gather together to form a people of the God of Abraham, for the shields of the earth belong to God. He is supremely exalted.

48.1: A song, a psalm of the sons of Korah.
48.2: The Lord is great and most worthy of praise, in the city of our God, on his holy mountain.
48.3: She rises gracefully, joy of all the earth, the mountain of Zion, on the farthest reaches of the north, the city of the great King.
48.4: God, in his palaces, has revealed himself as a refuge.
48.5: For behold, the kings had gathered together, they had advanced.
48.6: They saw it, suddenly, they were stunned, bewildered, they fled.
48.7: There a trembling seized them, a pain like that of a woman giving birth.
48.8: By the east wind, you shatter the ships of Tharsis.
48.9: What we had heard, we have seen in the city of the Lord of hosts, in the city of our God. God establishes it forever. Selah.
48.10: O God, we remember your goodness in the midst of your temple.
48.11: As your name, O God, so your praise reaches to the ends of the earth. Your right hand is full of justice.
48.12: Let Mount Zion rejoice, let the daughters of Judah be glad, because of your judgments.
48.13: Explore Zion and walk around it, count its fortresses,
48.14: Observe its rampart, examine its palaces, so that you can tell the story to future generations.
48.15: This is the God who is our God forever and always; he will be our guide throughout all ages.

49.1: To the choirmaster, a psalm of the sons of Korah.
49.2: Listen to all this, O peoples, lend an ear, all you inhabitants of the world,
49.3: common men and men of high standing, together rich and poor.
49.4: My mouth will speak wise words, and my heart has meaningful thoughts.
49.5: I listen to the sentences that God inspires in me, I explain my enigma to the sound of the harp.
49.6: Why should I fear in times of trouble, when the iniquity of my persecutors besieges me?
49.7: They who place their trust in their possessions, their glory in their great riches.
49.8: A man cannot redeem his brother, nor pay God his ransom.
49.9: Buying back their lives is too expensive; it is forever impossible.,
49.10: so that he may live forever and never see the grave.
49.11: No, he will see it; the wise die, the foolish and the stupid also perish, leaving their possessions to others.
49.12: They imagine that their houses will be eternal, that their dwellings will endure from age to age, and they name their estates after themselves.
49.13: But even in his splendor, man does not last; he is like deer that perish.
49.14: Such is their fate, these trusting men and those who follow them, approving their words. Sela.
49.15: Like a flock, they are driven into Sheol; death is their shepherd; in the morning, the upright rule over them, and their shade will be consumed in Sheol, with no other dwelling place.
49.16: But God will redeem my soul from the power of Sheol, for he will take me with him. Sela.
49.17: Therefore, do not be afraid when a man becomes wealthy, when the opulence of his house increases.
49.18: for he will take nothing with him when he dies, his wealth will not descend with him.
49.19: He may consider himself fortunate during his life, but you may be praised for the pleasures you give yourself:
49.20: You will join the generation of your fathers, who will never see the light again.
49.21: Man, even in his splendor, does not understand; he is like the beasts that perish.

50.1: A Psalm of Asaph. The God of gods, the Lord, speaks and summons the earth, from the rising of the sun to its setting.
50.2: From Zion, perfect beauty, God shines forth.
50.3: He is coming, our God, and he will not be silent. Before him is a devouring fire. Around him rages a tempest.
50.4: He calls upon the heavens above and the earth, to judge his people.
50.5: «"Gather for me my faithful ones who have made a covenant with me on sacrifice."»
50.6: And the heavens proclaim his righteousness, for it is God who will judge. Sela.
50.7: «Listen, my people, and I will speak, Israel, and I will rebuke you: I am God, your God.
50.8: It is not for your sacrifices that I blame you; your holocausts are constantly before me.
50.9: I will not take a bull from your house, nor goats from your sheepfolds.
50.10: For mine are all the animals of the forests, all the beasts of the mountains by the thousands.
50.11: I know all the mountain birds and what moves in the fields is within my grasp.
50.12: If I were hungry, I wouldn't tell you because the world is mine and everything in it.
50.13: Do I eat the flesh of bulls? Do I drink the blood of goats?
50.14: Offer thanksgiving as a sacrifice to God and fulfill your vows to the Most High.
50.15: And call upon me in the day of trouble; I will deliver you, and you will glorify me.»
50.16: But to the wicked God said, «What then do you say? You recite my precepts and you take my covenant on your lips,
50.17: you who hate discipline and throw my words behind you.
50.18: If you see a thief, you take pleasure in his company and make common cause with adulterers.
50.19: You give your mouth over to evil, and your tongue plots deceit.
50.20: You sit and speak against your brother, you slander your mother's son.
50.21: "That's what you did, and I kept quiet. You thought I was just like you, but I'm going to set you straight and lay it all out for you."»
50.22: Therefore, take heed, you who forget God, lest I tear you apart, with no one to deliver.
50.23: He who offers thanksgiving as a sacrifice honors me, and to him who plans his way, I will show the salvation of God.

51.1: To the choirmaster, a psalm of David.
51.2: When Nathan the prophet came to him, after he had gone to Bathsheba.
51.3: Have mercy on me, O God, according to your goodness; according to your great mercy blot out my transgressions.
51.4: Wash me thoroughly from my iniquity and cleanse me from my sin.
51.5: For I acknowledge my transgressions, and my sin is always before me.
51.6: It is against you, you only, that I have sinned, and done what is evil in your sight, so that you may be found righteous in your sentence and blameless in your judgment.
51.7: Behold, I was born in iniquity, and my mother conceived me in sin.
51.8: Behold, you desire sincerity to be in the heart; within me, make me know wisdom.
51.9: Purify me with hyssop and I shall be pure. Wash me and I shall be whiter than snow.
51.10: Tell me joy and gladness, and the bones you have crushed will rejoice.
51.11: Turn your face from my sins, blot out all my iniquities.
51.12: O God, create in me a pure heart and renew a steadfast spirit within me.
51.13: Do not cast me away from your presence, and do not take your Holy Spirit from me.
51.14: Restore to me the joy of your salvation and sustain me with a spirit of goodwill.
51.15: I will teach your ways to those who transgress them, and sinners will return to you.
51.16: O God, God of my salvation, deliver me from bloodshed, and my tongue will sing of your righteousness.
51.17: Lord, open my lips and my mouth will proclaim your praise.
51.18: For you do not desire sacrifices, I would offer them to you; you do not take pleasure in burnt offerings.
51.19: The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit; O God, you do not despise a broken and contrite heart.
51.20: In your goodness, bestow your blessings upon Zion, rebuild the walls of Jerusalem.
51.21: Then you will accept the sacrifices of righteousness, the burnt offering and the perfect gift; then bulls will be offered on your altar.

52.1: To the choirmaster. A Psalm of David.
52.2: When Doeg the Edomite came to Saul with this report: David has gone to the house of Ahimelech.
52.3: Why do you boast in evil, O hero? God's goodness endures forever.
52.4: Your tongue plots only malice, like a sharpened blade, you deceitful wretch.
52.5: You prefer evil to good, lies to righteousness. Sela.
52.6: You love all words of perdition, O lying tongue.
52.7: Therefore God will overthrow you forever; he will seize you and tear you from the tent; he will uproot you from the land of the living. Selah.
52.8: The righteous will see it and be terrified, and they will laugh at him:
52.9: «"This was the man who did not consider God his fortress, but trusted in the greatness of his riches and made himself strong in his malice."»
52.10: And I am like a green olive tree in the house of God; I trust in the goodness of God forever.
52.11: I will praise you continually because you have done this, and I will hope in your name, for it is good, in the presence of your faithful ones.

53.1: To the choirmaster, in a plaintive tone. A Psalm of David.
