53.2: The fool says in his heart, "There is no God." They are corrupt, they commit abominable crimes; there is none who does good.
53.3: God looks down from heaven upon the sons of men, to see if there is anyone who is wise, anyone who seeks God.
53.4: They have all gone astray, they have all become corrupt; there is none who does good, not even one.
53.5: Do those who commit iniquity have no knowledge? They devour my people as they eat bread, and they do not call upon God.
53.6: They will suddenly tremble with terror, though there is no cause for terror, for God has scattered the bones of those who encamped against you. You have confounded them, for God has rejected them.
53.7: Oh! May deliverance for Israel come from Zion when God restores the captives of his people; Jacob will rejoice, Israel will be glad.

54.1: To the choirmaster. With stringed instruments. A Psalm of David.
54.2: When the Ziphites came and told Saul: David is hiding among us.
54.3: O God, save me by your name, and vindicate me by your power.
54.4: O God, hear my prayer, give ear to the words of my mouth.
54.5: For foreigners have risen up against me, violent men seek my life; they do not set God before their eyes. Sela.
54.6: Behold, God is my helper, the Lord is the sustainer of my soul.
54.7: He will bring the evil back upon my adversaries; in your truth, annihilate them.
54.8: With all my heart I will offer sacrifices to you, I will praise your name, Lord, for it is good.
54.9: He delivers me from all anguish and my eyes rest with joy upon my enemies.

55.1: To the choirmaster, with stringed instruments. A Psalm of David.
55.2: O God, give ear to my prayer, do not turn away from my supplications.
55.3: Listen to me and answer me. I wander here and there, plaintive and moaning,
55.4: in the face of the enemy's threats, in the face of the oppression of the wicked, for they bring misfortune upon me and pursue me in anger.
55.5: My heart trembles within me, and the terrors of death descend upon me.
55.6: Fear and terror assail me, and a shiver envelops me.
55.7: And I say: Oh, if I had the wings of a dove, I would fly away and settle down at rest,
55.8: I would flee far away and remain in the desert. Sela.
55.9: I would hasten to seek refuge, far from the impetuous wind, far from the hurricane.
55.10: Destroy them, Lord, divide their tongues, for I see violence and discord in the city.
55.11: Day and night they patrol its ramparts; iniquity and vexation are in its midst.,
55.12: Perversity is within its walls; oppression and cunning never leave its places.
55.13: For it is not an enemy who insults me, I would bear it. It is not an adversary who rises up against me, I would hide from him.
55.14: But you, you were another version of myself, my confidant and my friend.
55.15: We lived together in sweet intimacy, we went with the crowd to the house of God.
55.16: Let death overtake them, let them descend alive into Sheol, for wickedness is in their dwelling place, in their midst.
55.17: As for me, I cry out to God and the Lord will save me.
55.18: Evening, morning, midday, I complain, I groan, and he will hear my voice.
55.19: He will deliver my soul in peace from the battle that is waged against me, for many are those who wage war against me.
55.20: God will hear and humble them, he who sits forever on his throne. Sela. For they have no change, nor do they fear God.
55.21: He lays hands on those who were at peace with him; he violates his covenant.
55.22: From his mouth come words as sweet as milk, yet war is in his heart. His speeches are smoother than oil, but they are naked swords.
55.23: Commit your way to the Lord and he will sustain you; he will never allow the righteous to be shaken.
55.24: And you, O God, will bring them down to the pit of destruction. The bloodthirsty and deceitful will not live half their lives. But I put my trust in you.

56.1: To the choirmaster, upon the silent dove of distant lands. A psalm of David. When the Philistines seized him in Geth.
56.2: Have mercy on me, O God, for man is relentlessly pursuing me all day long; they wage war against me, they persecute me.
56.3: All day long my adversaries harass me because there are many who fight me with their heads held high.
56.4: When I am afraid, I put my trust in you.
56.5: With God's help, I will celebrate the fulfillment of His word. I trust in God, I fear nothing; what can a weak mortal do to me?
56.6: They constantly poison my words; all their thoughts are against me, seeking to destroy me.
56.7: They are plotting, they are spying, they are watching my every move, because they want my life.
56.8: Charged with so many crimes, will they escape? In your anger, O God, strike down the peoples.
56.9: You have counted the steps of my wandering life, you have collected my tears in your wineskin, are they not inscribed in your book?
56.10: Then my enemies will turn back on the day I call upon you. I know that God is on my side.
56.11: Through God's help, I will celebrate the fulfillment of his word; through the Lord's help, I will celebrate the fulfillment of his promise.
56.12: I trust in God, I fear nothing, what can a weak mortal do to me?
56.13: The vows I have made to you, O God, I must fulfill. I will offer you sacrifices of thanksgiving.
56.14: For you have delivered my soul from death, have you not kept my feet from stumbling? So that I may walk before God in the light of the living.

57.1: To the choirmaster. "Do not destroy." A hymn of David, when, pursued by Saul, he took refuge in the cave.
57.2: Have mercy on me, O God, have mercy on me, for in you my soul seeks refuge. I will take shelter in the shadow of your wings, until the calamity has passed.
57.3: I cry out to the Most High God, the God who does everything for me.
57.4: He will send me salvation from heaven; my persecutor heaps insults upon me, Sela. God will send his goodness and his truth.
57.5: I lie among lions, men who breathe fire, whose teeth are spears and arrows, and whose tongues are sharp swords.
57.6: Rise above the heavens, O God, let your glory shine throughout the earth.
57.7: They had laid a trap before my steps, already my soul bowed, they had dug a pit before me, they fell into it, Sela.
57.8: My heart is steadfast, O God, my heart is steadfast, I will sing and make joyful instruments resound.
57.9: Awake, my glory. Awake, my lyre and my harp. Let me awaken the dawn.
57.10: I will praise you among the peoples, Lord; I will sing to you among the nations.
57.11: For your faithfulness reaches to the heavens and your truth to the clouds.
57.12: Rise above the heavens, O God, let your glory shine over all the earth.

58.1: To the choirmaster. Do not destroy. A hymn of David.
58.2: Is it by remaining silent that you administer justice? Is it according to the law that you judge, sons of men?
58.3: No, deep in your hearts you plot your wicked schemes, in the land you sell by weight the violence of your hands.
58.4: The wicked are corrupted from the womb, from birth; the deceitful have gone astray.
58.5: Their venom is similar to the venom of the snake, of the deaf viper that closes its ears
58.6: and does not hear the voice of the enchanter, the charmer skilled in his art.
58.7: O God, break their teeth in their mouths. Lord, tear out the jaws of the lion cubs.
58.8: Let them vanish like a flowing torrent. If they aim arrows, let them become blunt.
58.9: May they be like the slug that melts away. Like a woman's stillborn child, may they not see the sun.
58.10: Before your boilers smell of pine needles, green or burning, the hurricane will carry them away.
58.11: The righteous will rejoice at the sight of vengeance; they will bathe their feet in the blood of the wicked.
58.12: And it will be said: «Yes, there is a reward for the righteous. Yes, there is a God who judges on earth.».

59.1: To the choirmaster. Do not destroy. A Psalm of David. When Saul sent men to guard his house to kill him.
59.2: Deliver me from my enemies, O my God, protect me from my adversaries.
59.3: Deliver me from those who commit iniquity and save me from men of blood.
59.4: For behold, they are lying in wait to take my life. Violent men are plotting against me, though I am not guilty, though I have not sinned, Lord.
59.5: Despite my innocence, they rush in and lie in ambush. Wake up, come to me and look.
59.6: You, Lord, God of hosts, God of Israel, rise up to punish all the nations; have no mercy on these traitors and evildoers. Sela.
59.7: They come back in the evening, they growl like dogs, they roam around the town.
59.8: Behold, their mouths spew forth insults, there are swords upon their lips: "Who is listening?" they say.
59.9: And you, Lord, laugh at them, you mock all the nations.
59.10: My strength lies in you, for God is my fortress.
59.11: The God who favors me will come to meet me. God will allow me to see my enemies.
59.12: Do not kill them, lest my people forget; make them wander by your power and overthrow them, O Lord, our shield.
59.13: Their mouths sin with every word from their lips; let them be caught in their own pride because of the curses and lies they utter.
59.14: Destroy them in your fury, destroy them, and let them be no more, so that they may know that God reigns over Jacob, to the ends of the earth. Selah.
59.15: They come back in the evening, they growl like dogs, they roam around the town.
59.16: They wander here and there, searching for their prey, and they grunt if they are not satiated.
59.17: And I will sing of your strength and in the morning I will celebrate your goodness, for you are my fortress, a refuge in the day of my distress.
59.18: O my strength, I will sing in your honor, for God is my fortress, the God who is favorable to me.

60.1: To the choirmaster, according to the Lily of the Testimony. A hymn of David, to be taught.
60.2: When he waged war against the Syrians of Mesopotamia and the Syrians of Zobah, and Joab returned and defeated Edom in the Valley of Salt, killing twelve thousand of them.
60.3: O God, you have rejected us, you have scattered us, you were angry, restore our favor.
60.4: You have shaken the country, you have torn it apart. Repair its breaches, for it is tottering.
60.5: You have shown your people harsh trials, you have made us drink a wine of dizziness.
60.6: But you have given a banner to those who fear you, so that it may be raised because of your truth. Selah.
60.7: So that your beloved ones may be delivered, save by your right hand and answer me.
60.8: God has spoken in his holiness: «I will rejoice greatly. I will have Shechem as my inheritance and I will measure out the valley of Succoth.
60.9: Gilead is mine, Manasseh is mine. Ephraim is the armor of my head and Judah my scepter.
60.10: Moab is the basin where I wash. On Edom, I throw my sandal. Land of the Philistines, shout for me!»
60.11: Who will lead me to the fortified city? Who will bring me to Edom?
60.12: Was it not you, O God, who had rejected us, O God, who no longer went out with our armies?
60.13: Lend us your aid against the oppressor. Man's help is but vanity.
60.14: With God we will accomplish great things; he will crush our enemies.

61.1: To the choirmaster, on stringed instruments, of David.
61.2: O God, hear my cries, be attentive to my prayer.
61.3: From the ends of the earth I cry to you; in the anguish of my heart lead me to the rock that I cannot reach.
61.4: For you are my refuge, a strong tower against the enemy.
61.5: I would like to remain forever in your tent, to take refuge under the shelter of your wings. Sela.
61.6: For you, O God, have heard my vows; you have given me the inheritance of those who revere your name.
61.7: Add days to the king's days, that his years may be prolonged from age to age.
61.8: May he remain on the throne forever before God. Command your goodness and your truth to keep him.
61.9: Then I will celebrate your name forever and fulfill my vows every day.

62.1: To the choirmaster… Idithun, a psalm of David.
62.2: Yes, to God my soul surrenders itself in peace; from him comes my help.
62.3: Yes, he is my rock and my salvation, he is my fortress; I will not be shaken.
62.4: How long will you attack a man, to bring him down all together, like a leaning fence, like a crumbling wall?
62.5: Yes, they plot to throw him down from his height; they delight in lies; they bless with their mouths and curse in their hearts. Selah.
62.6: Yes, O my soul, surrender yourself in peace to God, for from him comes my hope.
62.7: Yes, he is my rock and my salvation, he is my fortress, I will not be shaken.
62.8: My salvation and my glory rest on God; the rock of my strength, my refuge, is in God.
62.9: At all times, O people, trust in him, pour out your hearts to him, God is our refuge. Selah.
62.10: Yes, mortals are vanity, the sons of men are lies; in the balance they would rise, all together lighter than a breath.
62.11: Do not trust in violence, nor place your hope in theft. If your riches increase, do not set your heart on them.
62.12: God said a word, or two, that I heard: «The power belongs to God,
62.13: "To you also, Lord, be goodness." For you repay each according to their deeds.

63.1: A Psalm of David. When he was in the wilderness of Judah.
63.2: O God, you are my God, I seek you from dawn, my soul thirsts for you, my flesh longs for you, in a dry and weary land where there is no water.
63.3: This is how I beheld you in the sanctuary, to see your power and your glory.
63.4: For your grace is better than life: let my lips celebrate your praises.
63.5: Therefore I will bless you all my life; in your name I will lift up my hands.
63.6: My soul is satisfied, as with marrow and fat, and with joyful lips, my mouth praises you.
63.7: When I think of you on my bed, I meditate on you during the night watches.
63.8: For you are my help, and I rejoice in the shadow of your wings.
63.9: My soul clings to you; your right hand upholds me.
63.10: But they seek to take my life; they will go to the depths of the earth.
63.11: They will be handed over to the sword, they will be prey to jackals.
63.12: And the king will rejoice in God. Whoever swears by him will boast, for the mouths of liars will be silenced.

64.1: To the choirmaster, a psalm of David.
64.2: O God, hear my voice when I cry out; defend my life against an enemy who terrifies me.,
64.3: protect me from the plots of evildoers, from the rebellious band of wicked men,
64.4: who sharpen their tongues like swords, who prepare their arrows, their bitter words,
64.5: To unleash them in the shadows against the innocent, they unleash them against him unexpectedly, without fear.
64.6: They become more determined in their wicked plans; they conspire to lay their traps, saying, "Who will see them?"«
64.7: They only plot crimes: "We are ready," they say, "our plan is well laid out." The inside of man and his heart are an abyss.
64.8: But God launched his arrows at them: suddenly they were wounded.
64.9: They are thrown to the ground, the arrows of their tongues fall back upon them. All who see them nod their heads.
64.10: All men are filled with awe; they proclaim the work of God, they understand what he has done.
64.11: The righteous rejoice in the Lord and trust in him; all the upright in heart glory in him.

65.1: To the choirmaster, a psalm of David. A song.
65.2: To you is due the praise, O God; in Zion, it is in your honor that vows are fulfilled.
65.3: O you who hear prayer, all men come to you.
65.4: A heap of iniquities weighed upon me: you forgive our transgressions.
65.5: Blessed is the one you choose and bring near to dwell in your courts. May we be satisfied with the good things of your house, your holy temple.
65.6: Through wonders, you answer us in your justice, God of our salvation, hope of the ends of the earth and of distant seas.
65.7: He established the mountains by his strength; he is girded with his power.,
65.8: It calms the fury of the seas, the fury of their waves, and the tumult of the peoples.
65.9: The inhabitants of distant lands fear before your wonders, you gladden the ends of the earth, the East and the West.
65.10: You have visited the earth to give it abundance, you fill it with riches. The divine spring is filled with water: you prepare the grain, when you fertilize it thus.
65.11: Watering its furrows, leveling its clods, you soften it with showers, you bless its sprouts.
65.12: You crown the year with your blessings, and fat flows in your wake.
65.13: The desert pastures are watered and the hills are clothed with gladness.
65.14: The meadows are covered with herds and the valleys are adorned with ears of corn, all rejoice and sing.

66.1: To the choirmaster. A song, a psalm. Shout for joy to God, all the earth.
66.2: Sing the glory of his name, celebrate his praises magnificently.
66.3: Say to God: "How awesome are your works because of your omnipotence, your enemies flatter you.
66.4: Let all the earth bow down before you, let them sing in your honor, let them sing your name.» Selah.
66.5: Come and see the works of God. He is awesome in his plans for the sons of men.
66.6: He turned the sea into dry land, we crossed the river on foot, so we rejoiced in him.
66.7: He reigns forever by his power; his eyes watch over the nations: let not the rebels rise up. Selah.
66.8: Peoples, bless our God, let his praise resound.
66.9: He preserved the life of our soul and did not allow our foot to falter.
66.10: For you have tested us, O God, you have refined us like silver.
66.11: You led us into the net, you laid a burden on our backs.
66.12: You made men walk over our heads, we went through fire and water, but you brought us out of it to fill us with good things.
66.13: I come to your house with burnt offerings, to fulfill my vows to you,
66.14: that my lips uttered, that my mouth spoke in the day of my distress.
66.15: I offer you fat sheep as a burnt offering; with the smoke of the rams I sacrifice the bull and the young goat. Selah.
66.16: Come, listen, and I will tell you, all you who fear God, what he has done to my soul.
66.17: I cried out to him with my mouth, and his praise was on my tongue.
66.18: If I had seen iniquity in my heart, the Lord would not have answered me.
66.19: But God answered me; he listened to the voice of my prayer.
66.20: Blessed be God, who has not rejected my prayer and has not withheld his grace from me.

67.1: To the choirmaster. With stringed instruments, a psalm. A song.
67.2: May God be favorable to us and bless us, and make his face shine upon us. Sela.
67.3: so that your way may be known on earth and your salvation among all nations.
67.4: Let the peoples praise you, O God, let all the peoples praise you.
67.5: Let the nations rejoice and be glad, for you judge the peoples with equity and guide the nations on earth. Selah.
67.6: Let the peoples praise you, O God, let all the peoples praise you.
67.7: The earth has given its produce; may God, our God, bless us.
67.8: May God bless us and may all the ends of the earth revere Him.

68.1: To the choirmaster, a psalm of David. A song.
68.2: May God arise, and may his enemies be scattered, and may those who hate him flee before his face.
68.3: As smoke is driven away, so drive them away as wax melts before the fire; let the wicked perish before God.
68.4: But let the righteous rejoice and exult before God; let them be filled with gladness.
68.5: Sing to God, celebrate his name. Make way for him who rides through the plains, the Lord is his name, exult before him.
68.6: He is a father to orphans and judge of widows, God in his holy dwelling.
68.7: To the abandoned, God gives a home, he delivers the captives and restores them to happiness, only the rebels remain in the burning desert.
68.8: O God, when you went out at the head of your people, when you advanced in the desert, Sela.
68.9: the earth shook, the heavens themselves melted before God, Sinai trembled before God, the God of Israel.
68.10: You sent down, O God, a shower of blessings; your inheritance was exhausted, you comforted it.
68.11: Sent by you, animals came to fall there in your goodness, O God, you prepare their food for the unfortunate.
68.12: The Lord has made his word heard; the women who announce the victory are a large company.
68.13: «"The kings of the armies flee, flee, and she who dwells in the house divides the spoils."»
68.14: When you were lying in the midst of the sheepfolds, the dove's wings were covered with silver and its feathers shone with the brilliance of gold.
68.15: When the Almighty scattered the kings in the land, snow fell on the Selmon.
68.16: Mountain of God, mountain of Bashan, mountain of lofty peaks, mountain of Bashan,
68.17: Why do you look with envy, high peaked mountains, at the mountain that God chose as his dwelling place? Yes, the Lord will dwell there forever.
68.18: The chariot of God is thousands upon thousands; the Lord comes from Sinai into his sanctuary.
68.19: You ascend on the heights, leading the multitude of captives; you receive gifts from men. Even the rebellious will dwell near the Lord God.
68.20: Blessed be the Lord. Every day he bears our burden; he is the God who saves us. Sela.
68.21: God is for us the God of deliverance, the Lord, the Lord, can deliver from death.
68.22: Yes, God will crush the head of his enemies, the hairy forehead of him who walks in iniquity.
68.23: The Lord said, «I will bring them back from Bashan, I will bring them back from the depths of the sea,
68.24: so that you may plunge your foot in blood and that the tongues of your dogs may have their share of the enemies.»
68.25: We see your steps, O God, the steps of my God, my King, into the sanctuary.
68.26: In front are the singers, then the musicians, in the middle, young girls beating tambourines.
68.27: «Bless the Lord in the congregations, you who are from the source of Israel.»
68.28: Here is Benjamin, the youngest, who rules over them; here are the princes of Judah with their troops, the princes of Zebulun, the princes of Naphtali.
68.29: Command, O God, by your power; establish, O God, what you have done for us.
68.30: To your sanctuary, which is high above Jerusalem, kings will offer gifts.
68.31: Threaten the beast of the reeds, the herd of bulls with the calves of the peoples, so that they will bow down with pieces of silver. Scatter the nations that delight in war.
68.32: Let the great ones come from Egypt, let Ethiopia hasten to stretch out her hands to God.
68.33: Kingdoms of the earth, sing to God, praise the Lord. Sela.
68.34: Sing to him who is carried upon the heavens, the ancient heavens. Behold, he makes his voice heard, his mighty voice.
68.35: Acknowledge the power of God. His majesty is over Israel and his power is in the clouds.
68.36: From your sanctuary, O God, you are awesome. The God of Israel gives strength and power to his people. Blessed be God.

69.1: To the choirmaster, according to the lilies, of David.
69.2: Save me, O God, for the waters have risen up to my soul.
69.3: I am sunk in deep mud and there is nowhere to put my foot. I have fallen into a watery abyss and the waves are engulfing me.
69.4: I exhaust myself screaming, my throat is on fire, my eyes are consumed in waiting for my God.
69.5: They are more numerous than the hairs on my head, those who hate me without cause; powerful are those who wish to destroy me, who are, without reason, my enemies. What I did not steal, I must return.
69.6: O God, you know my folly and my sins are not hidden from you.
69.7: Let those who hope in you not be put to shame because of me, Lord, Lord of hosts, let those who seek you not be confounded because of me, God of Israel.
69.8: For it is for you that I bear reproach, that shame covers my face.
69.9: I have become a stranger to my brothers, an unknown person to my mother's sons.
69.10: For zeal for your house consumes me, and the insults of those who insult you fall upon me.
69.11: I shed tears and I fast, and I am made a subject of shame.
69.12: I take a bag for clothes and I am the object of their sarcasm.
69.13: Those who sit at the gate talk about me, and the drinkers of strong liquor make songs about me.
69.14: And I address my prayer to you, Lord, in the favorable time, O God, according to your great goodness, hear me, according to the truth of your salvation.
69.15: Pull me out of the mud and let me not remain mired in it; let me be delivered from my enemies and the deep waters.
69.16: May the waves not overwhelm me again, may the abyss not swallow me up, may the pit not close over me.
69.17: Hear me, Lord, for your goodness is compassionate; in your great mercy turn to me.,
69.18: And do not hide your face from your servant. I am in anguish; answer me quickly.
69.19: Draw near to my soul, deliver it, save me from my enemies.
69.20: You know my reproach, my shame, my ignominy; all my persecutors are before you.
69.21: Shame has broken my heart and I am sick; I wait for pity, but in vain, for comforters, but I find none.
69.22: For food they give me bitter herbs. In my thirst, they give me vinegar to drink.
69.23: May their table be a trap for them, a net within their security.
69.24: May their eyes grow dim so they can no longer see. May their backs tremble forever.
69.25: Pour out your anger upon them and let the fire of your wrath overwhelm them.
69.26: May their dwelling be devastated, may there be no more inhabitants in their tents.
69.27: For they persecute the one you strike, they recount the sufferings of the one you wound.
69.28: Add iniquity to their iniquity, and let them have no share in your righteousness.
69.29: May they be blotted out of the book of life and not be enrolled with the righteous.
69.30: I am unhappy and suffering; may your help, O God, raise me up.
69.31: I will celebrate God's name with songs, I will exalt him with thanksgiving.
69.32: And the Lord will consider them more pleasing than a bull, than a young bull with horns and hooves.
69.33: The unfortunate, seeing him, will rejoice, and you who seek God, your heart will revive.
69.34: For the Lord listens to the poor and does not despise his prisoners.
69.35: Let heaven and earth praise him, the seas and all that moves in them.
69.36: for God will save Zion and rebuild the cities of Judah, and people will settle there and take possession of them.
69.37: The race of his servants will inherit it, and those who love his name will have their dwelling place there.

70.1: To the choirmaster, of David. In memorial.
70.2: O God, hasten to deliver me. Lord, hasten to help me.
70.3: Let them be ashamed and confused, those who seek my soul. Let them turn back and blush, those who desire my downfall.
70.4: Let them turn back in shame, those who say, "Ha! Ha!"«
70.5: May all who seek you rejoice and be glad in you. May those who love your salvation continually say, «Glory to God.».
70.6: I am poor and needy. O God, hasten to me. You are my help and my deliverer: Lord, do not delay.

71.1: Lord, in you I have placed my refuge; may I never be put to shame.
71.2: In your righteousness, deliver me and save me. Incline your ear to me and rescue me.
71.3: Be for me an inaccessible rock where I can always retreat. You have commanded me to save you, for you are my rock and my fortress.
71.4: My God, deliver me from the hand of the wicked, from the hand of the unjust and cruel man.
71.5: For you are my hope, Lord God, the object of my trust since my youth.
71.6: It is on you that I have relied since my birth, you who brought me forth from the womb, you are my praise forever.
71.7: I am like a prodigy to the crowd, but you are my mighty refuge.
71.8: May my mouth be filled with your praise, may it exalt your magnificence every day.
71.9: Do not cast me away in the days of my old age. Do not abandon me in the decline of my strength.
71.10: For my enemies conspire against me, and those who spy on my soul plot together.,
71.11: saying: "God has abandoned him. Pursue him. Seize him; there is no one to defend him."«
71.12: O God, do not be far from me, my God, hasten to my aid.
71.13: May they be confounded and perish, those who seek my life. May they be covered with shame and reproach, those who seek my downfall.
71.14: For my part, I will always hope for all your praise, and I will add new praise.
71.15: My mouth will proclaim your righteousness, all day long your favors, for I do not know their number.
71.16: I will speak of your mighty works, Lord God, I will recall your righteousness, yours alone.
71.17: O God, you have instructed me from my youth and to this day I proclaim your wonders.
71.18: Even until old age and gray hair, O God, do not forsake me, so that I may make known your strength to the present generation, your power to the next.
71.19: Your justice, O God, reaches to the heavens; you who accomplish great things, O God, who is like you?
71.20: You who have caused us many terrible trials. But you will restore our lives and from the depths of the earth, you will raise us up.
71.21: You will restore my greatness and once again you will comfort me.
71.22: And I will praise you with the sound of the lute, I will sing of your faithfulness, O my God, I will celebrate you with the harp, Holy One of Israel.
71.23: Joy will be on my lips when I sing to you, and in my soul, which you have delivered.
71.24: And my tongue will proclaim your righteousness every day, for those who seek my downfall will be ashamed and blushed.

72.1: Of Solomon. O God, give your judgments to the king and your righteousness to the king's son.
72.2: May he lead your people with justice and your poor with equity.
72.3: May the mountains bring peace to the people, and the hills, through justice.
72.4: May he do justice to the unfortunate of his people, may he assist the children of the poor, and may he crush the oppressor.
72.5: May you be revered, as long as the sun endures, as long as the moon shines, from age to age.
72.6: Let it fall like rain on the grass, like showers that water the earth.
72.7: May the righteous flourish in his days, with abundant peace, until the moon is no more.
72.8: He will rule from sea to sea, from the River to the ends of the earth.
72.9: Before him will the inhabitants of the desert prostrate themselves, and his enemies will bite the dust.
72.10: The kings of Tharsis and the islands will pay tribute, the kings of Sheba and Meroë will offer gifts.
72.11: All kings will bow down before him, all nations will serve him.
72.12: For he will deliver the poor who cry out to him, and the needy who have no helper.
72.13: He will have pity on the wretched and the needy, and he will save the life of the poor.
72.14: He will free them from oppression and violence, and their blood will be precious in his eyes.
72.15: They will live and give him gold from Sheba. They will continually make vows to him, they will bless him every day.
72.16: May the land be abundant in grain, even to the mountaintops; may its ears of grain wave like the trees of Lebanon; may men flourish in the city like the grass of the field.
72.17: May his name endure forever as long as the sun shines, may his name spread, may blessings be sought in him, may all nations proclaim him blessed.
72.18: Blessed be the Lord God, the God of Israel, who alone performs wonders.
72.19: Blessed be His glorious name forever. May the whole earth be filled with His glory. Amen. Amen.
72.20: The prayers of David, son of Jesse, have come to an end.

73.1: A Psalm of Asaph. Yes, God is good to Israel, to those who are pure in heart.
73.2: However, I was about to falter, my foot almost slipped.
73.3: For I was indignant against the godless, when I saw the prosperity of the wicked.
73.4: For them, there is no pain until death; their bodies are full of vigor.
73.5: They have no share in the labor of mortals, they are not struck down with the rest of men.
73.6: Pride is the adornment of their necks, and violence is the precious robe that covers them.
73.7: Iniquity comes forth from their entrails; the thoughts of their hearts are revealed.
73.8: They mock, they speak of iniquity and violence, they utter haughty speeches.
73.9: They direct their mouths against the heavens themselves, and their tongues practice on the earth.
73.10: That is why my people turn to them, they swallow the water in great gulps.
73.11: They say, "How would God know? How would the Most High know?"«
73.12: Such are the wicked, always happy, they increase their wealth.
73.13: So it was in vain that I kept my heart pure, that I washed my hands in innocence,
73.14: All day long I am struck; every morning my punishment is there.»
73.15: If I had said, "I want to speak like them," I would have betrayed the race of your children.
73.16: I thought about it to understand these things; the difficulty seemed great to me.,
73.17: until I have entered the sanctuary of God and considered their final fate.
73.18: Yes, you place them on slippery tracks, you make them fall and they are nothing but ruins.
73.19: What? In an instant they are destroyed. They are annihilated, they disappear in catastrophes.
73.20: As one does with a dream upon waking, Lord, when you awake, you reject their image.
73.21: When my heart grew sour and I felt deeply moved,
73.22: I was stupid and unintelligent, I was like a brute in front of you.
73.23: But I will be with you forever, you took my right hand,
73.24: You will guide me with your counsel and then receive me into glory.
73.25: Who else but you do I have in heaven? With you, I desire nothing on earth.
73.26: My flesh and my heart are consumed, but God is the rock of my heart and my portion forever.
73.27: Behold, those who stray from you perish; you exterminate all those who are unfaithful to you.
73.28: For me, being united with God is my happiness. In the Lord God I place my trust to recount all your works.

74.1: A Psalm of Asaph. Why, O God, have you rejected us forever? Why is your anger kindled against the flock of your pasture?
74.2: Remember your people whom you acquired in days of old, whom you redeemed to be the tribe of your inheritance. Remember your mountain Zion, where you made your dwelling.
74.3: Turn your steps towards these irreparable ruins; the enemy has ravaged everything in the sanctuary.
74.4: Your adversaries have roared in the midst of your holy courts; they have set up their emblems as symbols.
74.5: We saw them, like the lumberjack, raising his axe in a thick forest.
74.6: And now, all the sculptures together, they smashed them with axes and hammers.
74.7: They have burned your sanctuary; they have desecrated and profaned the dwelling place of your name.
74.8: They said in their hearts, "Let us destroy them all together." They burned all the holy places in the land.
74.9: We no longer see our signs, there is no longer a prophet, and no one among us knows how long this will last.
74.10: How long, O God, will the oppressor insult, will the enemy blaspheme your name forever?
74.11: Why do you withdraw your hand and your right hand? Pull it out of your bosom and destroy them.
74.12: Yet God is my king from ancient times, he who has brought about so many deliverances on earth.
74.13: It is you who divided the sea by your power, you who broke the heads of the monsters in the waters.
74.14: It was you who crushed the heads of Leviathan and gave it as food to the desert people.
74.15: It is you who caused the spring and the torrent to gush forth, you who dried up the rivers that never run dry.
74.16: Yours is the day, yours is the night, you created the moon and the sun.
74.17: You are the one who set all the boundaries of the earth, summer and winter; you are the one who established them.
74.18: Remember, the enemy insults the Lord, a foolish people blasphemes your name.
74.19: Do not give the soul of your turtledove to the beasts, do not forget forever the lives of your poor.
74.20: Beware of your alliance, for every corner of the country is full of dens of violence.
74.21: May the oppressed not return in shame, may the unfortunate and the poor bless your name.
74.22: Arise, O God, take up your cause, remember the insults that the fool hurls at you every day.
74.23: Don't forget the clamor of your adversaries, the ever-increasing insolence of those who hate you.

75.1: To the choirmaster. "Do not destroy." Psalm of Asaph. Song.
75.2: We praise you, O God, we praise you, your name is near, your wonders are recounted.
75.3: «"When the time comes, I will judge fairly.".
75.4: The earth is shaken, and all who dwell on it, but I will establish its pillars.» Selah.
75.5: I say to the proud: Do not be proud, and to the wicked: Do not lift up your heads.
75.6: Don't hold your head so high, don't speak with such arrogance.
75.7: For it is neither from the east, nor from the west, nor from the desert of the mountains.
75.8: No, it is God who exercises judgment; he humbles one and exalts another.
75.9: For in the Lord's hand is a cup, full of aromatic wine, which he pours out: yes, all the wicked of the earth will suck up the dregs and drink them.
75.10: And I will publish forever, I will sing the praises of the God of Jacob.
75.11: And I will cut off all the horns of the wicked, and the horns of the righteous will be exalted.

76.1: To the choirmaster. With stringed instruments, a psalm of Asaph, a song.
76.2: God has made himself known in Judah; in Israel his name is great.
76.3: His tabernacle is in Salem, and his dwelling place is in Zion.
76.4: It was there that he shattered the lightning bolts of the bow, the shield, the sword, and war. Sela.
76.5: You shine in your majesty, on the mountains from which you swoop down on your prey.
76.6: They were stripped bare, these kind-hearted heroes, they fell asleep in their sleep, they did not know, all these valiant men, how to use their arms.
76.7: At your threat, God of Jacob, chariot and steed remained motionless.
76.8: You are formidable. Who can stand before you when your anger erupts?
76.9: From heaven you proclaimed the sentence, the earth trembled and fell silent,
76.10: when God arose to do justice, to save all the unfortunate of the land. Sela.
76.11: Thus man's fury turns to glory and the remnants of anger.
76.12: Make vows and fulfill them to the Lord your God, that all those around him may bring gifts to the awesome God.
76.13: He humbles the pride of the powerful; he is fearsome to the kings of the earth.

77.1: To the songmaster, Idithun, psalm of Asaph.
77.2: My voice rises to God and I cry out. My voice rises to God, that he may hear me.
77.3: In the day of my distress, I seek the Lord; my hands are stretched out at night without ceasing; my soul refuses all consolation.
77.4: I remember God and I groan, I meditate and my spirit is downcast. Selah.
77.5: You hold my eyelids open and, in my agitation, I cannot speak.
77.6: So I think of bygone days, of years gone by.
77.7: I remember my hymns during the night, I reflect within my heart and my mind asks:
77.8: «Will the Lord reject forever? Will he no longer be favorable?”
77.9: Is His goodness exhausted forever? Are His promises for future ages fulfilled?
77.10: Has God forgotten his clemency? Has he, in his anger, withdrawn his mercy? Sela.
77.11: I say, "What causes my suffering is that the right hand of the Most High has changed."«
77.12: I want to recall the works of the Lord, for I remember your wonders of old.
77.13: I want to reflect on all your works and meditate on your great deeds.
77.14: O God, your ways are holy; what God is as great as our God?
77.15: You are the God who performs wonders; you have manifested your power among the nations.
77.16: With your arm you have delivered your people, the sons of Jacob and Joseph. Sela.
77.17: The waters saw you, O God, the waters saw you and trembled, the depths were shaken.
77.18: The clouds poured forth their waters, the clouds made their voices heard, and your arrows flew in every direction.
77.19: Your thunder resounded in the whirlwind, lightning illuminated the world, the earth shuddered and trembled.
77.20: The sea was your path, the great waters your trail, and your tracks could not be recognized.
77.21: You led your people like a flock, by the hand of Moses and Aaron.

78.1: A Psalm of Asaph. Listen, O my people, to my teaching; give ear to the words of my mouth.
78.2: I will open my mouth to speak pronouncements, I will publish the mysteries of ancient times.
78.3: What we have heard, what we have learned, what our fathers have told us,
78.4: We will not hide it from their children; we will tell the next generation the praises of the Lord and his power and the wonders he has performed.
78.5: He established a rule for Jacob, he instituted a law in Israel, which he commanded our fathers to teach to their children,
78.6: so that they may be known to future generations, to children who would be born and who would grow up, to tell them in turn to their children.
78.7: Thus they would put their trust in God, they would not forget God's works, and they would observe his precepts.,
78.8: They would not be, like their fathers, an unruly and rebellious race, a fickle race whose spirit is not faithful to God.
78.9: The sons of Ephraim, skilled archers, turned their backs on the day of battle.,
78.10: They did not keep God's covenant; they refused to walk according to his law.,
78.11: They have forgotten his great works and the wonders he had shown them.
78.12: Before their fathers, he had performed wonders in the land of Egypt, in the countryside of Tanis.
78.13: He parted the sea to let them pass through, He held back the waters piled up like a heap.
78.14: He led them by day by cloud and all night by brilliant fire.
78.15: He split the rocks in the desert and gave drink like abundant streams.
78.16: From the rock he made streams spring forth and water flow in torrents.
78.17: But they continued to sin against him, to rebel against the Most High in the desert.
78.18: They tested God in their hearts by asking for food according to their cravings.
78.19: They spoke against God and said, «Can God set up a table in the desert?
78.20: »Now that he has struck the rock, waters have gushed out and torrents have overflowed, will he also be able to give us bread or provide meat for his people?”
78.21: The Lord heard and was angry; a fire was kindled against Jacob, and your anger rose against Israel.,
78.22: because they had not had faith in God and had not hoped for his help.
78.23: However, he commanded the clouds above and opened the gates of heaven,
78.24: He rained down manna on them to feed them and gave them the wheat from heaven.
78.25: Everyone ate the bread of the strong. He sent them food until they were full.
78.26: He caused the east wind to blow across the sky, and by his power he brought the south wind,
78.27: He rained down upon them meat like dust and winged birds like the sand of the sea.
78.28: He made them fall in the middle of their camp, around their tents.
78.29: They ate and were more than satisfied. God gave them what they desired.
78.30: They had not yet satisfied their craving, and their food was still in their mouths.,
78.31: When God's anger rose against them, he struck down the most well-fed, he struck down the young men of Israel.
78.32: After all this, they sinned again and did not believe in his wonders.
78.33: Then he dissipated their days like a breath and their years with a sudden end.
78.34: When he struck them dead, they sought him out, they returned, eager to find God again,
78.35: They remembered that God was their rock and the Most High God their liberator.
78.36: But they deceived him with their words, and their tongues lied to him.,
78.37: Their hearts were not steadfast toward him; they were not faithful to his covenant.
78.38: But he is merciful, he forgives sin and does not destroy; often he restrained his anger and did not give free rein to his fury.
78.39: He remembered that they were only flesh, a breath that departs and never returns.
78.40: How often they rebelled against him in the desert, how often they angered him in the solitude.
78.41: They continued to test God and provoke the Holy One of Israel.
78.42: They no longer remembered his power, the day he delivered them from the oppressor,
78.43: where he showed his wonders in Egypt, his marvelous deeds in the campaigns of Tanis.
78.44: He turned their rivers into blood, and they could not drink from their streams.
78.45: He sent against them the gnat that devoured them and the frog that caused them to perish.
78.46: He gave their crops to the grasshopper, the produce of their labor to its swarms.
78.47: He destroyed their vineyards with hail and their sycamore trees with hailstones.
78.48: He abandoned their cattle to the hail and their herds to the lightning strikes.
78.49: He unleashed against them the fire of his wrath, fury, rage, and distress, a whole army of angels of misfortune.
78.50: He gave free rein to his anger; he did not save their souls from death, but delivered their lives to destruction.
78.51: He struck down all the firstborn in Egypt, the first fruits of strength under the tents of Ham.
78.52: He led his people out like sheep, he guided them like a flock in the desert.
78.53: He led them safely, without them having anything to fear, and the sea swallowed their enemies.
78.54: He brought them to his holy border, to the mountain that his right hand had conquered.
78.55: He drove out the nations before them, assigned them by lot their portion of inheritance, and made the tribes of Israel dwell in their tents.
78.56: However, they still tried and provoked the Most High God and did not observe his ordinances.
78.57: They have turned away and been unfaithful like their fathers; they have turned away like a deceitful bow.
78.58: They angered him with their high places, they aroused his jealousy with their idols.
78.59: God heard and was indignant; he took a great aversion to Israel.
78.60: He scorned the dwelling place of Shiloh, the tent where he lived among men.
78.61: He surrendered his strength to captivity and his majesty to the hands of the enemy.
78.62: He abandoned his people to the sword and he railed against his heritage.
78.63: The fire devoured her young men and her virgins did not hear the wedding song.
78.64: His priests fell by the sword, and his widows did not mourn.
78.65: The Lord awoke, like a sleeping man, like a warrior overcome by wine.
78.66: He struck his enemies from behind, inflicting eternal shame upon them.
78.67: But he abhorred Joseph's tent and he divorced the tribe of Ephraim.
78.68: He chose the tribe of Judah, the mountain of Zion which he loved.
78.69: And he built his sanctuary like the heights of heaven, like the earth which he founded forever.
78.70: He chose David, his servant, and took him from the sheepfolds; ;
78.71: He took him from behind the mother ewes, to shepherd Jacob, his people, and Israel, his inheritance.
78.72: And David guided them in the uprightness of his heart and led them with a skillful hand.

79.1: A Psalm of Asaph. O God, the nations have invaded your inheritance, they have defiled your holy temple, they have made Jerusalem a heap of ruins.
79.2: They have given the corpses of your servants as food to the birds of the air and the flesh of your faithful ones to the beasts of the earth.
79.3: They shed their blood like water all around Jerusalem, and no one was there to bury them.
79.4: We have become an object of scorn to our neighbors, of ridicule and mockery to those around us.
79.5: How long, Lord, will you be angry forever, and will your wrath burn like fire?
79.6: Pour out your fury on the nations that do not know you, on the kingdoms that do not call upon your name.
79.7: For they devoured Jacob and ravaged his dwelling.
79.8: Do not remember against us the iniquities of our fathers. Let your compassion come quickly to meet us, for our misery is at its fullest.
79.9: Help us, God of our salvation, for the glory of your name. Deliver us and forgive our sins for your name's sake.
79.10: Why should the nations say, "Where is their God?" Let it be known among the nations and before our eyes the vengeance you exact for the blood of your servants when it is shed.
79.11: Let the groans of the captives rise up to you. According to the greatness of your arm, save those who are about to perish.
79.12: Lord, make the insults that our neighbors have committed against you fall seven times over into their hearts.
79.13: And we, your people, the flock of your pasture, will give you glory forever; from age to age, we will proclaim your praises.

80.1: To the choirmaster, according to the lilies of the testimony, a psalm of Asaph.
80.2: Shepherd of Israel, give ear, you who lead Joseph like a flock, you who sit enthroned upon the Cherubim, appear in splendor.
80.3: Before Ephraim, Benjamin and Manasseh, rouse your strength and come to our aid.
80.4: O God, restore us, let your face shine upon us and we shall be saved.
80.5: Lord, God of hosts, how long will you be angry with the prayer of your people?
80.6: You fed them with the bread of tears, you gave them abundant tears to drink.
80.7: You have made us a subject of contention to our neighbors, and our enemies mock us.
80.8: God of hosts, restore us, let your face shine upon us and we shall be saved.
80.9: You brought a vine out of Egypt, you drove out the nations and planted it.
80.10: You made room for it, it put down its roots and filled the earth.
80.11: Its shadow covered the mountains and its branches the cedars of God,
80.12: It extended its branches to the Sea and its offshoots to the River.
80.13: Why did you break down its fences, so that all passersby devastate it?
80.14: The wild boar in the forest devours it, and the beasts of the fields feed on it.
80.15: God of hosts, come back, look down from heaven and see, consider this vine.
80.16: Protect what your right hand has planted and the son you have chosen for yourself.
80.17: She is burned by fire, she is cut, before your menacing face, everything perishes.
80.18: Let your hand be upon the man of your right hand, upon the son of man whom you have chosen for yourself.
80.19: And we will never turn away from you again. Give us life and we will call upon your name.
80.20: Lord, God of hosts, restore us. Let your face shine upon us, and we shall be saved.

81.1: To the choirmaster, according to the Gitthian. Of Asaph.
81.2: Sing with gladness to God, our strength; shout for joy to the God of Jacob.
81.3: Sing the hymn, to the sound of the tambourine, the harmonious harp and the lute.
81.4: Sound the trumpet at the new moon, at the full moon, for the day of our feast.
81.5: For it is a precept for Israel, an ordinance of the God of Jacob.
81.6: He made it a law for Joseph when he marched against the land of Egypt. I hear a voice I do not know:
81.7: «"I took the burden off her shoulder and her hands left the basket.".
81.8: You cried out in distress and I delivered you; I answered you from the heart of the stormy cloud. I tested you at the waters of Meribah. Sela.
81.9: «Listen, my people, I want to give you a warning, Israel, may you listen to me.
81.10: Let there be no foreign god among you; do not worship the god of another people.
81.11: «I am the Lord your God, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt. Open your mouth and I will fill it.”.
81.12: «But my people did not listen to my voice; Israel did not obey me.”.
81.13: So I abandoned him to the hardening of his heart, and they followed their own advice.
81.14: «Oh, if only my people would listen to me, if only Israel would walk in my ways,
81.15: Soon I will confound their enemies, I will turn my hand against their oppressors.
81.16: «Those who hate the Lord would flatter him, and the existence of Israel would be assured forever.”.
81.17: I would feed him with the finest wheat and satisfy him with honey from the rock.»

82.1: A Psalm of Asaph. God stands in the assembly of the Almighty; in the midst of the gods he renders his judgment:
82.2: «"How long will you judge unjustly and take the side of the wicked? Sela.".
82.3: «"Do justice to the weak and the orphan, and uphold the rights of the unfortunate and the poor,
82.4: Save the poor and needy, deliver them from the hand of the wicked.
82.5: «They have neither knowledge nor understanding; they walk in darkness; all the foundations of the earth are shaken.”.
82.6: «"I said: You are gods, you are all sons of the Most High.
82.7: However, you will die like men, you will fall like any other prince.»
82.8: Arise, O God, judge the earth, for all the nations belong to you.

83.2: O God, do not remain in inaction, do not be silent and do not rest, O God.
83.3: For behold, your enemies are noisily agitated, those who hate you are raising their heads.
83.4: They are plotting a treacherous scheme against your people; they are conspiring against those you protect.
83.5: «Come,» they say, “let us exterminate them from among the nations, and let the name of Israel be spoken no more.”
83.6: They all conspire together, forming an alliance against you.,
83.7: the tents of Edom and the Ishmaelites, Moab and the Hagarenes,
83.8: Gebal, Ammon and Amalek, the Philistines with the inhabitants of Tyre.
83.9: Assur also joined them and lent his arm to Lot's children. Sela.
83.10: Treat them like Midian, like Sisera, like Jabin at the Cishon River.
83.11: They were destroyed at Endor, they served as fertilizer for the earth.
83.12: Treat their leaders like Oreb and Zeb, and all their princes like Zeba and Shalman.
83.13: For they say, «Let us seize the dwellings of God.»
83.14: My God, make them like the whirlwind, like the stubble that the wind carries away.
83.15: Like fire devours the forest, like flames engulf the mountains,
83.16: So pursue them in your storm, terrify them in your hurricane.
83.17: Cover their faces with shame, so that they may seek your name, Lord.
83.18: May they forever be in confusion and terror, in shame and ruin.
83.19: Let them know that you, your name is the Lord, you alone are the Most High over all the earth.

84.1: To the choirmaster, according to the Gittite, a psalm of the sons of Korah.
84.2: How lovely are your dwelling places, Lord of hosts.
84.3: My soul faints, longing for the courts of the Lord; my heart and my flesh yearn for the living God.
84.4: Even the sparrow finds a home and the swallow a nest where she lays her young: Your altars, Lord of hosts, my king and my God.
84.5: Blessed are those who dwell in your house. They can still praise you. Selah.
84.6: Blessed are the men whose strength lies in you; they think only of holy ascents.
84.7: When they cross the Valley of Tears, they transform it into a place full of springs, and the autumn rain also covers it with blessings.
84.8: During their journey their strength increased, and they appeared before God in Zion:
84.9: «Lord, God of hosts,» they say, “hear my prayer, give ear, God of Jacob.” Selah.
84.10: You who are our shield, see, O God, and look upon the face of your Anointed One.
84.11: For one day in your courts is better than a thousand; I would rather be a doorkeeper in the house of my God than dwell in the tents of the wicked.
84.12: For the Lord God is a sun and shield; the Lord bestows favor and glory; he withholds no good thing from those who walk in innocence.
84.13: Lord of hosts, blessed is he who trusts in you.

85.1: To the choirmaster, a psalm of the sons of Korah.
85.2: You have been favorable to your country, Lord; you have restored the captives of Jacob.,
85.3: You have forgiven your people their iniquity; you have covered all their sins. Selah.
85.4: You have withdrawn all your indignation, you have turned away from the heat of your anger.
85.5: Restore us, God of our salvation, and put an end to your feelings against us.
85.6: Will you always be angry with us? Will you prolong your wrath forever?
85.7: Will you not restore us to life, so that your people may rejoice in you?
85.8: Lord, show us your goodness and grant us your salvation.
85.9: I want to listen to what the Lord God will say. He has words of peace for his people and for his faithful, provided they do not return to their folly.
85.10: Yes, his salvation is near to those who fear him, and glory will dwell again in our land.
85.11: Goodness and truth will meet, justice and peace will embrace.
85.12: Truth will spring forth from the earth, and justice will look down from heaven.
85.13: The Lord himself will grant all good things, and our land will yield its fruit.
85.14: Justice will go before him and pave the way for his steps.

86.1: David's Prayer. Give ear, Lord, answer me, for I am poor and needy.
86.2: Guard my soul, for I am devout; save your servant, O my God, who trusts in you.
86.3: Have mercy on me, Lord, for I cry out to you all day long.
86.4: Rejoice the soul of your servant, for to you, Lord, I lift up my soul.
86.5: For you are good, Lord, and merciful and full of compassion to all who call upon you.
86.6: Lord, give ear to my prayer, be attentive to the voice of my supplications.
86.7: I call upon you in the day of my distress, and you will answer me.
86.8: No one among the gods can compare with you, Lord; nothing resembles your works.
86.9: All the nations you have made will come and worship before you, Lord, and give glory to your name.
86.10: For you are great and you perform wonders. You alone are God.
86.11: Teach me your ways, Lord, that I may walk in your faithfulness. Attach my heart to the fear of your name.
86.12: I will praise you with all my heart, Lord, my God, and I will glorify your name forever.
86.13: For your kindness is great towards me; you have drawn my soul from the depths of Sheol.
86.14: O God, the proud have risen up against me, a band of violent men seek my life, without any regard for you.
86.15: But you, Lord, are a merciful and compassionate God, slow to anger, abounding in steadfast love and faithfulness.
86.16: Turn your eyes toward me and have mercy on me, give your strength to your servant and save the son of your handmaid.
86.17: Show your kindness to me, that my enemies may see it and be confounded, for it is you, Lord, who assists and comforts me.

87.1: A Psalm of the sons of Korah. A Song. He founded it on the holy mountains.
87.2: The Lord loves the gates of Zion more than all the dwellings of Jacob.
87.3: Glorious things have been said about you, city of God. Selah.
87.4: «Among those who know me, I will name Rahab and Babylon; behold, the Philistines and Tyre, with Ethiopia: for there they were born.»
87.5: And it will be said of Zion: This one and that one were born there; it is He, the Most High, who founded it.
87.6: The Lord will record in the rolls of the peoples: «This one was born there.» Sela.
87.7: And singers and musicians say, "All my sources are in you."«

88.1: Songs, a psalm of the sons of Korah. To the choirmaster. To be sung with a mournful tone. A psalm of Heman the Ezrahite.
88.2: Lord, God of my salvation, when I cry out to you in the night,
88.3: May my prayer reach your presence; listen to my supplications.
88.4: for my soul is full of evils and my life draws near to Sheol.
88.5: I am counted among those who go down into the pit; I am like a man at the end of his strength.
88.6: I am like one abandoned among the dead, like the corpses lying in the tomb, whom you no longer remember and who are taken from your hand.
88.7: You threw me into the pit, into the darkness, into the abyss.
88.8: Your fury weighs heavily upon me, you overwhelm me with all your waves. Sela.
88.9: You have taken my friends away from me, you have made me an object of horror to them, I am imprisoned without being able to get out.
88.10: My eyes are consumed by suffering. I call upon you all day long, Lord, I stretch out my hands to you.
88.11: Will you perform a miracle for the dead, or will the shades rise to praise you? Sela.
88.12: Is your goodness proclaimed in the tomb, your faithfulness in the grave?
88.13: Are your wonders known in the region of darkness, and your righteousness in the land of forgetfulness?
88.14: And I, Lord, cry out to you; my prayer comes to you early in the morning.
88.15: Why, Lord, do you reject my soul, hide your face from me?
88.16: I have been unhappy and dying since my youth, under the weight of your terrors, I do not know what to become.
88.17: Your fury overwhelms me, your terrors crush me.
88.18: Like overflowing waters they surround me all day long, they all besiege me together.
88.19: You have taken away from me my friends and my relatives; my companions are the darkness of the grave.

89.2: I want to sing forever of the Lord's goodness; to all generations my mouth will make known your faithfulness.
89.3: For I say: Goodness is an eternal building; in heaven you have established your faithfulness.
89.4: «I have made a covenant with my chosen one, I have sworn this oath to David, my servant:
89.5: "I want to establish your race forever, to set up your throne for all generations." Sela.
89.6: The heavens praise your wonders, Lord, and your faithfulness in the assembly of the saints.
89.7: For who in heaven can compare with the Lord? Who is like the Lord among the sons of God?
89.8: God is awesome in the great assembly of the saints; he is dreadful to all who surround him.
89.9: Lord, God of hosts, who is like you? You are mighty, Lord, and your faithfulness surrounds you.
89.10: It is you who tames the pride of the sea; when its waves rise, it is you who calms them.
89.11: It was you who crushed Rahab like a corpse, who scattered your enemies by the strength of your arm.
89.12: Yours are the heavens, and yours also the earth, the world and all that is in it; you founded it.
89.13: You created the north and the south; Tabor and Hermon tremble at your name.
89.14: Your arm is strong, your hand is strong, your right hand is raised.
89.15: Justice and fairness are the foundation of your throne; kindness and faithfulness stand before your face.
89.16: Blessed are the people who know joyful acclamations, who walk in the light of your face, Lord.
89.17: He rejoices continually in your name and is exalted by your righteousness.
89.18: For you are his glory and his power, and your favor exalts our strength.
89.19: For from the Lord comes our shield, and from the Holy One of Israel our king.
89.20: You once spoke in a vision to your beloved, saying: «I have helped a hero, I have raised up a young man from among the people.
89.21: I have found David my servant, and I have anointed him with my holy oil.
89.22: My hand will be constantly with him and my arm will strengthen him.
89.23: «The enemy will not surprise him, and the son of iniquity will not prevail over him.”.
89.24: I will crush his adversaries before him and strike down those who hate him.
89.25: My faithfulness and my goodness will be with him, and through my name his power will increase.
89.26: I will stretch out his hand over the sea and his right hand over the rivers.
89.27: «He will call on me: You are my Father, my God, and the rock of my salvation,
89.28: and I will make him the firstborn, the highest of the kings of the earth.
89.29: I will maintain my kindness to him forever, and my covenant with him will be faithful.
89.30: I will establish his descendants forever, and his throne will endure for all the days of heaven.
89.31: «If his sons forsake my law and do not walk according to my ordinances,
89.32: if they violate my precepts and do not observe my commandments,
89.33: I will punish their transgressions with the rod and their iniquities with blows,
89.34: But I will not withdraw my kindness from him, nor will I betray my faithfulness.
89.35: «I will not violate my covenant and I will not alter the word that has come out of my lips.”.
89.36: I swore once by my holiness, no, I will not lie to David.
89.37: His posterity will endure forever, his throne will be before me like the sun,
89.38: Like the moon, it is established forever, and the witness in heaven is faithful.» Selah.
89.39: And you have rejected and scorned and become angry with your Anointed One.
89.40: You have loathed the alliance with his servant, you have cast his desecrated diadem to the ground.
89.41: You have torn down all its walls, you have laid its fortresses to ruin.
89.42: All the passers-by are robbing him; he has become the disgrace of his neighbors.
89.43: You have exalted the right hand of his oppressors, you have gladdened all his enemies.
89.44: You turned back the edge of his sword and you did not support him in the fight.
89.45: You have stripped it of its splendor and thrown its throne to the ground.
89.46: You cut short the days of his youth and covered him with disgrace. Selah.
89.47: How long, Lord, will you hide yourself forever, and will your fury burn like fire?
89.48: Remember how short my life is, and for what futility you have created the sons of men.
89.49: Who is the living one who will not see death, who will deliver his soul from the power of Sheol? Sela.
89.50: Where, Lord, are your former mercies, which you swore to David in your faithfulness?
89.51: Remember, Lord, the reproach of your servants, remember that I bear in my breast the insults of so many numerous peoples.
89.52: Remember the insults of your enemies, Lord, their insults against the steps of your Anointed One.
89.53: Blessed be the Lord forever. Amen. Amen.

90.1: A prayer of Moses, man of God. Lord, you have been our refuge from age to age.
90.2: Before the mountains were born and you brought forth the earth and the world, from eternity to eternity you are, O God.
90.3: You reduce mortals to dust and you say, "Return, son of man."«
90.4: For a thousand years in your sight are like yesterday when it passes, or like a watch in the night.
90.5: You carry them away, like a dream, in the morning; like grass, they spring up again.
90.6: In the morning, it blooms and grows. In the evening, it withers and dries up.
90.7: Thus we are consumed by your anger and your fury terrifies us.
90.8: You have set before you our iniquities, our hidden sins, in the light of your face.
90.9: All our days disappear through your wrath, we see our years vanish like a faint sound.
90.10: Our days amount to seventy years, and in their full measure to eighty years, and their splendor is but toil and misery, for they pass quickly and we fly away.
90.11: Who understands the power of your anger and your wrath, according to the fear that is due you?
90.12: Teach us to number our days well, so that we may acquire a wise heart.
90.13: Return, Lord, how long will it be? Have mercy on your servants.
90.14: Satisfy us in the morning with your goodness, and we shall be glad and rejoice all our days.
90.15: Rejoice us for as many days as you have humiliated us, for as many years as we have known misfortune.
90.16: May your work be manifested to your servants, and your glory to their children.
90.17: May the favor of the Lord our God be upon us. Establish for us the work of our hands. Yes, establish the work of our hands.

91.1: He who takes refuge under the protection of the Most High rests in the shade of the Almighty.
91.2: I say to the Lord, «You are my refuge and my fortress, my God, in whom I trust.»
91.3: For it is he who delivers you from the snare of the fowler and from the deadly plague.
91.4: He will cover you with his wings, and under his feathers you will find refuge. His faithfulness is a shield and a breastplate.
91.5: You will have no fear of the terrors of the night, nor of the arrow that flies by day,
91.6: neither the plague that walks in the darkness, nor the contagion that ravages at midday.
91.7: Though a thousand fall at your side and ten thousand at your right hand, you will not be harmed.
91.8: With your own eyes you will look and you will see the retribution of the wicked.
91.9: For you have said, «You are my refuge, Lord.» You have made the Most High your dwelling place.
91.10: No harm will come near you, no plague will approach your tent.
91.11: For he will command his angels concerning you to guard you in all your ways.
91.12: They will lift you up in their hands, so that your foot will not strike against a stone.
91.13: You will tread on the lion and the asp, you will trample the young lion and the dragon.
91.14: «Because he has attached himself to me, I will deliver him, I will protect him, because he knows my name.”.
91.15: He will call upon me and I will answer him. I will be with him in trouble. I will deliver him and honor him.
91.16: I will satisfy him with long life and show him my salvation.»

92.1: Psalm. A song for the Sabbath day.
92.2: It is good to praise the Lord and to celebrate your name, O Most High,
92.3: to proclaim in the morning your goodness and your faithfulness during the night,
92.4: on the ten-stringed instrument and on the lute, with the chords of the harp.
92.5: You make me glad, Lord, by your works, and I rejoice in the works of your hands.
92.6: How great are your works, Lord, how profound your thoughts.
92.7: A stupid man knows nothing about it, and a fool cannot understand it.
92.8: When the wicked spring up like grass and all evildoers flourish, they are to be exterminated forever.
92.9: But you, Lord, are exalted for eternity.
92.10: For behold, your enemies, Lord, behold, your enemies perish; all who do evil are scattered.
92.11: And you raise my horn, like that of a buffalo, I am sprinkled with fresh oil.
92.12: My eye delights in beholding my enemies and my ear in hearing the wicked who rise up against me.
92.13: The righteous will flourish like a palm tree, they will grow like a cedar of Lebanon.
92.14: Planted in the house of the Lord, they will flourish in the courts of our God.
92.15: They will still bear fruit in old age, they will be full of sap and green,
92.16: to proclaim that the Lord is just: he is my rock and there is no injustice in him.

93.1: The Lord is king, he is clothed with majesty, the Lord is clothed, he is girded with strength, therefore the world is firm, it does not totter.
93.2: Your throne was established from the beginning; you are from eternity.
93.3: The rivers raise their voices, O Lord, the rivers raise their resounding waves.
93.4: More than the voice of the great waters, of the powerful waves of the sea, the Lord is magnificent on high.
93.5: Your testimonies are immutable; holiness befits your house, Lord, for all the days of your life.

94.1: God of vengeance, Lord, God of vengeance, appear.
94.2: Arise, judge of the earth, and repay the proud according to their deeds.
94.3: How long, Lord, how long will the wicked triumph?
94.4: They indulge in arrogant speeches, they glorify themselves, all these artisans of iniquity.
94.5: Lord, they crush your people, they oppress your heritage,
94.6: They slaughter widows and foreigners, they massacre orphans.
94.7: And they say, "The Lord does not see; the God of Jacob does not pay attention."«
94.8: Understand, you stupid children of the people. Fools, when will you gain intelligence?
94.9: He who planted the ear, will he not hear? He who formed the eye, will he not see?
94.10: He who chastises the nations, will he not punish? He who gives man understanding, will he not recognize?
94.11: The Lord knows the thoughts of men; he knows that they are vain.
94.12: Blessed is the man whom you instruct, Lord, and to whom you give the teaching of your law.,
94.13: to appease him in the days of trouble, until the pit is dug for the wicked.
94.14: For the Lord will not reject his people, he will not abandon his inheritance,
94.15: But the judgment will once again conform to justice, and all upright men will applaud it.
94.16: Who will rise up for me against the wicked? Who will stand up for me against those who do evil?
94.17: If the Lord were not my help, my soul would soon dwell in the abode of silence.
94.18: When I say, "My foot is slipping," your goodness, Lord, sustains me.
94.19: When anxieties crowd my thoughts, your consolations gladden my soul.
94.20: Does the tribunal of perdition, which does evil within the bounds of legality, have nothing in common with you?
94.21: They hasten against the life of the righteous and they condemn innocent blood.
94.22: But the Lord is my fortress, my God is the rock in whom I take refuge.
94.23: He will bring their iniquity back upon them, he will exterminate them by their own wickedness, he will exterminate them, Lord, our God.

95.1: Come, let us sing for joy to the Lord. Let us shout for joy to the Rock of our salvation.
95.2: Let us go to meet him with praise, let us raise hymns in his honor.
95.3: For the Lord is a great God, a great king above all gods.
95.4: In his hand he holds the foundations of the earth, and the mountain peaks belong to him.
95.5: The sea is his, for he made it, and the earth also, his hands formed it.
95.6: Come, let us bow down and worship, let us kneel before the Lord our Creator.
95.7: For he is our God, and we are the people of his pasture, the flock under his care. Oh, that you would listen to his voice today!.
95.8: Do not harden your hearts as you did at Meribah, as you did on the day of Massah in the desert,
95.9: where your fathers tested me, tried me, although they had seen my works.
95.10: For forty years I loathed this race and said, "They are a people whose hearts go astray, and they have not known my ways."«
95.11: So I swore in my anger, "They shall not enter my rest."«

96.1: Sing to the Lord a new song. Sing to the Lord, all you inhabitants of the earth!
96.2: Sing to the Lord, bless his name. Proclaim his salvation day after day.
96.3: Declare his glory among the nations, his wonders among all peoples.
96.4: For the Lord is great and worthy of all praise; he is to be feared above all gods,
96.5: For all the gods of the peoples are worthless. But the Lord made the heavens.
96.6: Splendor and magnificence are before him; power and majesty are in his sanctuary.
96.7: Ascribe to the Lord, O family of nations, ascribe to the Lord glory and power.
96.8: Ascribe to the Lord the glory due his name. Bring the offering and come into his courts.
96.9: Bow down before the Lord in the holy garment; tremble before him, all you inhabitants of the earth.
96.10: Say among the nations: «The Lord is king; therefore the world shall be established and not to fall; he shall judge the peoples with equity.»
96.11: Let the heavens rejoice and let the earth be glad. Let the sea surge, and all that is in it.
96.12: May the countryside rejoice with all that it contains, may all the trees in the forests cry out with joy,
96.13: before the Lord, for he is coming, for he is coming to judge the earth. He will judge the world with justice and the peoples according to his faithfulness.

97.1: The Lord is king, let the earth rejoice, let the many islands be glad.
97.2: Clouds and shadows surround him; justice and equity are the foundation of his throne.
97.3: The fire advances before him and devours his adversaries around him.
97.4: His lightning illuminates the world; the earth sees it and trembles.
97.5: The mountains melt like wax before the Lord, before the Lord of all the earth.
97.6: The heavens proclaim his righteousness, and all the peoples behold his glory.
97.7: All image worshippers, who are proud of their idols, will be confounded. All the gods prostrate themselves before him.
97.8: Zion heard and rejoiced, the daughters of Judah exulted because of your judgments, Lord.
97.9: For you, Lord, are the Most High over all the earth; you are exalted far above all gods.
97.10: You who love the Lord, hate evil. He protects the souls of his faithful ones; he delivers them from the hand of the wicked.
97.11: Light is sown for the righteous, and joy for those who have an upright heart.
97.12: Righteous ones, rejoice in the Lord and give glory to his holy name.

98.1: Psalm. Sing to the Lord a new song, for he has done marvelous things; his holy right hand and arm have given him victory.
98.2: The Lord has manifested his salvation; he has revealed his righteousness in the sight of the nations.
98.3: He remembered his kindness and faithfulness to the house of Israel. All the ends of the earth have seen the salvation of our God.
98.4: Shout for joy to the Lord, all you inhabitants of the earth, burst into joyful celebration with the sound of instruments.
98.5: Praise the Lord with the harp, with the harp and with the sound of hymns.
98.6: With trumpets and the sound of the horn, shout for joy before the King, the Lord.
98.7: Let the sea stir with all that it contains, let the earth and its inhabitants burst forth in joy.
98.8: Let the rivers applaud, let the mountains together shout for joy
98.9: before the Lord, for he comes to judge the earth; he will judge the world with justice and the peoples with equity.

99.1: The Lord is king, the peoples tremble, he sits upon the cherubim, the earth quakes.
99.2: The Lord is great in Zion; he is exalted above all peoples.
99.3: Let us celebrate your great and awesome name. It is holy.
99.4: Let us celebrate the power of the King who loves justice. You establish equity, you exercise justice and equity in Jacob.
99.5: Exalt the Lord our God and worship at his footstool. He is holy.
99.6: Moses and Aaron were among his priests, and Samuel among those who called on his name. They called on the Lord, and he answered them.,
99.7: He spoke to them in the pillar of cloud, and they observed his commandments and the law that he had given them.
99.8: Lord, our God, you answered them, you were a merciful God to them and you punished them for their sins.
99.9: Exalt the Lord our God and worship at his holy mountain, for the Lord our God is holy.

100.1: A psalm of praise. Shout for joy to the Lord, all you inhabitants of the earth.
100.2: Serve the Lord with joy, come before him with gladness.
100.3: Acknowledge that the Lord is God. He is the one who made us and we belong to him; we are his people and the sheep of his pasture.
100.4: Come to his porticoes with praise, to his courts with songs of praise, celebrate him, bless his name.
100.5: For the Lord is good, his mercy endures forever, and his faithfulness continues through all generations.

101.1: A Psalm of David. I will sing of goodness and justice. It is you, Lord, whom I will praise.
101.2: I will heed the path of innocence. When will you come to me? I will walk in the integrity of my heart, in the midst of my house.
101.3: I will not set any evil deed before my eyes. I hate perverse conduct; it will not cling to me.
101.4: A false heart will never be mine; I do not want to know evil.
101.5: The detractor who secretly tears apart his neighbor, I will exterminate him. The man with a haughty gaze and a heart swollen with pride, I will not tolerate him.
101.6: I will keep my eyes on the faithful men of the land, that they may remain with me. He who walks in a blameless way will be my servant.
101.7: He who acts deceitfully will have no place in my house. He who utters lies will not stand before my eyes.
101.8: Every morning I will exterminate all the wicked of the land, in order to cut off from the city of the Lord all those who commit iniquity.

102.1: Prayer of the unfortunate, when he is overwhelmed and pours out his complaint before the Lord.
102.2: Lord, hear my prayer and let my cry reach you.
102.3: Do not hide your face from me in the day of my distress; incline your ear to me when I cry out; answer me quickly.
102.4: For my days vanish like smoke, and my bones are consumed as by fire.
102.5: Struck like grass, my heart withers, I even forget to eat my bread.
102.6: From screaming and moaning, my bones cling to my flesh.
102.7: I resemble the desert pelican, I have become like the owl of the ruins.
102.8: I spend the nights sleepless, like the lonely bird on the roof.
102.9: All day long my adversaries insult me, my furious enemies swear my ruin.
