102.10: I eat the ashes like bread and I mix my tears with my drink,
102.11: because of your anger and indignation, for you lifted me up and threw me away.
102.12: My days are like a lengthening shadow, and I wither like grass.
102.13: But you, Lord, are seated on an eternal throne, and your memory lives on from age to age.
102.14: You will arise, you will have compassion on Zion, for the time to show her mercy, the appointed time has come.
102.15: For your servants cherish its stones, they despise its dust.
102.16: Then the nations will revere the name of the Lord, and all the kings of the earth your majesty,
102.17: because the Lord has rebuilt Zion, he has shown himself in his glory.
102.18: He turned to the prayer of the wretched man; he did not disdain his supplication.
102.19: Let this be written for the future generation, and let the people yet to be created praise the Lord.,
102.20: because he looked down from his holy height, because the Lord looked down from heaven upon earth,
102.21: to hear the groans of the captives, to deliver those condemned to death,
102.22: so that they may proclaim the name of the Lord in Zion and his praise in Jerusalem
102.23: when all the peoples and kingdoms are gathered together to serve the Lord.
102.24: He broke my strength on the path, he shortened my days.
102.25: I say: My God, do not take me away in the midst of my days, you whose years endure from age to age.
102.26: In the beginning you laid the foundations of the earth, and the heavens are the work of your hands.
102.27: They will perish, but you remain. They will all wear out like a garment. You will change them like a cloak, and they will be changed.,
102.28: But you, you remain the same and your years have no end.
102.29: The sons of your servants will dwell in their land, and their descendants will be established before you.

103.1: Of David. My soul, bless the Lord, and all that is within me, bless his holy name.
103.2: My soul, bless the Lord and do not forget his many benefits.
103.3: He is the one who forgives all your iniquities, who heals all your diseases.
103.4: It is he who delivers your life from the pit, who crowns you with goodness and mercy.
103.5: He is the one who fulfills your desires with good things, and your renewed youth has the strength of an eagle.
103.6: The Lord exercises justice; he gives justice to all the oppressed.
103.7: He revealed his ways to Moses, his great works to the children of Israel.
103.8: The Lord is merciful and compassionate, slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love.
103.9: He does not reprimand forever; he does not hold onto his anger forever.
103.10: He does not treat us according to our sins, nor punish us according to our iniquities.
103.11: For as high as the heavens are above the earth, so great is his goodness to those who fear him.
103.12: As far as the east is from the west, so too does it distance us from our transgressions.
103.13: As a father has compassion on his children, the Lord has compassion on those who fear him.
103.14: Because he knows what we are made of, he remembers that we are dust.
103.15: Man, his days are like grass, he flourishes like a flower of the field.
103.16: Let a breath pass over him, and he is no more, and the place he occupied no longer recognizes him.
103.17: But the Lord's goodness endures forever for those who fear him, and his righteousness for their children's children.,
103.18: for those who keep his covenant and remember to observe his commandments.
103.19: The Lord has established his throne in the heavens, and his dominion extends over all things.
103.20: Bless the Lord, you his angels, who are mighty and strong and who carry out his commands, obeying the voice of his word.
103.21: Bless the Lord, all you his armies, who are his servants and who carry out his will.
103.22: Bless the Lord, all you his works, in all places of his dominion. My soul, bless the Lord.

104.1: My soul, bless the Lord. Lord, my God, you are infinitely great. You are clothed in majesty and splendor.
104.2: He wraps himself in light as with a cloak, he unfurls the heavens like a tent.
104.3: In the waters of heaven he builds his dwelling, he makes his chariot of the clouds, he rides on the wings of the wind.
104.4: He makes the winds his messengers, and the flames of fire his servants.
104.5: He has established the earth on its foundations; it is forever unshakeable.
104.6: You had wrapped her in the abyss as with a garment; the waters covered the mountains.
104.7: They fled from your threat, at the sound of your thunder, they retreated in terror.
104.8: The mountains rose up, the valleys were carved out, in the place you had assigned them.
104.9: You set a boundary that the waters will no longer cross; they will never again cover the earth.
104.10: It sends the springs into the valleys, they flow between the mountains.
104.11: They provide water for all the field animals; the onagers come to quench their thirst there.
104.12: The birds of the sky live on their banks and make their voices resound in the foliage.
104.13: From his lofty abode, he waters the mountains; the earth is satisfied with the fruit of your works.
104.14: He makes grass grow for the flocks and plants for human use. He brings forth bread from the earth.
104.15: And the wine that gladdens the heart of man, he gives him the oil that shines on his face and the bread that strengthens his heart.
104.16: The Lord's trees are full of sap, and the cedars of Lebanon that he planted.
104.17: This is where the birds make their nests and the stork that lives in the cypress trees.
104.18: The high mountains are for the chamois, the rocks are the shelter of the jerboas.
104.19: He made the moon to mark the times and the sun, which knows the time of its setting.
104.20: He brings the darkness and it is night; immediately all the beasts of the forest begin to move.
104.21: The lion cubs roar after the prey and ask God for their food.
104.22: The sun rises, they retreat and go to sleep in their dens.
104.23: The man then goes out to his work and to his job until evening.
104.24: How many are your works, Lord! You have made them all in wisdom. The earth is full of your creatures.
104.25: Here is the sea, wide and vast, teeming with countless animals, both small and large.
104.26: There the ships sail and the leviathan that you formed to play in the waves.
104.27: They all expect you to give them food at the right time.
104.28: You give it to them and they gather it up. You open your hand and they are satisfied with your bounty.
104.29: You hide your face, they are terrified. You take away their breath, they exhale and return to their dust.
104.30: You send forth your breath, they are created, and you renew the face of the earth.
104.31: May the glory of the Lord endure forever. May the Lord rejoice in his works.
104.32: He looks at the earth and it trembles. He touches the mountains and they smoke.
104.33: I want to sing to the Lord as long as I live, to celebrate my God as long as I exist.
104.34: May my song be pleasing to him. I find my joy in the Lord.
104.35: May sinners vanish from the earth and may the wicked be no more. My soul, bless the Lord. Alleluia.

105.1: Praise the Lord, call upon his name, make known among the nations his great works.
105.2: Sing to him, celebrate him. Proclaim all his wonders.
105.3: Glory in his holy name. Joyful be the hearts of those who seek the Lord.
105.4: Seek the Lord and his strength; do not cease to seek his face.
105.5: Remember the wonders he has performed, his miracles, and the judgments that came from his mouth,
105.6: descendants of Abraham, his servant, children of Jacob, his chosen ones.
105.7: He, the Lord, is our God. His judgments reach all the earth.
105.8: He will forever remember his covenant, the word he affirmed for a thousand generations,
105.9: of the covenant he made with Abraham and the oath he swore to Isaac.
105.10: He established it as a law for Jacob, as an everlasting covenant for Israel,
105.11: saying, "I will give you the land of Canaan as your inheritance."«
105.12: Since they were then few in number, few in number, and foreigners in the country,
105.13: that they went from one nation to another and from one kingdom to another people,
105.14: He allowed no one to oppress them, and he punished the kings because of them:
105.15: «Do not touch those who have received my anointing, and do not harm my prophets.»
105.16: He brought famine upon the land, depriving them of the bread that sustained them.
105.17: He sent a man ahead of them, and Joseph was sold into slavery.
105.18: They bound his feet with shackles, they threw him into irons,
105.19: until the day when his prediction was fulfilled and the word of God vindicated him.
105.20: The king sent to remove his bonds, and the sovereign of the people set him free.
105.21: He established him as lord of his house and governor of all his domains.,
105.22: in order to bind the princes, according to his will, and to teach wisdom to his elders.
105.23: Then Israel came to Egypt, and Jacob sojourned in the land of Ham.
105.24: God greatly increased his people and made them more powerful than their oppressors.
105.25: He changed their hearts, to the point that they hated his people and acted treacherously towards his servants.
105.26: He sent Moses, his servant, and Aaron, whom he had chosen.
105.27: They performed his wonders among them; they did miracles in the land of Ham.
105.28: He sent darkness and made it night, and they did not rebel against his word.
105.29: He turned their waters into blood and caused their fish to perish.
105.30: Their country teemed with frogs, even in the chambers of their kings.
105.31: He spoke, and a swarm of insects came, gnats all over their territory.
105.32: He gave them hail for rain, flames of fire in their land.
105.33: He struck their vines and their fig trees and broke the trees of their land.
105.34: He spoke, and there came the locust, locusts without number.
105.35: They devoured all the grass of their land, they devoured the produce of their fields.
105.36: It struck all the firstborn of their countries, the very beginnings of their full vigor.
105.37: He brought his people out with silver and gold, and none of his tribes wavered.
105.38: The Egyptians rejoiced at their departure, for fear of Israel had seized them.
105.39: He spread the cloud to cover them and the fire to light their way at night.
105.40: At their request, he brought quails and fed them with the bread of heaven.
105.41: He opened the rock and water gushed out, flowing like a river in the desert.
105.42: For he remembered his holy word to Abraham, his servant.
105.43: He led his people out in jubilation, his chosen ones amid shouts of joy.
105.44: He gave them the lands of the nations, and they possessed the fruits of the peoples' labor.,
105.45: provided that we keep its precepts and observe its laws. Hallelujah.

106.1: Alleluia. Praise the Lord, for he is good, for his mercy endures forever.
106.2: Who can tell of the mighty deeds of the Lord, who can proclaim all his glory?
106.3: Blessed are those who observe the law, who do what is right at all times.
106.4: Remember me, Lord, in your goodness to your people, visit me with your help,
106.5: so that I may see the happiness of your chosen ones, rejoice in the joy of your people, and glory with your inheritance.
106.6: We have sinned like our fathers, we have committed iniquity, we have done evil.
106.7: Our fathers in Egypt did not regard your wonders, they did not remember the multitude of your graces, they rebelled at the sea, at the Red Sea.
106.8: Yet he saved them because of his name, to display his power.
106.9: He rebuked the Red Sea and it dried up, and he made them walk through the abyss as through a desert.
106.10: He saved them from the hand of the one who hated them, he delivered them from the hand of the oppressor.
106.11: The waves covered their adversaries; not one escaped.
106.12: They then believed his words, they sang his praises.
106.13: But they soon forgot his works; they did not wait for him to carry out his plans.
106.14: They were seized with lust in the desert, and they tested God in the solitude.
106.15: He granted them what they asked for, but he struck them with wasting away.
106.16: Then they were jealous of Moses in the camp and of Aaron, the holy one of the Lord.
106.17: The earth opened and swallowed Dathan and closed again on Abiron's troop.
106.18: The fire devoured their troop, the flame consumed the wicked.
106.19: They made a calf at Mount Horeb, and they worshiped an image of molten metal.
106.20: They exchanged their glory for the image of an ox eating grass.
106.21: They forgot God, their savior, who had done great things in Egypt,
106.22: miracles in the land of Ham, wonders at the Red Sea.
106.23: He spoke of exterminating them, if Moses, his chosen one, had not stood in the breach before him, to prevent his anger from destroying them.
106.24: They scorned the land of delights; they did not believe the word of the Lord.
106.25: They murmured in their tents and did not obey his voice.
106.26: Then he raised his hand against them, swearing to destroy them in the desert,
106.27: to destroy their race among the nations and to scatter them in other countries.
106.28: They became attached to Beelphegor and ate victims offered to the dead.
106.29: They angered the Lord by their actions, and a plague broke out among them.
106.30: Phinehas stood up and gave satisfaction, and the plague was stopped.
106.31: This action was attributed to justice, from age to age, forever.
106.32: They angered the Lord at the waters of Meribah, and Moses suffered because of them.
106.33: because they embittered his mind and he spoke rashly.
106.34: They did not exterminate the peoples whom the Lord had commanded them to destroy.
106.35: They mingled with the nations and learned their works.
106.36: They served their idols, who became a trap for them.
106.37: They sacrificed their sons and daughters to demons.
106.38: They shed innocent blood, the blood of their sons and daughters, whom they sacrificed to the idols of Canaan, and the land was desecrated by murders.
106.39: They defiled themselves by their deeds, they prostituted themselves by their actions.
106.40: The Lord's anger was kindled against his people, and he abhorred his inheritance.
106.41: He delivered them into the hands of the nations, and those who hated them ruled over them.
106.42: Their enemies oppressed them and they were humiliated under their hand.
106.43: Many times he delivered them, but they were rebellious in their plans and were destroyed by their iniquities.
106.44: Nevertheless, he looked at their distress when he heard their pleas.
106.45: He remembered his covenant with them, and he had compassion on them according to his great mercy.
106.46: and he showed them mercy in the presence of all those who held them captive.
106.47: Save us, Lord our God, and gather us from among the nations, so that we may celebrate your holy name and glory in praising you.
106.48: Blessed be the Lord, God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting, and let all the people say: Amen. Alleluia.

107.1: Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good; his mercy endures forever.
107.2: Let the redeemed of the Lord say this, those whom he has redeemed from the hands of the enemy.
107.3: and that he has gathered from all countries, from the east and from the west, from the north and from the sea.
107.4: They wandered in the desert, along a lonely path, without finding a city to inhabit.
107.5: Overcome by hunger and thirst, they felt their souls failing.
107.6: In their distress they cried out to the Lord and he delivered them from their anguish.
107.7: He led them along the right path, to bring them to a habitable city.
107.8: Let them praise the Lord for his goodness and for his wonders in favor of the son of man.
107.9: For he has quenched the thirst of the thirsty soul and filled the hungry soul with good things.
107.10: They dwelt in darkness and the shadow of death, prisoners in suffering and in chains.
107.11: Because they had rebelled against the oracles of God and despised the counsel of the Most High,
107.12: He humbled their hearts with suffering; they collapsed and no one came to their aid.
107.13: In their distress they cried out to the Lord and he saved them from their anguish.
107.14: He brought them out of darkness and the shadow of death, and he broke their chains.
107.15: Let them praise the Lord for his goodness and for his wonders toward the sons of men.
107.16: For he has broken down the bronze gates and cut through the iron bars.
107.17: The fools, through their criminal conduct and iniquities, had brought suffering upon themselves.
107.18: Their souls abhorred all food and they were near the gates of death.
107.19: In their distress they cried out to the Lord and he saved them from their anguish.
107.20: He sent his word and he healed them and he rescued them from their tombs.
107.21: Let them praise the Lord for his goodness and for his wonders toward the sons of men.
107.22: Let them offer sacrifices of thanksgiving and proclaim his works with shouts of joy.
107.23: They had gone down to sea in ships, to trade on the vast waters,
107.24: These are the ones who have seen the works of the Lord and his wonders in the midst of the abyss.
107.25: He spoke, and he caused a stormy wind to rise up, which lifted the waves of the sea.
107.26: They ascended to the heavens, they descended into the abyss, their souls faltered in sorrow.
107.27: Overcome with dizziness, they staggered like a drunken man, and all their wisdom was annihilated.
107.28: In their distress they cried out to the Lord, and he delivered them from their anguish.
107.29: He changed the hurricane into a light breeze and the waves of the sea fell silent.
107.30: They rejoiced when they saw them at peace, and the Lord led them to the desired port.
107.31: Let them praise the Lord for his goodness and for his wonders toward the sons of men.
107.32: Let them exalt him in the assembly of the people and celebrate him in the council of elders.
107.33: He turned rivers into deserts and springs of water into arid land.
107.34: The fertile land is covered in salt flats because of the wickedness of its inhabitants.
107.35: He made the desert a pool of water and the arid land a soil full of springs.
107.36: He settled the starving people there, and they founded a city to inhabit.
107.37: They sowed fields and planted vineyards and reaped abundant harvests.
107.38: He blessed them and they multiplied greatly, and he did not let their flocks decrease.
107.39: They had been reduced to a small number and humiliated, under the weight of misfortune and suffering.
107.40: He had spread shame upon their princes, he had made them wander in pathless deserts.
107.41: But he lifted the poor from their misery and made families like flocks.
107.42: The righteous see it and rejoice, and all the wicked shut their mouths.
107.43: Let the wise take heed to these things and understand the goodness of the Lord.

108.2: My heart is steadfast, O God, I will sing and make joyful instruments resound. Arise, my glory!
108.3: Awake, my lyre and my harp, that I may awaken the dawn.
108.4: I will praise you among the peoples, Lord; I will sing to you among the nations.
108.5: For your goodness is higher than the heavens, and your faithfulness reaches to the clouds.
108.6: Rise above the heavens, O God, let your glory shine over all the earth
108.7: so that your beloved ones may be delivered, save by your right hand and answer me.
108.8: God has spoken in his holiness: «I will rejoice greatly, I will have Shechem as my inheritance, I will measure out the valley of Succoth.
108.9: Gilead is mine, Manasseh is mine, Ephraim is the armor of my head, and Judah is my scepter.
108.10: Moab is my washbasin; on Edom I throw my sandal; on the land of the Philistines I shout for joy.»
108.11: Who will lead me to the fortified city? Who will bring me to Edom?
108.12: Was it not you, O God, who had rejected us, O God, who no longer went out with our armies?
108.13: Lend us your aid against the oppressor. Man's help is but vanity.
108.14: With God we will accomplish great things; he will crush our enemies.

109.1: To the choirmaster, a psalm of David. God of my praise, do not remain silent
109.2: For the mouth of the wicked, the mouth of the treacherous, opens open against me. They speak against me with a lying tongue,
109.3: They besiege me with hateful words and they wage war against me without cause.
109.4: In return for my affection, they fight me, and all I do is pray.
109.5: They repay me with evil for good and hatred for love.
109.6: Put it in the power of a wicked man, and let the accuser stand at his right hand.
109.7: When he is judged, let him be found guilty and let his prayer be considered a sin.
109.8: May his days be shortened and may another take his place.
109.9: May his children become orphans, may his wife be widowed.
109.10: May his children be vagrants and beggars, seeking their bread far from their ruined homes.
109.11: Let the creditor seize all that belongs to him, and let foreigners plunder what he has earned through his labor.
109.12: May he have no one to show him affection, may no one have pity on his orphans.
109.13: May his descendants be doomed to ruin and may their name be erased in the second generation.
109.14: May the iniquity of their fathers be remembered before the Lord, and may the sin of their mother not be blotted out.
109.15: May they always be before the Lord, and may he erase their memory from the earth.
109.16: Because he did not remember to show mercy, because he persecuted the unfortunate and the needy and the brokenhearted to put them to death.
109.17: He loved the curse, it falls upon him. He scorned the blessing, it turns away from him.
109.18: He has clothed himself with the curse as with a garment; like water it enters into him, and like oil it penetrates his bones.
109.19: May she be for him the garment that envelops him, the belt that never ceases to encircle him.
109.20: May this be the reward from the Lord for my adversaries and for those who speak maliciously against me.
109.21: And you, Lord God, defend me because of your name; in your great goodness, deliver me.
109.22: For I am unhappy and needy, and my heart is wounded within me.
109.23: I am fading away like a shadow in its decline, I am swept away like a grasshopper.
109.24: From fasting, my knees are shaky and my body is exhausted from being too thin.
109.25: I am an object of scorn to them; they look at me and nod their heads.
109.26: Help me, Lord, my God, save me in your goodness.
109.27: Let them know that it is your hand, that it is you, Lord, who did it.
109.28: They will curse, but you will bless; they will rise up, but they will be put to shame, and your servant will rejoice.
109.29: My adversaries will be clothed in ignominy; they will be wrapped in their shame as in a cloak.
109.30: My lips will praise the Lord highly; I will extol him in the midst of the multitude
109.31: for he stands at the right hand of the poor, to save them from those who condemn them.

110.1: A Psalm of David. The Lord said to my Lord, «Sit at my right hand until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.»
110.2: The Lord will extend your mighty scepter from Zion: Reign as master in the midst of your enemies.
110.3: Your people flock to you on the day you assemble your army, adorned with sacred regalia. From the womb of dawn comes to you the dew of your young warriors.
110.4: The Lord has sworn, and he will not change his mind: «You are a priest forever, in the order of Melchizedek.»
110.5: The Lord is on the right. He will crush kings on the day of his wrath.
110.6: He executes his judgment among the nations, everything is filled with corpses, he crushes the heads of the whole earth.
110.7: He drinks from the stream on the way, that's why he raises his head.

111.1: Hallelujah. ALEPH. I want to praise the Lord with all my heart. BETH. In the gathering of the righteous and in the assembly.
111.2: GHIMEL. Great are the works of the Lord. DALETH. Sought after for all the delights they provide.
111.3: HEY. His work is nothing but splendor and magnificence. VAV. And his justice endures forever.
111.4: ZAÏN. He left a memory of his wonders. HETH. The Lord is merciful and compassionate.
111.5: TETH. He gave food to those who fear him. YOD. He remembers his covenant forever.
111.6: CAPH. He demonstrated to his people the power of his works. LAMED. By delivering to them the heritage of the nations.
111.7: MEM. The works of his hands are truth and justice. NUN. All his commandments are immutable.
111.8: SAMECH. Established forever. AÏN. Made according to truth and righteousness.
111.9: PHE. He sent redemption to his people. TZADÉ. He established his covenant forever. QOPH. His name is holy and awesome.
111.10: RESCH. The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom. SHIN. Truly, those who observe his law are wise. TALV. His praise endures forever.

112.1: Hallelujah. Aleph. Blessed is the man who fears the Lord. Beth. Who finds his delight in keeping his precepts.
112.2: GHIMEL. His descendants will be mighty on earth. DALETH. The race of the righteous will be blessed.
112.3: HEY. He has in his house well-being and riches. VAV. And his righteousness endures forever.
112.4: ZAÏN. Light dawns in the darkness for the upright. HETH. For the one who is merciful, compassionate, and just.
112.5: TETH. Blessed is the man who shows mercy and lends. YOD. In justice he prevails.
112.6: CAPH. For it will never be shaken. LAMED. The righteous will leave an eternal memory.
112.7: MEM. He is not afraid of bad news. NUN. His heart is steadfast, trusting in the Lord.
112.8: SAMECH. His heart is unwavering; he does not fear. AÏN. Until he sees his enemies defeated.
112.9: PHE. He sows alms, he gives to the needy. TZADÉ. His righteousness endures forever. QOPH. His horn is lifted up in glory.
112.10: RESCH. The wicked see it and are angered. SCHIN. He gnashes his teeth and envy consumes him. THAV. The desire of the wicked will perish.

113.1: Alleluia. Praise, servants of the Lord, praise the name of the Lord.
113.2: Blessed be the name of the Lord, now and forever.
113.3: From sunrise to sunset, praise be to the name of the Lord.
113.4: The Lord is exalted above all nations, his glory is above the heavens.
113.5: Who is like the Lord our God? He sits enthroned on high.
113.6: and he looks down, at the heavens and at the earth.
113.7: He raises the unfortunate from the dust, he lifts the poor from the dung heap,
113.8: to have them sit with the princes, with the princes of his people.
113.9: He gives a home to the barren woman of the house; he makes her a joyful mother in the midst of her children. Alleluia.

114.1: When Israel left Egypt, when the house of Jacob departed from a barbarous people,
114.2: Judah became his sanctuary, Israel his domain.
114.3: The sea saw it and fled, the Jordan turned back.
114.4: The mountains leaped like rams, the hills like lambs.
114.5: What ails you, sea, that you flee? Jordan, that you turn back?
114.6: What is it you mountains have that makes you leap like rams, and you hills like lambs?
114.7: Tremble, O earth, before the face of the Lord, before the face of the God of Jacob,
114.8: which transforms the rock into a pond, the boulder into a spring of water.

115.1: Not to us, Lord, not to us, but to your name give glory, because of your goodness, because of your faithfulness.
115.2: Why should the nations say, "Where is their God?"«
115.3: Our God is in heaven. He does whatever he wants.
115.4: Their idols are silver and gold, the work of human hands.
115.5: They have mouths but do not speak, they have eyes but do not see.
115.6: They have ears but cannot hear, they have nostrils but cannot smell.
115.7: They have hands but do not touch, they have feet but do not walk, they make no sound from their throats.
115.8: May those who create them and anyone who confides in them resemble them.
115.9: Israel, put your trust in the Lord. He is their help and their shield.
115.10: House of Aaron, put your trust in the Lord. He is their help and their shield.
115.11: You who fear the Lord, put your trust in the Lord. He is their help and their shield.
115.12: The Lord has remembered us; he will bless us. He will bless the house of Israel, he will bless the house of Aaron.,
115.13: He will bless those who fear the Lord, the small with the great.
115.14: May the Lord multiply his favors upon you and upon your children.
115.15: May you be blessed by the Lord, who made heaven and earth.
115.16: The heavens are the Lord's heavens, but he has given the earth to the sons of men.
115.17: It is not the dead who praise the Lord, those who descend into the place of silence.
115.18: But we will bless the Lord, now and forever. Hallelujah.

116.1: I love Him, for the Lord hears my voice, my supplications.
116.2: For he has inclined his ear to me, and all my life I will invoke him.
116.3: The bonds of death surrounded me and the anguish of Sheol had seized me; I was prey to distress and affliction.
116.4: And I called upon the name of the Lord: «Lord, save my soul.»
116.5: The Lord is merciful and just, our God is compassionate.
116.6: The Lord protects the weak; I was unfortunate and he saved me.
116.7: My soul, return to your rest, for the Lord has filled you with good things.
116.8: Yes, you saved my soul from death, my eye from tears, my feet from falling.
116.9: I will walk before the Lord again, in the land of the living.
116.10: I have faith, even as I say, "I am excessively unhappy."«
116.11: In my despondency, I said, "Every man is a liar."«
116.12: What shall I give back to the Lord for all his blessings towards me?.
116.13: I will lift up the cup of salvation and call upon the name of the Lord.
116.14: I will fulfill my vows to the Lord in the presence of all his people.
116.15: The death of his faithful is precious in the eyes of the Lord.
116.16: Ah Lord, because I am your servant, your servant, son of your handmaid, you have loosed my bonds.
116.17: I will offer you a sacrifice of thanksgiving and I will call upon the name of the Lord.
116.18: I will fulfill my vows to the Lord, in the presence of all his people,
116.19: in the courts of the house of the Lord, within your walls, Jerusalem. Alleluia.

117.1: All nations, praise the Lord; all peoples, extol him.
117.2: For his goodness to us is great, and the truth of the Lord endures forever. Alleluia.

118.117: in the Vulgate) At the start of the procession.
118.1: Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good; his mercy endures forever.
118.2: Let Israel say, «Yes, His mercy endures forever.»
118.3: Let the house of Aaron say, «Yes, his mercy endures forever.»
118.4: Let those who fear the Lord say, «Yes, his mercy endures forever.» During the journey.
118.5: Out of my distress I called upon the Lord: the Lord answered me and set me free.
118.6: The Lord is for me; I fear nothing. What can men do to me?
118.7: The Lord is on my side among those who help me; I will see the ruin of those who hate me.
118.8: It is better to seek refuge in the Lord than to trust in men.
118.9: It is better to seek refuge in the Lord than to trust in princes.
118.10: All nations surrounded me, in the name of the Lord, I cut them to pieces.
118.11: They surrounded and enveloped me; in the name of the Lord, I cut them to pieces.
118.12: They surrounded me like bees, they died out like a fire of thorns, in the name of the Lord, I cut them to pieces.
118.13: You pushed me violently to make me fall, but the Lord helped me.
118.14: The Lord is my strength and the subject of my songs; he has been my salvation.
118.15: Shouts of triumph and deliverance resound in the tents of the righteous. The right hand of the Lord has displayed its power.
118.16: The right hand of the Lord is exalted; the right hand of the Lord has displayed its strength.
118.17: I will not die, but I will live and proclaim the works of the Lord.
118.18: The Lord has severely punished me, but he has not given me over to death. The leader arrived before the temple.
118.19: Open to me the gates of righteousness, that I may enter and praise the Lord. The priests.
118.20: This is the gate of the Lord; the righteous may enter through it. The leader of the people.
118.21: I will celebrate you, because you answered my call and you have been my salvation.
118.22: The stone rejected by the builders has become the cornerstone. The priests.
118.23: This is the Lord's work; it is a marvelous thing in our eyes. The people, upon entering.
118.24: This is the day the Lord has made; let us rejoice and be glad.
118.25: O Lord, grant salvation. O Lord, grant prosperity. The priests, to the leader.
118.26: Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord. We bless you from the house of the Lord.
118.27: The Lord is God; he makes light shine upon us. The priests, to the people: Bind the victim with cords, up to the horns of the altar. The people.
118.28: You are my God and I will praise you, my God and I will exalt you. All together.
118.29: Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good; his mercy endures forever.

119.118: You despise all those who deviate from your laws, for their cunning is nothing but lies.
119.1: Blessed are those whose ways are blameless, who walk according to the law of the Lord.
119.2: Blessed are those who keep his teachings, who seek him with all their heart.,
119.3: who do not commit iniquity and who walk in his ways.
119.4: You prescribed your orders so that they would be carefully followed.
119.5: May my ways be guided so that I may observe your laws.
119.6: Then I will not have to be ashamed, in view of all your commandments.
119.7: I will praise you with the uprightness of my heart, learning the precepts of your righteousness.
119.8: I want to keep your laws: don't abandon me completely. BETH.
119.9: How can a young man keep his path pure? By keeping to your word.
119.10: I seek you with all my heart: do not let me wander far from your commandments.
119.11: I have kept your word hidden in my heart, so that I might not sin against you.
119.12: Blessed are you, Lord, teach me your laws.
119.13: From my lips I enumerate all the precepts of your mouth.
119.14: I find joy in following your teachings, as if I possessed all the treasures.
119.15: I want to meditate on your precepts, to keep my eyes on your paths.
119.16: I delight in your laws; I will not forget your word. GHIMEL.
119.17: Show kindness to your servant, so that I may live and keep your word.
119.18: Open my eyes, so that I may behold the wonders of your law.
119.19: I am a stranger on earth, do not hide your commandments from me.
119.20: My soul is broken by desire, which always draws it towards your precepts.
119.21: You threaten the proud, those cursed ones, who stray far from your commandments.
119.22: Keep shame and contempt away from me, for I observe your teachings.
119.23: Let the princes sit and speak against me: your servant will meditate on your laws.
119.24: Yes, your teachings are my delight; they are the men of my council. DALETH.
119.25: My soul clings to the dust; give me life, according to your word.
119.26: I have explained my ways to you, and you have answered me: teach me your laws.
119.27: Help me understand the way of your ordinances and I will meditate on your wonders.
119.28: My soul, overcome with sorrow, melts into tears: raise me up according to your word.
119.29: Turn away from me the way of falsehood and grant me the favor of your law.
119.30: I have chosen the path of faithfulness; I place your precepts before my eyes.
119.31: I have clung to your teachings: Lord, do not let me be put to shame.
119.32: I run in the way of your commandments, for you have enlarged my heart. HE.
119.33: Teach me, Lord, the way of your precepts, so that I may keep it until the end of my life.
119.34: Give me understanding so that I may keep your law and observe it with all my heart.
119.35: Lead me in the path of your commandments, for I find happiness there.
119.36: Incline my heart towards your teachings and not towards gain.
119.37: Turn my eyes away from vanity, and lead me to live in your way.
119.38: Fulfill your promise to your servant, which you made to those who fear you.
119.39: Remove from me the reproach that I dread, for your precepts are good.
119.40: I earnestly desire to carry out your ordinances; by your justice, give me life. VAV.
119.41: May your mercy come upon me, Lord, and your salvation, according to your word.
119.42: And I will be able to answer the one who insults me, because I trust in your word.
119.43: Do not take the word of truth entirely from my mouth, for I have hope in your precepts.
119.44: I want to keep your law constantly, always, and in perpetuity.
119.45: I will walk far out, for I seek your prescriptions.
119.46: I will speak of your teachings before kings, and I will not be ashamed.
119.47: I will delight in your commandments, for I love them.
119.48: I will raise my hands to your commandments which I love, and I will meditate on your laws. ZAÏN.
119.49: Remember the promise you made to your servant, on which you have placed my hope.
119.50: It is my consolation in misery, that your word gives me life.
119.51: The proud heap their mockery upon me: I do not deviate from your law.
119.52: I think of your precepts of times past, Lord, and I am comforted.
119.53: Indignation seizes me because of the wicked, who abandon your law.
119.54: Your laws are the subject of my songs, in the place of my pilgrimage.
119.55: At night I remember your name, Lord, and I observe your law.
119.56: This is the portion given to me: I keep your ordinances. HETH.
119.57: My part, Lord, I say, is to keep your words.
119.58: I implore you with all my heart; have mercy on me according to your word.
119.59: I reflect on my paths and I turn my steps back towards your teachings.
119.60: I hasten, I do not delay to observe your commandments.
119.61: The traps of the wicked surround me, and I do not forget your law.
119.62: In the middle of the night, I rise to praise you, because of your righteous judgments.
119.63: I am a friend to all who fear you and to those who keep your precepts.
119.64: The earth is full of your goodness, Lord: teach me your laws. TETH.
119.65: You have shown kindness to your servant, Lord, according to your word.
119.66: Teach me right judgment and understanding, for I have faith in your commandments.
119.67: Before I was humiliated, I went astray; now I heed your word.
119.68: You are good and benevolent, teach me your laws.
119.69: Proud people are inventing lies against me; but I will keep your decrees with all my heart.
119.70: Their heart is as callous as fat; but I delight in your law.
119.71: It is good for me to have been humbled, so that I may learn your precepts.
119.72: Better to me is the law from your mouth than heaps of gold and silver. YOD.
119.73: Your hands made me and fashioned me; give me understanding to learn your commandments.
119.74: Those who fear you will rejoice when they see me, for I trust in your word.
119.75: I know, Lord, that your judgments are just; it is in your faithfulness that you have humbled me.
119.76: May your goodness be my comfort, according to your word given to your servant.
119.77: May your compassion come upon me and let me live, for your law is my delight.
119.78: May the proud who unjustly mistreat me be confounded. I, however, meditate on your decrees.
119.79: Let those who fear you and those who know your teachings turn to me.
119.80: May my heart be wholly devoted to your laws, so that I may not be put to shame. CAPH.
119.81: My soul longs for your salvation; I hope in your word.
119.82: My eyes yearn for your promise, I say, "When will you comfort me?"«
119.83: For I am like a wineskin exposed to smoke, but I do not forget your laws.
119.84: How many days has your servant lived? When will you bring justice to those who persecute me?
119.85: The proud dig pits to destroy me; they are the adversaries of your law.
119.86: All your commandments are faithful, they persecute me without cause: help me.
119.87: They almost wiped me out in the country, and I will not abandon your orders.
119.88: Restore my life in your goodness, and I will observe the teaching from your mouth. LAMED.
119.89: Forever, Lord, your word is established in heaven.
119.90: Your faithfulness endures from age to age; you have founded the earth, and it remains.
119.91: It is according to your laws that everything exists to this day, for everything obeys your commands.
119.92: If your law did not bring me delight, I would already have perished in my misery.
119.93: I will never forget your prescriptions, because it was through them that you gave me back my life.
119.94: I am yours: save me, for I seek your precepts.
119.95: The wicked are waiting to destroy me: I pay attention to your teachings.
119.96: I have seen limits to everything that is perfect, but your command has no limits. MEM.
119.97: How I love your law. It is the object of my meditation all day long.
119.98: Through your commandments, you make me wiser than my enemies, for I always have them with me.
119.99: I am wiser than all my teachers, for your teachings are the object of my meditation.
119.100: I am more intelligent than the old men, for I observe your ordinances.
119.101: I keep my foot far from every evil path, so that I may keep your word.
119.102: I do not deviate from your precepts, for it is you who instructed me.
119.103: How sweet your words are to my palate, sweeter than honey to my mouth.
119.104: By your decrees I become wise, therefore I hate all paths of falsehood. NUN.
119.105: Your word is a torch before my feet, a light for my path.
119.106: I have sworn, and I will be faithful to it, to observe the precepts of your justice.
119.107: I am reduced to extreme affliction: Lord, restore me to life, according to your word.
119.108: Accept, Lord, the offering of my lips and teach me your precepts.
119.109: My life is continually in my hands and I do not forget your law.
119.110: The wicked lay traps for me, but I do not stray far from your commandments.
119.111: I have your teachings as an inheritance forever, for they are the joy of my heart.
119.112: I have inclined my heart to observe your laws, always, until the end. SAMECH.
119.113: I hate men with double hearts and I love your law.
119.114: You are my refuge and my shield; I trust in your word.
119.115: Depart from me, you wicked, and I will observe the commandments of my God.
119.116: Uphold me according to your promise, so that I may live, and do not let me be put to shame in my hope.
119.117: Be my support and I will be saved, and I will always have your laws before my eyes.
119.119: You reject all the wicked of the earth like dross, that is why I love your teachings.
119.120: My flesh trembles with fear before you, and I dread your judgments. AÏN.
119.121: I uphold law and justice, and will not surrender to my oppressors.
119.122: Take under your guarantee the property of your servant and let not the proud oppress me.
119.123: My eyes long for your salvation and for the promise of your justice.
119.124: Deal with your servant according to your goodness and teach me your laws.
119.125: I am your servant: give me understanding, so that I may know your teachings.
119.126: It is time for the Lord to intervene; they are violating your law.
119.127: That is why I love your commandments more than gold and fine gold.
119.128: That is why I find all your precepts just; I hate every path of falsehood. PHEASANT.
119.129: Your teachings are wonderful, and so my soul observes them.
119.130: The revelation of your words illuminates; it gives understanding to the simple.
119.131: I open my mouth and breathe in, for I long for your commandments.
119.132: Turn your face toward me and have mercy on me, for this is justice toward those who love your name.
119.133: Strengthen my steps in your word and let no iniquity have dominion over me.
119.134: Deliver me from the oppression of men and I will keep your ordinances.
119.135: Let your face shine upon your servant and teach me your laws.
119.136: My eyes overflow with tears because your law is not observed. TSADÉ.
119.137: You are just, Lord, and your judgments are fair.
119.138: You gave your teachings according to justice and perfect faithfulness.
119.139: My zeal consumes me, because my adversaries forget your words.
119.140: Your word is entirely tried and true, and your servant loves it.
119.141: I am small and despised, but I do not forget your prescriptions.
119.142: Your justice is eternal justice, and your law is truth.
119.143: Distress and anguish have overtaken me, but your commandments are my delight.
119.144: Your teachings are eternally true; grant me understanding, so that I may live. QOPH.
119.145: I call upon you with all my heart, hear me, Lord, so that I may keep your laws.
119.146: I call upon you, save me, so that I may observe your teachings.
119.147: I rise before dawn and cry out to you; I hope in your word.
119.148: My eyes stay up before the watches of the night, to meditate on your word.
119.149: Listen to my voice according to your goodness, Lord; restore me to life according to your judgment.
119.150: They are approaching, those who persecute crime, who have strayed from your law.
119.151: You are near, Lord, and all your commandments are truth.
119.152: I have long known about your teachings that you have established them forever. RESCH.
119.153: See my misery and deliver me, for I do not forget your law.
119.154: Defend my cause and be my avenger, give me back my life according to your word.
119.155: Salvation is far from the wicked, for they do not care about your laws.
119.156: Your mercies are many, Lord; restore me to life according to your judgments.
119.157: Many are my persecutors and enemies, but I do not depart from your teachings.
119.158: At the sight of the unbelievers, I felt horror, because they do not observe your word.
119.159: Consider that I love your precepts, Lord; restore me to life according to your goodness.
119.160: The summary of your word is truth, and all your laws of justice are eternal. SIN, SHIN.
119.161: Princes persecute me without cause; it is because of your words that my heart is afraid.
119.162: I rejoice in your word, as if I had found rich spoils.
119.163: I hate lies, I abhor them, I love your law.
119.164: Seven times a day I praise you, because of your righteous laws.
119.165: There is great peace for those who love your law, and nothing will cause them to stumble.
119.166: I hope in your salvation, Lord, and I practice your commandments.
119.167: My soul observes your teachings and is enamored of them.
119.168: I keep your ordinances and your teachings, for all my ways are before you. THAV.
119.169: May my cry reach you, Lord. According to your word, give me understanding.
119.170: May my supplication reach you. According to your word, deliver me.
119.171: May my lips utter your praise, for you have taught me your laws.
119.172: Let my tongue proclaim your word, for all your commandments are righteous.
119.173: May your hand reach out to help me, for I have chosen your precepts.
119.174: I long for your salvation, Lord, and your law is my delight.
119.175: May my soul live to praise you, and may your judgments come to my aid.
119.176: I am wandering like a lost sheep; seek your servant, for I do not forget your commandments.

120.1: Song of Ascents. To the Lord, in my distress, I cried out, and he answered me.
120.2: «"Lord, deliver my soul from lying lips and from a deceitful tongue."»
120.3: What will be given to you, what will be your gain, you treacherous tongue?
120.4: The sharp arrows of the Almighty, with the burning coals of the broom.
120.5: How unhappy I am to be staying in Mesek, to be living under Cedar's tents.
120.6: For too long I have stayed with those who hate peace.
120.7: I am a man of peace, and when I talk to them, they are for war.

121.1: A hymn for the ascents. I lift my eyes to the mountains, where will my help come from?
121.2: My help will come from the Lord who made heaven and earth.
121.3: He will not allow your foot to stumble; he who watches over you will not slumber.
121.4: No, he who guards Israel neither slumbers nor sleeps.
121.5: The Lord is your keeper; the Lord is your refuge, always at your right hand.
121.6: During the day, the sun will not strike you, nor the moon during the night.
121.7: The Lord will keep you from all harm; he will keep your soul.
121.8: The Lord will watch over your going out and your coming in, now and forever.

122.1: A song of ascents, by David. I rejoiced when they said to me, «Let us go to the house of the Lord.»
122.2: Finally, our feet stop at your gates, Jerusalem.
122.3: Jerusalem, you are built like a city where everything fits together.
122.4: There the tribes go up, the tribes of the Lord, according to the law of Israel, to praise the name of the Lord.
122.5: There are seats for judgment, the seats of the house of David.
122.6: Make vows for Jerusalem. May those who love you be blessed.
122.7: May peace reign within your walls, and prosperity within your palaces.
122.8: For the sake of my brothers and friends, I ask for peace for you.
122.9: For the sake of the house of the Lord our God, I desire happiness for you.

123.1: Song of Ascents. I lift up my eyes to you, O you who sit enthroned in the heavens.
123.2: As the eye of a servant looks to the hand of his master, and the eye of a maid to the hand of her mistress, so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until he has mercy on us.
123.3: Have mercy on us, Lord, have mercy on us, for we have been more than filled with reproach.
123.4: Our soul has been more than satiated with the mockery of the proud, with the contempt of the arrogant.

124.1: A song of ascents, of David. If the Lord had not been for us, let Israel proclaim it,
124.2: If the Lord had not been on our side when men rose up against us,
124.3: They would have devoured us alive when their anger was kindled against us.,
124.4: The waters would have engulfed us, the torrent would have swept over our souls,
124.5: The impetuous waters would have passed over our souls.
124.6: Blessed be the Lord, who did not deliver us to their teeth.
124.7: Our soul, like the sparrow, has escaped from the fowler's snare. The snare is broken, and we have been delivered.
124.8: Our help is in the name of the Lord, who made heaven and earth.

125.1: A song of ascents. Those who trust in the Lord are like Mount Zion, which cannot be shaken, but is established forever.
125.2: Jerusalem has a belt of mountains around it, so the Lord surrounds his people, now and forever.
125.3: The scepter of the wicked will not remain over the inheritance of the righteous, so that the righteous will not also turn their hands toward iniquity.
125.4: Lord, shower your goodness on the good and on those who have a righteous heart.
125.5: But as for those who turn aside to crooked ways, may the Lord abandon them with those who do evil. Peace be upon Israel.

126.1: A song of ascents. When the Lord restored the fortunes of Zion, it was like a dream to us.
126.2: Then our mouths shouted for joy, our tongues sang songs of gladness. Then it was said among the nations, «The Lord has done great things for them.»
126.3: Yes, the Lord has done great things for us, and we are filled with joy.
126.4: Lord, restore our captives, as you make the streams flow in the Negev.
126.5: Those who sow in tears will reap in gladness.
126.6: They go, weeping, carrying seed to sow, and they will return with shouts of joy, carrying sheaves of their harvest.

127.1: A Song of Ascents, by Solomon. Unless the Lord builds the house, the builders labor in vain. Unless the Lord watches over the city, the watchman stands guard at its gates in vain.
127.2: It is in vain that you rise before the day and delay your rest, eating the bread of sorrow, for he gives as much to his beloved in his sleep.
127.3: Behold, children are a heritage from the Lord, and the fruits of a fruitful womb a reward.
127.4: Like arrows in the hand of a warrior, so are the sons of youth.
127.5: Happy is the man who has filled his quiver with them. They will not be ashamed when they answer their enemies at the city gate.

128.1: A song of ascents. Blessed is the man who fears the Lord, who walks in his ways.
128.2: You then feed yourself from the work of your hands, you are happy and filled with possessions.
128.3: Your wife is like a fruitful vine within your house, your children like young olive shoots around your table.
128.4: This is how the man who fears the Lord will be blessed.
128.5: May the Lord bless you from Zion. May you see Jerusalem flourishing all the days of your life.
128.6: May you see your children's children. Peace be upon Israel.

129.1: Song of Ascents. They have cruelly oppressed me from my youth, let Israel say.
129.2: They cruelly oppressed me from my youth, but they did not prevail against me.
129.3: They plowed my back, they traced long furrows there.
129.4: But the Lord is just; he has cut the chains of the wicked.
129.5: May they be confounded and turned back, all those who hate Zion.
129.6: May they be like the grass on the rooftops, which dries up before it is pulled up.
129.7: The reaper does not fill his hand, nor does the one who binds the sheaves fill his lap,
129.8: And the passersby do not say, "The blessing of the Lord be upon you; we bless you in the name of the Lord."«

130.1: Song of Ascents. From the depths of the abyss I cry out to you, Lord.
130.2: Lord, hear my voice, let your ears be attentive to the sounds of my prayer.
130.3: If you, Lord, keep a record of iniquity, Lord, who can stand?
130.4: But with you is forgiveness, so that you may be revered.
130.5: I hope in the Lord, my soul hopes and I wait for his word.
130.6: My soul longs for the Lord more than watchmen long for the dawn.
130.7: Israel, put your hope in the Lord, for with the Lord is mercy, with him is abundant deliverance.
130.8: He is the one who will redeem Israel from all its iniquities.

131.1: A Song of Ascents, of David. Lord, my heart is not proud, nor are my eyes haughty. I do not seek great things, nor what is exalted above me.
131.2: No, I keep my soul in calm and silence. Like a weaned child at its mother's breast, like a weaned child my soul is within me.
131.3: Israel, put your hope in the Lord now and forever.

132.1: Song of Ascents. Remember, Lord, David, and all his sufferings.
132.2: He made this oath to the Lord, this vow to the Fortress of Jacob:
132.3: «I will not enter the tent where I live, I will not climb onto the bed where I rest,
132.4: I will grant no sleep to my eyes, nor slumber to my eyelids,
132.5: until I find a place for the Lord, a dwelling place for Jacob's Fortress."»
132.6: We heard that she was in Ephrathah; we found her in the fields of Jaar.
132.7: Let us go to the tabernacle of the Lord, let us bow down before the footstool of his feet.
132.8: Arise, O Lord, come to your resting place, you and the ark of your majesty.
132.9: May your priests be clothed with righteousness and may your faithful shout for joy.
132.10: For the sake of David, your servant, do not reject the face of your Anointed One.
132.11: The Lord swore to David a sure oath, and he will not depart from it: «From the fruit of your womb I will place on your throne.
132.12: If your sons keep my covenant and the statutes I will teach them, their sons also will sit on your throne forever.»
132.13: For the Lord has chosen Zion, he has desired it for his dwelling place.
132.14: «"This is the place of my rest forever; I will dwell there, for I have desired it.".
132.15: I will shower rich blessings upon his sustenance, I will satisfy his poor with bread.
132.16: I will clothe her priests with salvation and her faithful will shout for joy.
132.17: There I will increase the power of David, I will prepare a torch for my Anointed One.
132.18: I will shame his enemies, and his diadem will shine upon his brow.»

133.1: A song of ascents, of David. How good and pleasant it is when brothers dwell together in unity!.
133.2: It is like the precious oil that, poured on the head, runs down on the beard, on Aaron's beard, and down onto the edge of his garment.
133.3: It is like the dew of Hermon, which falls on the peaks of Zion, for it is there that the Lord has established the blessing, life, forever.

134.1: Song of Ascents. Behold, bless the Lord, all you servants of the Lord, who serve in the house of the Lord by night.
134.2: Raise your hands toward the sanctuary and bless the Lord.
134.3: May the Lord bless you from Zion, he who made heaven and earth.

135.1: Alleluia. Praise the name of the Lord, praise him, servants of the Lord,
135.2: you who serve in the house of the Lord, in the courts of the house of our God.
135.3: Praise the Lord, for the Lord is good; sing his name on the harp, for he is full of sweetness.
135.4: For the Lord chose Jacob for himself, he chose Israel to be his inheritance.
135.5: Yes, I know it, the Lord is great, our Lord is above all gods.
135.6: Whatever the Lord wills, he does, in heaven and on earth, in the sea and in all the depths.
135.7: He makes the clouds rise from the ends of the earth, he produces lightning with the rain, he draws the wind from his treasures.
135.8: It once struck down the firstborn of Egypt, from man to animal.
135.9: He performed signs and wonders in your midst, O Egypt, against Pharaoh and all his servants.
135.10: He struck down many nations and killed powerful kings,
135.11: Sihon, king of the Amorites, Og, king of Bashan, and all the kings of Canaan.
135.12: And he gave their land as an inheritance, as an inheritance to Israel, his people.
135.13: Lord, your name endures forever. Lord, your memory persists from generation to generation.
135.14: For the Lord does justice to his people and has compassion on his servants.
135.15: The idols of the nations are silver and gold, the work of human hands.
135.16: They have mouths but do not speak, they have eyes but do not see.
135.17: They have ears but cannot hear. There isn't even a breath in their mouths.
135.18: May those who make them resemble them, whoever trusts in them.
135.19: House of Israel, bless the Lord. House of Aaron, bless the Lord.
135.20: House of Levi, bless the Lord. You who fear the Lord, bless the Lord.
135.21: Blessed be the Lord from Zion, who dwells in Jerusalem. Alleluia.

136.1: Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good; his mercy endures forever.
136.2: Give thanks to the God of gods, for his mercy endures forever.
136.3: Give thanks to the Lord of lords, for his mercy endures forever.
136.4: To him who alone performs great wonders, for his mercy is eternal.
136.5: Who made the heavens with wisdom, for his mercy endures forever.
136.6: Who spread out the earth upon the waters, for his mercy is eternal.
136.7: Who made the great lights, for his mercy is eternal.
136.8: The sun to rule over the day, for its mercy is eternal.
136.9: The moon and the stars to rule over the night, for His mercy is eternal.
136.10: To him who struck the Egyptians in their firstborn, for his mercy is eternal.
136.11: He brought Israel out from among them, for his mercy endures forever.
136.12: With a strong hand and an outstretched arm, for his mercy is eternal.
136.13: To him who divided the Red Sea in two, for his mercy endures forever.
136.14: Who allowed Israel to pass through, for his mercy is eternal.
136.15: And he hurled Pharaoh and his army into the Red Sea, for his mercy endures forever.
136.16: To him who led his people in the desert, for his mercy is eternal.
136.17: Who struck down great kings, for his mercy is eternal.
136.18: And he destroyed mighty kings, for his mercy is eternal.
136.19: Sehon, king of the Amorites, for his mercy is eternal.
136.20: And Og, king of Bashan, for his mercy is eternal.
136.21: Who gave their land as an inheritance, for his mercy is eternal.
136.22: As an inheritance to Israel, his servant, for his mercy is eternal.
136.23: To him who remembered us when we were humbled, for his mercy endures forever.
136.24: And he delivered us from our oppressors, for his mercy is eternal.
136.25: To him who gives food to all that lives, for his mercy is eternal.
136.26: Give thanks to the God of heaven, for his mercy endures forever.

137.1: By the rivers of Babylon we sat and wept, remembering Zion.
137.2: We had hung our harps from the willows in its valleys.
137.3: For there, those who held us captive demanded hymns and songs from us, our oppressors, joyful songs: «Sing us a song of Zion.»
137.4: How can we sing the Lord's song in a foreign land?
137.5: If I ever forget you, Jerusalem, may my right hand be paralyzed.
137.6: May my tongue cling to the roof of my mouth if I cease to think of you, if I do not consider Jerusalem my highest joy.
137.7: Remember, Lord, the children of Edom, when on the day of Jerusalem, they said, «Destroy it, destroy it to its foundations.»
137.8: Daughter of Babylon, doomed to ruin, happy is he who will repay you for the evil you have done to us.
137.9: Blessed is the one who seizes your little children and dashes them against a stone.

138.1: Of David. I want to praise you with all my heart, to sing to you on the harp, in the presence of the angels.
138.2: I want to bow down in your holy temple and praise your name, because of your goodness and faithfulness, because you have made a magnificent promise, above all the glories of your name.
138.3: The day I called upon you, you answered my prayer, you restored strength and courage to my soul.
138.4: All the kings of the earth will praise you, Lord, when they hear the oracles from your mouth.
138.5: They will celebrate the ways of the Lord, for the glory of the Lord is great.
138.6: For the Lord is exalted and sees the humble, but he knows the proud from afar.
138.7: Though I walk in distress, you preserve my life; you stretch out your hand to restrain the wrath of my enemies, and your right hand saves me.
138.8: The Lord will complete what he has done for me. Lord, your goodness endures forever; do not abandon the work of your hands.

139.1: To the choirmaster, a psalm of David. Lord, you have searched me and known me,
139.2: You know, when I'm sitting or standing, you can tell what I'm thinking from afar.
139.3: You observe me when I am walking or lying down, and all my ways are familiar to you.
139.4: Before the word is on my tongue, Lord, you know it completely.
139.5: You circle me from side to side and place your hand on me:
139.6: Science is too wonderful for me; it is too lofty for me to attain.
139.7: Where can I go far from your mind, where can I flee far from your face?
139.8: If I ascend to heaven, you are there; if I make my bed in Sheol, you are there.
139.9: If I take the wings of dawn and go to live at the edge of the sea,
139.10: There again your hand will guide me and your right hand will grasp me.
139.11: And I say: At least darkness will cover me and night will be the only light that surrounds me.
139.12: Even darkness has no darkness for you; for you the night shines like the day, and darkness like light.
139.13: It is you who formed my inmost being and knit me together in my mother's womb.
139.14: I praise you for having made me such a wonderful creature; your works are admirable, and my soul delights in acknowledging it.
139.15: My substance was not hidden from you, when I was formed in secret, artfully woven in the depths of the earth.
139.16: I was but a formless seed, and your eyes saw me, and in your book were written all the days that were destined for me, before any of them yet came to be.
139.17: O God, how delightful your thoughts seem to me. How numerous they are.
139.18: If I count them, they outnumber grains of sand. I wake up and I am still with you.
139.19: O God, will you not destroy the wicked? Men of blood, depart from me.
139.20: They speak of you in a criminal manner; they take your name in vain, they, your enemies.
139.21: Lord, should I not hate those who hate you, abhor those who rise up against you?
139.22: Yes, I hate them with a complete hatred; they are enemies to me.
139.23: Search me, O God, and know my heart; test me and know my anxious thoughts.
139.24: See if I am on a fatal path and lead me on the eternal path.

140.1: To the choirmaster, a psalm of David.
140.2: Lord, deliver me from the wicked man, preserve me from violent men,
140.3: who plot evil in their hearts, who continually stir up war against me,
140.4: who sharpen their tongues like a snake and have the venom of the asp under their lips. Sela.
140.5: Lord, keep me from the hands of the wicked, preserve me from violent men who plot to make me stumble.
140.6: The proud have set a trap for me, and snares; they have placed snares along my path, they have laid traps for me. Selah.
140.7: I say to the Lord: You are my God. Hear, Lord, the voice of my supplications.
140.8: Lord God, my mighty savior, you cover my head in the day of battle.
140.9: Lord, do not grant the desires of the wicked, do not let his plans succeed, he would be too proud. Selah.
140.10: May the iniquity of their lips fall back upon the heads of those who besiege me,
140.11: May burning coals be shaken over them. May God cast them into the fire, into the abyss from which they will never rise again.
140.12: No, the slanderer will not prosper on earth, and misfortune will relentlessly pursue the violent man.
140.13: I know that the Lord gives justice to the poor and right to the needy.
140.14: Yes, the righteous will celebrate your name, and the upright will dwell in your presence.

141.1: A Psalm of David. Lord, I call upon you; come quickly to me. Give ear to my voice when I call upon you.
141.2: May my prayer be before your face like incense, and the raising of my hands like the evening offering.
141.3: Lord, set a guard over my mouth, a watchman at the door of my lips.
141.4: Do not incline my heart to evil things, do not incline it to engage in wicked deeds with men who commit iniquity, that I may not take part in their feasts.
141.5: Let the righteous strike me, it is a favor; let him rebuke me, it is perfume on my head; my head will not refuse it, for then I will only oppose their evil designs with my prayer.
141.6: But soon their leaders will be hurled down the rocks and my words will be heeded because they are pleasing.
141.7: Just as when we plow and loosen the soil, so our bones are sown at the edge of Sheol.
141.8: For to you, Lord God, I turn my eyes. In you I seek refuge; do not abandon my soul.
141.9: Protect me from the traps they set for me, from the snares of those who do evil.
141.10: May the wicked fall into their own traps and may I escape at the same time.

142.1: A Psalm of David. When he was in the cave. A prayer.
142.2: With my voice I cry out to the Lord, with my voice I implore the Lord.
142.3: I pour out my complaint in his presence; before him I lay bare my distress.
142.4: When my mind fails me, you know my path, you know that on the road I walk, they lay traps for me.
142.5: Look to my right and see: no one recognizes me, no refuge is found in me, no one cares for my soul.
142.6: I cry out to you, Lord, I say: You are my refuge, my portion in the land of the living.
142.7: Listen to my complaint, for I am exceedingly unhappy; deliver me from those who pursue me, for they are stronger than I.
142.8: Deliver my soul from this prison, so that I may praise your name. The righteous will triumph with me because of the good you have done for me.

143.1: A Psalm of David. Lord, hear my prayer, give ear to my supplications, answer me in your truth and in your justice.
143.2: Do not enter into judgment with your servant, for no living man is righteous before you.
143.3: The enemy seeks my soul, he tramples my life to the ground. He relegates me to dark places, like those who have long since died.
143.4: My mind is failing me, my heart is troubled in my chest.
143.5: I think of the days of old, I meditate on all your works, I reflect on the work of your hands.
143.6: I stretch out my hands and my soul to you; like parched land, I yearn for you. Sela.
143.7: Lord, answer me quickly, my spirit fails; do not hide your face from me, I am becoming like those who go down to the pit.
143.8: Let me feel your goodness early, for it is in you that I hope. Show me the way I should walk, for it is to you that I lift up my soul.
143.9: Deliver me from my enemies, Lord, I take refuge in you.
143.10: Teach me to do your will, for you are my God. May your good spirit guide me in the right path.
143.11: For your name's sake, Lord, give me life; in your righteousness, deliver my soul from distress.
143.12: In your goodness, annihilate my enemies and destroy all those who oppress me, for I am your servant.

144.1: Of David. Blessed be the Lord, my rock, who has trained my hands for battle and my fingers for war,
144.2: my benefactor and my fortress, my high refuge and my deliverer, my shield, the one who is my refuge, who gathers my people under me.
144.3: Lord, what is man that you are mindful of him, the son of man that you are mindful of him?
144.4: Man is like a breath, his days are like a passing shadow.
144.5: Lord, bow down your heavens and come down, touch the mountains and let them burn with fire,
144.6: Make the lightning flash and scatter the enemies, launch your arrows and rout them.
144.7: Stretch out your hands from on high, deliver me and save me from the great waters, from the hand of the foreigners,
144.8: whose mouth utters lies and whose hand is a perjured hand.
144.9: O God, I will sing to you a new song, I will praise you on the ten-stringed lute.
144.10: You who give victory to kings, who save David, your servant, from the deadly sword,
144.11: Deliver me and save me from the hand of the sons of foreigners, whose mouths speak lies and whose hands are perjured hands.
144.12: May our sons, like vigorous plants, grow in their youth. May our daughters be like corner pillars, carved like those of a temple.
144.13: May our granaries be filled and overflowing with all kinds of provisions. May our sheep in our pastures multiply by thousands and by myriads.
144.14: May our heifers be fertile. May there be no breach or surrender in our walls, nor any cry of alarm in our public squares.
144.15: Blessed are the people who enjoy these blessings. Blessed are the people whose God is the Lord.

145.1: A song of praise, by David. ALEPH. I will exalt you, my God, O King, and bless your name forever and ever.
145.2: BETH. I want to bless you every day and celebrate your name always and forever.
145.3: GHIMEL. The Lord is great and worthy of all praise, and his greatness is unfathomable.
145.4: DALETH. Each generation will tell the next of the praise of your works, they will proclaim your wonders.
145.5: HEY. I will sing of the glorious splendor of your majesty and your prodigious works.
145.6: WAV. And we will speak of your formidable power and I will recount your greatness.
145.7: ZAÏN. We will proclaim the memory of your immense goodness and we will celebrate your justice.
145.8: HETH. The Lord is merciful and compassionate, slow to anger and abounding in kindness.
145.9: TETH. The Lord is good to all and his mercy extends to all his creatures.
145.10: YOD. All your works praise you, Lord, and your faithful bless you.
145.11: CAPH. They speak of the glory of your reign and proclaim your power,
145.12: LAMED. In order to make known to the sons of men his wonders and the glorious splendor of his reign.
145.13: MEM. Your kingdom is an eternal kingdom and your dominion endures throughout all ages.
145.14: SAMECH. The Lord upholds all who fall, he raises up all who are bowed down.
145.15: AÏN. The eyes of all beings are turned towards you in expectation and you give them their food in due time.
145.16: PHE. You open your hand and you satisfy with your bounty all that breathes.
145.17: TSADÉ. The Lord is just in all his ways and merciful in all his works.
145.18: QOPH. The Lord is near to all who call upon Him, to all who call upon Him with a sincere heart.
145.19: RESCH. He fulfills the desires of those who fear him, he hears their cry and he saves them.
145.20: SCHIN. The Lord protects all who love him and destroys all the wicked.
145.21: THAV. May my mouth proclaim the praise of the Lord, and may all flesh bless his holy name, always and forever.

146.1: Alleluia. My soul, praise the Lord.
146.2: All my life, I want to praise the Lord; as long as I live, I want to sing to my God.
146.3: Do not put your trust in princes, in the son of man, who cannot save.
146.4: His breath departs, he returns to his dust, and on that same day, his plans vanish.
146.5: Blessed is he whose help is the God of Jacob, whose hope is in the Lord his God.
146.6: The Lord made heaven and earth, the sea and all that is in it. He remains faithful forever.
146.7: He gives justice to the oppressed, he feeds the hungry. The Lord sets the captives free.
146.8: The Lord opens the eyes of the blind. The Lord lifts up those who are bowed down. The Lord loves the righteous.
146.9: The Lord protects the foreigner, he sustains the orphan and the widow, but he makes the way of the wicked crooked.
146.10: The Lord is king forever, your God, O Zion, from age to age. Alleluia.

147.1: Alleluia. Praise the Lord, for it is good to praise our God; for he is gracious, it is lovely to praise him.
147.2: The Lord rebuilds Jerusalem; he gathers the scattered people of Israel.
147.3: He heals the brokenhearted and binds up their wounds.
147.4: He counts the number of stars, he calls them all by name.
147.5: Our Lord is great, and his strength is infinite and his intelligence has no limits.
147.6: The Lord helps the humble, he casts the wicked to the ground.
147.7: Sing to the Lord a song of thanksgiving; praise our God with the harp.
147.8: He covers the skies with clouds and prepares rain for the earth. He makes grass grow on the mountains.
147.9: He gives food to the cattle, to the young ravens that cry out to him.
147.10: It is not in the vigor of the horse that he takes pleasure, nor in the legs of the man that he places his delight.
147.11: The Lord delights in those who fear him, in those who hope in his goodness.
147.12: Jerusalem, praise the Lord. Zion, praise your God.
147.13: For he strengthens the bars of your gates, he blesses your children in your midst,
147.14: He ensures peace at your borders, he satisfies you with the finest wheat.
147.15: He sends his orders to the earth, his word travels swiftly.
147.16: It makes the snow fall like wool, it scatters the frost like ashes.
147.17: He throws his ice cubes in chunks; who can withstand his cold?
147.18: He sends forth his word and melts them. He makes his wind blow and the waters flow.
147.19: It was he who revealed his word to Jacob, his laws and his will to Israel.
147.20: He has not done the same for all other nations; they do not know his will. Hallelujah.

148.1: Alleluia. Praise the Lord from the heavens, praise him in the heights.
148.2: Praise him, all you his angels; praise him, all you his armies.
148.3: Praise him, sun and moon; praise him, all you shining stars.
148.4: Praise him, you highest heavens, and you waters above the heavens.
148.5: Let them praise the name of the Lord, for he commanded and they were created.
148.6: He established them forever and ever; he laid down a law that shall not be transgressed.
148.7: From the earth, praise the Lord, sea monsters and all you oceans,
148.8: Fire and hail, snow and vapors, impetuous winds, who carry out his orders,
148.9: mountains and all of you, hills, fruit trees and all of you, cedars,
148.10: wild animals and herds of all kinds, reptiles and winged birds,
148.11: kings of the earth and all peoples, princes, and all you judges of the earth,
148.12: young men and young virgins, old men and children.
148.13: Let them praise the name of the Lord, for his name alone is great; his glory is above heaven and earth.
148.14: He has raised up the power of his people, a subject of praise for all his faithful, for the children of Israel, the people who are near to him, Alleluia.

149.1: Alleluia. Sing to the Lord a new song, let his praise resound in the assembly of the saints.
149.2: Let Israel rejoice in its Creator, let the children of Zion exult in their King.
149.3: Let them praise his name in their dances, let them sing to him with the tambourine and the harp.
149.4: For the Lord takes pleasure in his people; he glorifies the humble by saving them.
149.5: The faithful triumph in glory; they leap for joy on their beds.
149.6: The praises of God are in their mouths, and in their hands they hold a two-edged sword.,
149.7: to exact vengeance upon the nations and bring punishment upon the peoples,
149.8: to bind their kings with chains and their nobles with iron stocks,
149.9: to execute against them the written decree, that is the glory reserved for all his faithful. Alleluia.

150.1: Hallelujah. Praise God in his sanctuary. Praise him in the dwelling place of his power.
150.2: Praise him for his mighty deeds. Praise him according to the immensity of his greatness.
150.3: Praise him with the sound of the trumpet. Praise him with the harp and the lyre.
150.4: Praise him in your dances with the tambourine. Praise him with the stringed instruments and the pipe.
150.5: Praise him with clear cymbals. Praise him with resounding cymbals.
150.6: Let everything that has breath praise the Lord. Alleluia.
